## PAGE 06187: Selecting element types

- page_id: `243968511`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968511/Selecting+element+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:01.334+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

804386951

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

804386954

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

804386952

In the **Select Element Type** dialog, you can select the check box next to the model element type to be filtered; i.e., displayed or searched for. This dialog opens when filtering items in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space='']**Containment** tab and when searching or filtering for elements, as outlined below:

- When [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Searching' space=''] for items, the purpose of the Select Element Type dialog is to select the type of element to search.
- When [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering' space=''] items in the Containment tab, the purpose of the Select Element Type dialog is to provide the ability to hide elements you do not want to see in the Containment tab.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Element Type dialog.

The following table explains the functions of the tabs, options, and buttons in the **Select Element Type** dialog.

| Button/Tab | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Search text box | Specifies a search term to filter the search results of element types in the List, Inheritance, and Structural tabs.NoteThe appearances of the search term in the search results are highlighted in yellow. |
| List | Click the List button to list the types of elements in one alphabetic list. There are two groups in the list: Element and Symbol. |
| Inheritance | Click the Inheritance button to group the types of elements according to the inheritance. For example, types of elements are grouped to the following inheritances: Named Element, Relations, and others. The Symbol group is listed also. |
| Structural | Click the Inheritance button to group the types of elements according to the following groups: DSL Customization, MagicDraw Profile, UML2 Metamodel, Symbol. |
| Select All | Selects all types of elements in the list – all elements will be displayed after filtering or searching. |
| Clear All | Clears all types of elements in the list – no elements will be displayed after filtering or searching. |
| Specifying optionsInclude Subtypes | Includes the subtypes of the selected element types. For example, if you select a Package, its subtypes (e.g., Model and Profile) will also be included. The option is enabled by default. |
| Include Custom Types | Includes the custom types extending the selected element types. For example, if you select a Package, the custom types (e.g., Smart Package) that extend the Package will also be included. The option is disabled by default. |
| OK | Applies the selected/cleared element types to display or hide them in the Containment tab when you have opened the dialog from the Containment tab.Applies the selected/cleared element types to filter and display them in the Search Results tab in the Model Browser when you have opened the dialog from the Find dialog. |
| Cancel | Cancels the selecting/clearing or filtering of the element types and closes the dialog. |
| Help | Opens the Selecting element types page with the description of the dialog. |

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Search Results tab' space='']**Search Results** tab opens in the Model Browser to display the search results according to the search criteria you have created from selecting/clearing types of elements in the **Select Element Type** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Search Results tab in the Model Browser displays the search results.

804386950

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Filtering' space='']

## PAGE 06188: Selecting elements

- page_id: `243968390`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968390/Selecting+elements
- version_number: 5
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:05:58.449+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The element Selection dialog is used for selecting elements from available contents and adding them to a certain destination scope. The title of the dialog varies depending on the way it is invoked. Depending on the content, the element Selection dialog is used for selecting an element, diagram, or owner. The types of elements available for selection determine the presence of the specific element type filters in the dialog.

#### WARNING: Teamwork Cloud projects

Teamwork Cloud projects

The following message indicates that the representation text indexing is not complete. It is not recommended that you perform element searches while the indexing is in progress. Otherwise, depending on the project size, the search operation might be slow, and the memory consumption might increase.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The element Selection dialog also opens when assigning a property value - the particular element or elements. For example, the element Selection dialog opens when assigning the base classifier in the element [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

In the element Selection dialog, you can perform the following actions:

- Select one or more elements to assign them as property values.
- Search for an element.
- Create elements.
- Clone a selected element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Select Element dialog.

The table below describes the components of the **Select Element** dialog.

| Dialog element name/image | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Search for:Type and PackagePropertyAny Element | Select one of the predefined element type filters to narrow down the search results:Type and Package - select to search for types and packages only.Property - select to search for properties only.Any Element - select to search among all the elements in the model. |
|  | Click to customize the search options to optimize the search operation.Match Text AnywhereTo search for a matching phrase in any part of the element name, click the command to add the check mark.To explicitly search from the beginning of the element name, click the command to remove the check mark.Use Camel Case To search for a matching phrase typed in a camel case, click the command to add the check mark.To search for a matching phrase typed in a regular case, click the command to remove the check mark.You can use camel case as follows:Type only the first letters (either capitalized or not) of each word, for example, “and” or “AND” to find “Analysis and Design”.Type skipping spacers, for example, “cv3” to find “CV-3”.Perform a partial search, for example, “ibd” to find SysML v1 Internal Block diagram. Be sure the Match Text Anywhere command is checked! |
| More | Click the button to load the next 100 matches. By default, the first 100 matches are shown in the dialog. |
| PreviousNext | Available in the Tree view only.Click to navigate to a previous or next result.Alternatively, you can press the Shift + F3 keyboard shortcut to select the previous match or press F3 to select the next match. |
| Switch to Tree | Click to switch from the List to Tree view.Alternatively, press the Alt+B keyboard shortcut. |
| Apply Filter(Ctrl+Space) | applyfilterClick to clear the check box if you need to remove the search scope filter. If you clear the Apply Filter check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time. |
| Filter Options | Select to customize the filter options to narrow down the search scope. - reduces the search results list by hiding uncommon elements, keeping elements such as Interface, Association Class, Class, and Component in the list.Exclude Auxiliary Resources - reduces the search results list by excluding elements marked as auxiliary. By default, elements in the standard/system profiles are marked as auxiliary. This option does not hide the following:UML datatypes (placed under MagicDraw Profile) and UML2 Metamodel (placed under UML Standard profile) only for UML project.UML2 Metamodel, SysML v1 value types, and stereotypes for SysML v1 project.Elements with <<ignoreAuxFilter>> stereotype applied and all its elements recursively. >]]> - reduces the search results list by showing elements from the imported packages only.Available for the usage element only, i.e., when selecting the type/behavior for properties/actions. |
| Creation Mode | Click to turn on the element creation mode. |
| Create | Click to create a new element. |
| Clone | Click to clone the selected element. |
| Single Selection/Multiple Selection | Click to expand or collapse to select a single element or multiple elements. |

When searching for an element, type the search phrase in the Search By Name box. The following table describes the search types that can be used.

| Search Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Simple search in theList and Tree views | In the Search by Name box, type a phrase to search for element names. Elements matching the search phrase will be displayed in the List or Tree view.When switching between element selection views, the selected elements are remembered. That is, if the element is selected in the Tree view, it will be selected in the List view as well. |
| Search using wild cards | In the Search by Name box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:“*” substitutes any range of characters. So if your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names can be found. For example, “Address” and “ThreadDeath”.“?” substitutes exactly one character. So if your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “Address”. “ThreadDeath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.Match Text Anywhere commandBe sure the Match Text Anywhere command is unchecked, when using wildcard characters! Click to find the command. |

**Related pages**

If you clear the </span><strong>Apply Filter</strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"> check box once, your selection is remembered for the next time.</span><span> </span></p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Filter Options</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="filter_options.png" /></ac:image></p></div></td><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><span>Select to customize the filter options to narrow down the search scope.</span></p><ul><li data-uuid="18e2ccb5-cab8-442e-bb25-d86791a463f0"><strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Hide Uncommon Elements filter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Hide Uncommon Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </strong><span>- r</span><span>educes the search results list by hiding uncommon elements, keeping elements such as Interface, Association Class, Class, and Component in the list.</span></li><li data-uuid="f6622e58-fe06-440a-82ea-0a003b07ab55"><strong>Exclude Auxiliary Resources </strong><span>- r</span><span>educes the search results list by excluding elements marked as auxiliary. By default, elements in the standard/system profiles are marked as auxiliary.</span> This option does not hide the following:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="433f98b7-1ac7-461a-b52d-3f52aeec933e"><ol><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ol><li>UML datatypes (placed under MagicDraw Profile) and UML2 Metamodel (placed under UML Standard profile) only for UML project.</li><li>UML2 Metamodel,  SysML v1 value types, and stereotypes for SysML v1 project.</li><li>Elements with &lt;&lt;<strong>ignoreAuxFilter</strong>&gt;&gt; stereotype applied and all its elements recursively. <ac:link ac:anchor="Applying a stereotype"><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to apply stereotype >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ol></li></ol></li></ul><ul><li data-uuid="51ed020c-a533-404e-86c7-71fcac4f88c0"><p><strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Filter By Package Imports filter" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Filter by Package Imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> </span></strong><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">- r<span>educes the search results list by showing elements from the imported packages only.</span></span><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);"><span><br /></span></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61c45734-7f36-4739-b7f7-f971f53580a6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">Available for the usage element only, i.e., when selecting the type/behavior for properties/actions.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Creation Mode</strong></p></div></td><td colspan="1">Click to turn on the element creation mode.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Create </strong></td><td colspan="1">Click to create a new element.</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1"><strong>Clone</strong></td><td colspan="1">Click to clone the selected element.</td></tr><tr class=""><td rowspan="9"><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong>Single Selection/Multiple Selection</strong></p></div></td><td rowspan="9"><p>Click to expand or collapse to select a single element or multiple elements.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br />When searching for an element, type the search phrase in the Search By Name box. The following table describes the search types that can be used.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 100.0%;"><colgroup class=""><col class="" style="width: 13.0077%;" /><col class="" style="width: 86.9923%;" /></colgroup><tbody class=""><tr class=""><th>Search Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr class=""><td><p><strong>Simple search in the</strong></p><strong>List and Tree views</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>In the <strong>Search by Name </strong>box, type a phrase to search for element names. Elements matching the search phrase will be displayed in the <strong>List </strong>or <strong>Tree </strong>view.</p><p>When switching between element selection views, the selected elements are remembered. That is, if the element is selected in the <strong>Tree </strong>view, it will be selected in the <strong>List </strong>view as well.</p></div></td></tr><tr class=""><td><strong>Search using wild cards</strong></td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>In the <strong>Search by Name </strong>box, type a phrase using the following wildcard characters:</p><ul><li data-uuid="b8b7914d-335a-42ab-bd95-167646400d43">“*” substitutes any range of characters. So if your search phrase is “*dd”, all the elements containing “dd” in their names can be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress” and “Threa<u>dD</u>eath”.</li><li data-uuid="4faccdf3-6c39-45f6-bfbb-488346c56129">“?” substitutes exactly one character. So if your search phrase is “?dd”, only the elements with “dd” in their names starting from the second character can be found. For example, “A<u>dd</u>ress”. “Threa<u>dD</u>eath” cannot be found in this case, because it contains more than one character before the search phrase “dd”.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4da044d-4ce8-48ee-bc96-b8d67c2baa2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Match Text Anywhere command</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Be sure the <strong>Match Text Anywhere </strong>command is unchecked, when using wildcard characters! Click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="search_for_element_button.png" /></ac:image> to find the command.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></div></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="b4acb923-5671-4fa8-84e2-dbcd3b3707c2" /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562439 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06189: Selecting Environment

- page_id: `249562439`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562439/Selecting+Environment
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-12-12T12:26:05.546+01:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling
- labels: ['environment-selection-dialog', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Environment Selection** dialog opens when starting the UAF plugin for the first time.

To select the enterprise architecture framework environment

1. Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build: Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog. This information may help you decide what to choose.
  - EA-BPMN
  - UAF
  - DoDAF 2.0
  - MODAF
  - NAF v. 3
  - NAF 4.0
  - BPMN
  - SysML v1
  - DoDAF 1.5
  - TOGAF
2. Click to select the Expert check box if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the Expert check box empty.
3. Click the Continue button. The sample project corresponding to the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using the Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).

For information about changing the environment, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks' space=''].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Environment Selection</strong> dialog opens when starting the UAF plugin for the first time.</p><p>To select the enterprise architecture framework environment</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the environment according to the architecture you are going to build:<br /><ul><li><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">EA-BPMN</span></li><li>UAF</li><li>DoDAF 2.0</li><li>MODAF</li><li>NAF v. 3</li><li>NAF 4.0</li><li>BPMN</li><li>SysML v1</li><li>DoDAF 1.5</li><li>TOGAF</li></ul>Read the brief descriptions of each enterprise architecture framework provided in the dialog. This information may help you decide what to choose.</li><li>Click to select the <strong>Expert</strong> check box if you want the user interface to be complex and have all details exposed. And if you are a new user, leave the <strong>Expert</strong> check box empty.</li><li>Click the <strong>Continue</strong> button. The sample project corresponding to the selected enterprise architecture framework will be opened (only if using the Cameo Enterprise Architecture tool).</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c3a5619-bc24-48cf-9e54-25b1c53712c1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For information about changing the environment, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Converting model between Enterprise Architecture Frameworks" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968215 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06190: Selecting multiple property values

- page_id: `243968215`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968215/Selecting+multiple+property+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:53.350+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Selecting values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this case you can assign more than one value to a property in the**Select Element**dialog.Examples of properties that may have multiple values: *Classifier*, *Method*.

To assign multiple property values

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the following figure). The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='']** dialog opens.
3. Select existing and / or create elements.
4. Click **OK**when you are done.

Make sure the Multiple Selection mode is turned on.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Selecting elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Select Element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> <span>dialog opens.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(199,200,202);"><span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(168,169,173);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Select existing and / or create elements.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(199,200,202);"><span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(168,169,173);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Click <span><strong>OK </strong><span>when you are done.</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c987a926-7086-45ff-80dd-f5f142caf788"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Make sure the Multiple Selection mode is turned on.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assigning_multiple_property_values.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968425 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06191: Selecting port and part types

- page_id: `243968425`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968425/Selecting+port+and+part+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:58.859+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1822737895

1822737899

1822737898

INLINE

The **Type Selection Mode** turns on the automatic port and part type selection. When the function is turned on:

- On a part creation, the Select Type dialog appears. The same is valid when creating ports. [ATTACHMENT filename='selecting_type_on_part_creation.png']
- If a part does not have a specified type, then a new type for the part will be created on a nested port creation. A type for the new port will be created, too.
- If a part does not have a name, type the name on the part shape on the diagram and the type will be created automatically. The typed name will be the name of the part type (NOTE: that part will be without a name).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To turn on the **Type Selection Mode**

- On the Composite Structure, Class, Component, or Package diagrams [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''], click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The**Type Selection Mode**works separately for each project.

You can save the time spent looking for elements in the type selection list by using [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package import' space='MED'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f4c76bc-54ba-47fe-8e05-1a3e1a2d5da9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1822737895</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3807e4c0-665f-4d84-918e-b61acd664b5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1822737899</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fa0ea0d8-213b-42ae-a707-43fce7b57308"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1822737898</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6ed3c4c4-4771-4351-8493-7076df38404c"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Type Selection Mode</strong> turns on the automatic port and part type selection. When the function is turned on:</p><ul><li>On a part creation, the <strong>Select Type </strong>dialog appears. The same is valid when creating ports.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="selecting_type_on_part_creation.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>If a part does not have a specified type, then a new type for the part will be created on a nested port creation. A type for the new port will be created, too.</p></li><li><p>If a part does not have a name, type the name on the part shape on the diagram and the type will be created automatically. The typed name will be the name of the part type (NOTE: that part will be without a name).</p></li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="typing_part_name_with_type_selection_mode_on.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To turn on the <strong>Type Selection Mode</strong></p><hr /><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the Composite Structure, Class, Component, or Package diagrams <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[palette]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, click <ac:image ac:title="Type Selection Mode" ac:alt="Type Selection Mode"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specify_type.png" /></ac:image> .</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a792f8e-361c-4f23-8d0f-6accbb0243b6"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span><span class="confluence-link">The<strong> Type Selection Mode </strong>works separately for each project.</span></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="62b3dd84-8460-4e39-88bf-a7f534c7c7cf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can save the time spent looking for elements in the type selection list by using <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Package import" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[package imports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968389 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06194: Selecting referenced element in Containment tree

- page_id: `243968389`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968389/Selecting+referenced+element+in+Containment+tree
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:28:56.530+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Manage Navigation > Defining hyperlinks
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can select a referenced element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] directly from the hyperlinked text or element.

To select a referenced element in the Containment tree

1. Select the hyperlinked element on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] or in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , or hower your mouse pointer at the hyperlinked text.
2. Do one of the following: - Press Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element. - Press Ctrl+Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element. The referenced element is selected in the Containment tree.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p>You can select a referenced element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> directly from the hyperlinked text or element.</p><p>To select a referenced element in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li class="auto-cursor-target">Select the hyperlinked element on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, or hower your mouse pointer at the hyperlinked text.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target">Do one of the following:<br /> - Press Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element.<br /> - Press Ctrl+Alt and double click the hyperlinked text or element.<br />The referenced element is selected in the Containment tree.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968209 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06195: Selecting single property value from editable list

- page_id: `243968209`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968209/Selecting+single+property+value+from+editable+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:53.210+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Selecting values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This is the case of assigning a value to a property in one of the following ways:

- Selecting a value either from a predefined value (auto-completion) list or from the whole model in the **Select Element** dialog.
- Creating an element and selecting it as property value.

To select a property value from a predefined (auto-completion) list

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the arrow (see the highlighted button in the following figure).
3. In the drop-down list, select a value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To assign a property value in the **Select Element** dialog

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. Click the Edit button (see the highlighted button in the figure below). The **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space='']******dialog opens.
3. Select an existing or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new elements' space=''] an element.
4. Click **OK**when you are done.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To remove a value selected for a single value type property

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968206 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06196: Selecting single property value from non-editable list

- page_id: `243968206`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968206/Selecting+single+property+value+from+non-editable+list
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:53.119+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Editing property values > Selecting values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In this case you can choose one of the predefined values. 
To select a property value from the non-editable predefined list

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select the value.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To remove a property value selected from the non-editable predefined list

1. Click an appropriate property value cell.
2. In the drop-down list, select *<UNSPECIFIED>*.

The property value can be removed only for those properties, that can have an empty value.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968671 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06197: Selecting source model

- page_id: `243968671`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968671/Selecting+source+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:06.688+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the second step of the wizard, the Transformation Source tree displays all project data, i.e., the packages and their inner elements, that can be selected as a transformation source. Transformation will take the selected elements as input data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the second step of the wizard, the Transformation Source tree displays all project data, i.e., the packages and their inner elements, that can be selected as a transformation source. Transformation will take the selected elements as input data.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_2nd_step.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968668 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06198: Selecting transformation type

- page_id: `243968668`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968668/Selecting+transformation+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:06.548+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the first step of the Model Transformation Wizard, a list of the available transformation types is displayed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Transformation types are displayed in the list window.

The following operations are available in the Select transformation type window:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Next | Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select source). |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the first step of the Model Transformation Wizard, a list of the available transformation types is displayed.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_1st_step.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Transformation types are displayed in the list window.</p><p>The following operations are available in the  Select transformation type window:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Next</strong></td><td> Proceed to the next step (in this case, Select source).</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968676 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06199: Selecting type mappings

- page_id: `243968676`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968676/Selecting+type+mappings
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:06.964+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The fourth step in the wizard allows for selecting a type map that will be applied during the transformation. Usually transformation has and brings in some predefined type map, but if you want, you can specify another type map.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

A type map can be regarded as a collection of rules of the form “Replace the usage of type X in the used project with the usage of type Y”.

A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transformation Type Mapping' space=''] is a model object, i.e. a package with a collection of dependencies, hence all model manipulation operations can be performed on it. In particular, it can be refactored into a used project and mounted into any project, which needs it. It can be a simple package in your project as well, if you need a custom, one-off type map. A predefined type map can be taken from the modeling tool module and edited.

To see a list of the type maps available in your model, click the down arrow in the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transformation Type Mapping' space='']** combo box. These type maps specify the mapping rules that will be applied to the model during the transformation.

When you select a particular map, its contents are displayed in a table below. Each row in the table is a rule to remap one particular type to another. The **From type** and **To type** columns in the table show the source and target types.

The **Run type mapping in reverse order** check box creates the opposite type mapping. Type maps can be bidirectional, e.g., the same type map is reused both in the UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations. This checkbox governs the direction in which the type map should be used.

The following operations are available in the **Select type mapping** window:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Back | Return to the previous dialog box. |
| Next | Proceed to the next step (in this case, Specify transformation details). NOTE: This button is disabled during the Any to Any transformations. |
| Finish | Finish the configuration of the transformation. The Model Transformations Wizard exits and the transformation results appear in the project. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The fourth step in the wizard allows for selecting a type map that will be applied during the transformation. Usually transformation has and brings in some predefined type map, but if you want, you can specify another type map.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_4th_step.png" /></ac:image></p><p>A type map can be regarded as a collection of rules of the form “Replace the usage of type X in the used project with the usage of type Y”.</p><p>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transformation Type Mapping" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[type map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is a model object, i.e. a package with a collection of dependencies, hence all model manipulation operations can be performed on it. In particular, it can be refactored into a used project and mounted into any project, which needs it. It can be a simple package in your project as well, if you need a custom, one-off type map. A predefined type map can be taken from the modeling tool module and edited.</p><p>To see a list of the type maps available in your model, click the down arrow in the <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Transformation Type Mapping" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Transformation type map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> combo box. These type maps specify the mapping rules that will be applied to the model during the transformation.</p><p>When you select a particular map, its contents are displayed in a table below. Each row in the table is a rule to remap one particular type to another. The <strong>From type</strong> and <strong>To type</strong> columns in the table show the source and target types.</p><p>The <strong>Run type mapping in reverse order</strong> check box creates the opposite type mapping. Type maps can be bidirectional, e.g., the same type map is reused both in the UML to XML schema and XML schema to UML transformations. This checkbox governs the direction in which the type map should be used.</p><p>The following operations are available in the <strong>Select type mapping</strong> window:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Back</strong></td><td>Return to the previous dialog box.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Next</strong></td><td><p>Proceed to the next step (in this case, Specify transformation details). <br /><strong>NOTE:</strong> This button is disabled during the <strong>Any to Any</strong> transformations.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Finish</strong></td><td><p>Finish the configuration of the transformation. The <strong>Model Transformations Wizard</strong> exits and the transformation results appear in the project.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968205 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06200: Selecting values

- page_id: `243968205`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968205/Selecting+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:53.020+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements > Editing property values
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Selectable value type property values are selected from lists. There can be two types of lists:

- **Non-editable**- for properties with value ranges restricted by UML (for example, Visibility, Message Sort, Event Type).
- **Editable**- for properties with value ranges not restricted by UML and can be defined by the user (for example, Type Modifier, Multiplicity).

Learn about selecting property values from lists in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting single property value from non-editable list' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting single property value from editable list' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting multiple property values' space='']

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

true1[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Selectable value type property values are selected from lists. There can be two types of lists:</span></p><ul><li style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Non-editable </strong><span>- for properties with value ranges restricted by UML (for example, Visibility, <span>Message Sort, Event Type).</span></span></span></span></span></li><li style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Editable </strong><span>- for properties with value ranges not restricted by UML and can be defined by <span>the user (for example, Type Modifier, Multiplicity).<br /></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span>Learn about selecting property values from lists in:</span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span><span non-editable list" /></ac:link></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span /></ac:link></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(124,133,153);"><span <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967312 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 06201: Selection and multiple selections

- page_id: `243967312`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967312/Selection+and+multiple+selections
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:28.216+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

570038830

570038832

570038831

To select a shape

- Click the desired shape on the Diagram pane.

To select all shapes of the same type

- Press and hold the Alt key, and click one shape.

To select all shapes on the diagram, do one of the following

- From the Edit menu, choose Select All .
- Press Ctrl+A.

To make multiple selections, do one of the following

- Select a shape on the diagram pane and, holding down Shift, select as many shapes as you need.
- Drag the cursor diagonally across the area you wish to select.

##### Select a group of shapes

To select a group of shapes

- Drag the cursor diagonally across the area you wish to select.

This is a simple and fast way to select a group of shapes on the diagram.

The following rectangular selection modes are available:

1. Partial coverage. Symbols that are only partly covered with the rectangular selector are selected.
2. Complete coverage. Only those symbols that are fully covered by the rectangular selector are selected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The default selection mode is Partial coverage.

To change the group selection mode from Partial coverage to Complete coverage mode and vice versa

- Press Ctrl and drag the cursor diagonally across the area you want to select.

To change the group selection mode for the whole project, do one of the following

- In the main diagram toolbar, click the Complete coverage mode for group selection button.
- In the Environment Options dialog, Diagram > Symbols Manipulation group, change the property of the Group selection mode option.

570038829

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagramming' space='']

Symbols that are only partly covered with the rectangular selector are selected.</li><li>Complete coverage. Only those symbols that are fully covered by the rectangular selector are selected.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="complete_coverage_mode_button.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The default selection mode is Partial coverage.</p><p><br /></p><p>To change the group selection mode from Partial coverage to Complete coverage mode and vice versa</p><hr /><ul><li>Press Ctrl and drag the cursor diagonally across the area you want to select. </li></ul><p><br /></p><p>To change the group selection mode for the whole project, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>In the main diagram toolbar, click the <em>Complete coverage mode for group selection</em> button.</li><li>In the Environment Options dialog, <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Diagram</strong> &gt; <strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Symbols Manipulation</strong> group, change the property of the Group selection mode option.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c45236a8-b9d5-44a3-a89f-0e47ecfc276d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">570038829</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Diagramming" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567541 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06202: Selector and field

- page_id: `249567541`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567541/Selector+and+field
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:31:02.649+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > XML schemas > XML schema mapping to UML elements
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Sample of XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions

36447579

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Sample of XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions

36447582

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Sample of XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions

36447580

Maps to UML TaggedValues named “selector” and “field” of a UML Attribute representing key, keyRef, or unique. The “selector” tag has a value representing the “xpath” and “field," a list of values representing the field “xpath”. ID values shall be skipped and annotation documentation will be applied to the tagged value according to the annotation rule (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Annotation' space='']). For the values annotation field, documentation shall be merged into one.

#### PANEL: selector and field mapping to UML attributes

selector and field mapping to UML attributes

```text
<selector	id = ID
```

```text
xpath = a subset of XPath expression, see below
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</selector>
```

```text
<field
```

```text
id = ID
```

```text
xpath = a subset of XPath expression, see below
```

```text
{any attributes with non-schema namespace…}>
```

```text
Content: (annotation?)
```

```text
</field>
```

#### PANEL: selector and field XML code sample

selector and field XML code sample

```text
<xs:key name = "fullName">
```

```text
<xs:selector xpath=".//person"/>	<xs:field xpath="forename"/>	<xs:field xpath="surname"/></xs:key>
```

```text
<xs:keyref name="personRef" refer="fullName">	<xs:selector xpath=".//personPointer"/>	<xs:field xpath="@first"/>
```

```text
<xs:field xpath="@1ast"/>
```

```text
</xs:keyref>
```

```text
<xs:unique name="nearlyID">	<xs:selector xpath=".//*"/>	<xs:field xpath="@id"/></xs:unique>
```

##### XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions

#### PANEL: Sample of XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions

Sample of XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions

```text
<xs:element name="state">
```

```text
<xs:complexType>
```

```text
<xs:sequence>
```

```text
<xs:element name="code" type="twoLetterCode"/>			<xs:element ref="vehicle" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>			<xs:element ref="person" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>		</xs:sequence>
```

```text
</xs:complexType>
```

```text
<xs:key name="reg"> <!-- vehicles are keyed by their plate within states -->
```

```text
<xs:selector xpath=".//vehicle"/>
```

```text
<xs:field xpath="@plateNumber"/>
```

```text
</xs:key>
```

```text
</xs:element><xs:element name="root">	<xs:complexType>
```

```text
<xs:sequence>
```

```text
…
```

```text
<xs:element ref="state" maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
```

```text
…
```

```text
</xs:sequence> 	</xs:complexType>
```

```text
<xs:key name="state"> <!-- states are keyed by their code --> 		<xs:selector xpath=" .//state"/>
```

```text
<xs:field xpath="code"/>
```

```text
</xs:key>
```

```text
<xs:keyref name="vehicleState" refer="state"> <!-- every vehicle refers to its state -->		<xs:selector xpath=".//vehicle"/>
```

```text
<xs:field xpath="@state"/>
```

```text
</xs:keyref>
```

```text
<xs:key name="regKey"> <!-- vehicles are keyed by a pair of state and plate-->
```

```text
<xs:selector xpath=".//vehicle"/>		<xs:field xpath="@state"/>
```

```text
<xs:field xpath="@plateNumber"/>	</xs:key>
```

```text
<xs:keyref name="carRef" refer="regKey"› <!-- people's cars are a reference -->
```

```text
<xs:selector xpath=".//car"/>		<xs:field xpath="@regState"/>		<xs:field xpath="@regPlate"/>	</xs:keyref>
```

```text
</xs:element>
```

```text
<xs:element name="person"›	<xs:complexType>
```

```text
<xs:seguence>
```

```text
...
```

```text
<xs:element name="car">				<xs:complexType>
```

```text
<xs:attribute name="regState" type="twoLetterCode"/>					<xs:attribute name="regPlate" type="xs:integer"/> 				</xs:complexType>
```

```text
</xs:element>
```

```text
</xs:sequence>
```

```text
</xs:complexType>
```

```text
</xs:element>
```

A state element is defined, which contains a code child and some vehicle and person children. A vehicle in turn has a plateNumber attribute, which is an integer, and a state attribute. State's code s are a key for them within the document. Vehicle's plateNumber s are a key for them within states, and state and plateNumber is asserted to be a key for vehicle within the document as a whole. Furthermore, a person element has an empty car child, with regState and regPlate attributes, which are then asserted together to refer to vehicles via the carRef constraint. The requirement that a vehicle's state match its containing state's code is not expressed here.

For selector and field UML model example, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Keyref' space=''].

ID values shall be skipped and annotation documentation will be applied to the tagged value according to the annotation rule (see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Annotation" /></ac:link>). refer=&quot;fullName&quot;&gt;<br />	&lt;xs:selector xpath=&quot;.//personPointer&quot;/&gt;<br />	&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@first&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@1ast&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:keyref&gt;<br /><br /></pre><pre>&lt;xs:unique name=&quot;nearlyID&quot;&gt;<br />	&lt;xs:selector xpath=&quot;.//*&quot;/&gt;<br />	&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@id&quot;/&gt;<br />&lt;/xs:unique&gt;<br /><br /><br /></pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="feefb804-d771-4c41-9118-d6d910f5a816"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of XML representations for the three kinds of identity-constraint definitions</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><pre>&lt;xs:element name=&quot;state&quot;&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:sequence&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:element name=&quot;code&quot; type=&quot;twoLetterCode&quot;/&gt;<br />			&lt;xs:element ref=&quot;vehicle&quot; maxOccurs=&quot;unbounded&quot;/&gt;<br />			&lt;xs:element ref=&quot;person&quot; maxOccurs=&quot;unbounded&quot;/&gt;<br />		&lt;/xs:sequence&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre><br />	&lt;xs:key name=&quot;reg&quot;&gt; &lt;!-- vehicles are keyed by their plate within states --&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:selector xpath=&quot;.//vehicle&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@plateNumber&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:key&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:element&gt;<br /><br />&lt;xs:element name=&quot;root&quot;&gt;<br />	&lt;xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:sequence&gt;</pre><pre>			…</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:element ref=&quot;state&quot; maxOccurs=&quot;unbounded&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>			…</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:sequence&gt; <br />	&lt;/xs:complexType&gt;<br /><br /></pre><pre>	&lt;xs:key name=&quot;state&quot;&gt; &lt;!-- states are keyed by their code --&gt; <br />		&lt;xs:selector xpath=&quot; .//state&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;code&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:key&gt;<br /><br /></pre><pre>	&lt;xs:keyref name=&quot;vehicleState&quot; refer=&quot;state&quot;&gt; &lt;!-- every vehicle refers to its state --&gt;<br />		&lt;xs:selector xpath=&quot;.//vehicle&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@state&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:keyref&gt;<br /><br /></pre><pre>	&lt;xs:key name=&quot;regKey&quot;&gt; &lt;!-- vehicles are keyed by a pair of state and plate--&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:selector xpath=&quot;.//vehicle&quot;/&gt;<br />		&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@state&quot;/&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@plateNumber&quot;/&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:key&gt;<br /><br /></pre><pre>	&lt;xs:keyref name=&quot;carRef&quot; refer=&quot;regKey&quot;› &lt;!-- people's cars are a reference --&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:selector xpath=&quot;.//car&quot;/&gt;<br />		&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@regState&quot;/&gt;<br />		&lt;xs:field xpath=&quot;@regPlate&quot;/&gt;<br />	&lt;/xs:keyref&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:element&gt;<br /><br /></pre><pre>&lt;xs:element name=&quot;person&quot;›<br />	&lt;xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;xs:seguence&gt;</pre><pre>...</pre><pre>			&lt;xs:element name=&quot;car&quot;&gt;<br />				&lt;xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>					&lt;xs:attribute name=&quot;regState&quot; type=&quot;twoLetterCode&quot;/&gt;<br />					&lt;xs:attribute name=&quot;regPlate&quot; type=&quot;xs:integer&quot;/&gt; <br />				&lt;/xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>			&lt;/xs:element&gt;</pre><pre>		&lt;/xs:sequence&gt;</pre><pre>	&lt;/xs:complexType&gt;</pre><pre>&lt;/xs:element&gt;</pre></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A state element is defined, which contains a code child and some vehicle and person children. A vehicle in turn has a plateNumber attribute, which is an integer, and a state attribute. State's code s are a key for them within the document. Vehicle's plateNumber s are a key for them within states, and state and plateNumber is asserted to be a key for vehicle within the document as a whole. Furthermore, a person element has an empty car child, with regState and regPlate attributes, which are then asserted together to refer to vehicles via the carRef constraint. The requirement that a vehicle's state match its containing state's code is not expressed here.</p><p>For selector and field UML model example, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Keyref" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566493 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06203: Send Task

- page_id: `249566493`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566493/Send+Task
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:22:05.498+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram > Activities > Tasks
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1485782623

1485782624

1485782624

**Description**

A Send Task is a simple task that is designed to send a message to an external participant. Once the message has been sent, the task is completed.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1485782622

**Related elements**

- Activities
- Task
- Service Task
- Receive Task
- User Task
- Manual Task
- Business Rule Task
- Script Task

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram

**Related procedure**

- Creating and Using Tasks

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="97964fc9-fe78-4713-91e4-a83981a93268"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485782623</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="35a2444b-3969-480b-922c-2943cb69a278"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485782624</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="af4ddfb8-d628-4b41-a0be-d6e3410979f4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485782624</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Send Task is a simple task that is designed to send a message to an external participant. Once the message has been sent, the task is completed.</p><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="send_task.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="840b6fb0-e544-41da-b3c4-36f9cfdeba6b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1485782622</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Activities">Activities</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Task">Task</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Service+Task">Service Task</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Receive+Task">Receive Task</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/User+Task">User Task</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Manual+Task">Manual Task</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Business+Rule+Task">Business Rule Task</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Script+Task">Script Task</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Creating+and+Using+Tasks">Creating and Using Tasks</a></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968101 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06204: Separator

- page_id: `243968101`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968101/Separator
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:50.317+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Auxiliary diagram symbols
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

490991590

490991592

490991591

You can use either a horizontal or vertical separator to rule off different parts of a diagram. The **Rotate** button allows changing the separator from the horizontal to vertical position, and vice versa.

See some examples of the types of separators in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using the vertical, horizontal, and rectangular separators

You can format the separator symbol properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']**Symbol Properties** dialog.

You can display text next to the separator as plain text or HTML text. You can edit the HTML text using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='HTML editor' space=''].

To change separator properties

1. From the separator's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties or select the separator and press Alt+Enter.
2. The Symbol Properties dialog opens. In that dialog, set particular property values, such as Text Position , Line Style , Line Width and other, as you need.
3. Click Close , when you are done.

To change the separator's position

1. Select the separator on the diagram pane.
2. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='Screen Shot 2017-06-28 at 5.32.52 PM.png'] Rotate button at the corner of the selection.

To select the type of separator from the diagram palette

1. Find the Separator under the Common pallet.
2. Click the arrow on the right side of the button to reveal a drop-down menu with the following options: Vertical Separator , Horizontal Separator , and Rectangular Shape .
3. Select the separator you would like to use for your model.

To set the text position on a separator

1. From the separator's shortcut menu, select Symbol Properties .
2. In the Symbol Properties dialog, change the Text Horizontal Position property to one of the following: Left (Default), Center , or Right .

To set a separator line style

1. From the separator's shortcut menu, select Line style .
2. Select the style you need: Dashed (Default), Solid , or Dotted .

490991589

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Auxiliary diagram symbols' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966411 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06205: Sequence diagram

- page_id: `243966411`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966411/Sequence+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:59.212+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

307649973

307649975

307649974

INLINE

**Overview**

The Sequence diagram is a type of Interaction diagram (which falls under the behavioral diagramming family) that focuses on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Message' space='MED']interchange between various [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lifeline' space='MED'].

A Sequence diagram shows the interaction information with an emphasis on the time sequence. The diagram has two dimensions: the vertical axis representing time and the horizontal axis representing the participating objects. The time axis could be an actual reference point by placing the time labels as text boxes. The horizontal ordering of the objects is not significant to the operation, and you can rearrange them as necessary.

There are two Sequence Diagram modeling approaches:

- Activation bars as the lifelines.
- Activation bars as the Execution Specifications (new modeling approach).

**Purpose**

The purpose of the Sequence diagram is to represent some aspect of the communication between objects to perform a task. The main benefit of the diagram is that it helps to identify the messages exchanged between objects.

**Usage**

A Sequence diagram can be used to:

- represent the details of a UML Use Case
- model the logic of a sophisticated procedure, function, or operation
- analyze the interaction of objects and components to complete a process
- plan and understand the detailed functionality of an existing or future scenario.

**Summary**

Sequence diagrams are valuable because they:

- allow seeing the specific questions, commands, and data being communicated during the execution of a specific task
- identify the communication required to fulfill an interaction
- identify the objects that participate in an interaction
- identify the data that is passed as part of the interactions.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a Sequence diagram

307649971

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dragging objects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Combined Fragment' space='MED']

The diagram has two dimensions: the vertical axis representing time and the horizontal axis representing the participating objects. The time axis could be an actual reference point by placing the time labels as text boxes. The horizontal ordering of the objects is not significant to the operation, and you can rearrange them as necessary.</p><p>There are two Sequence Diagram modeling approaches:</p><ul><li>Activation bars as the lifelines.</li><li>Activation bars as the Execution Specifications (new modeling approach).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Purpose</strong></p><p>The purpose of the Sequence diagram is to represent some aspect of the communication between objects to perform a task. The main benefit of the diagram is that it helps to identify the messages exchanged between objects. </p><p><strong>Usage</strong></p><p>A Sequence diagram can be used to: </p><ul><li>represent the details of a UML Use Case</li><li>model the logic of a sophisticated procedure, function, or operation</li><li>analyze the interaction of objects and components to complete a process</li><li>plan and understand the detailed functionality of an existing or future scenario. </li></ul><p><strong>Summary</strong></p><p>Sequence diagrams are valuable because they:</p><ul><li>allow seeing the specific questions, commands, and data being communicated during the execution of a specific task</li><li>identify the communication required to fulfill an interaction</li><li>identify the objects that participate in an interaction</li><li>identify the data that is passed as part of the interactions. </li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sequence_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of a Sequence diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5ef1b413-2340-4506-a10f-a90ce9158c43"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">307649971</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="28af2e40-ad93-46f6-9884-ed9d4ac7f968" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="DragginginSequencediagrams"><ri:page ri:content-title="Dragging objects" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Dragging in Sequence diagrams]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Combined Fragment" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
