The Services Structure (Sv-Sr) domain shows the the composition of services and how services are combined into a higher level service required to exhibit a capability or support an operational activity.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564255 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06226: Services Taxonomy

- page_id: `249564255`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564255/Services+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:10.987+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

921953081

921953084

921953083

**Description**

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain shows the service types and required and provided service levels of these types.

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain specifies a hierarchy of services. The elements in the hierarchy are services (i.e., service interfaces), and the relationships between the elements are specializations – i.e., one Service is a special type of another.

**Implementation**

The Services Taxonomy (Sv-Tx) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Taxonomy diagram' space=''] , which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services Taxonomy table' space=''] . This table can be used to create and describe major domain elements faster.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of Services Taxonomy diagram

921953080

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Measurement' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Measurement Set' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Property Set' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Property Set' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Provided Service Level' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Required Service Level' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Policy' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564270 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06227: Services Traceability

- page_id: `249564270`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564270/Services+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:11.247+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Services
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922069205

922069207

922069206

**Description**

The Services Traceability (Sv-St) domain shows the traceability between operational activities and services that support them and depicts how services contribute to the achievement of a capability.

**Implementation**

The Services Traceability (Sv-St) domain is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space=''] . The intended usage of the Service Exhibits Traceability includes:
  - Tracing Capability requirements to Services .
  - Capability audit.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Operational Activities Mapping matrix' space='']. 
The intended usage of the Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix includes:
  - Tracing service functional requirements to user requirements.
  - Tracing solution options to requirements.
  - Identification of overlaps or gaps.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix' space=''] . The Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services to Capabilities Mapping Matrix

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Services to Operational Activities Mapping Matrix

922069204

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562637 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06228: Services viewpoint

- page_id: `249562637`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562637/Services+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:38.134+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['services-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394609712

394609714

394609713

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Services Viewpoint describes services and their interconnections providing or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Service Models associate service resources to the operational and capability requirements. These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

394609711

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe3fef66-fa3e-413c-90fb-99c497b3c658"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609712</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a3f99e09-18a9-4aab-be9f-618b4506968b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609714</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="911042dd-8152-48cd-97d4-803d645a0bc6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609713</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>“The DoDAF-described Models within the Services Viewpoint describes services and their interconnections providing or supporting, DoD functions. DoD functions include both warfighting and business functions. The Service Models associate service resources to the operational and capability requirements. These resources support the operational activities and facilitate the exchange of information. The relationship between architectural data elements across the Services Viewpoint to the Operational Viewpoint and Capability Viewpoint can be exemplified as services are procured and fielded to support the operations and capabilities of organizations. The structural and behavioral models in the OVs and SvcVs allow architects and stakeholders to quickly ascertain which functions are carried out by humans and which by Services for each alternative specification and so carry out trade analysis based on risk, cost, reliability, etc.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d17dbc01-880e-42a0-a24d-4eb8b80deb66"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394609711</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c9c73c39-ed1b-4d6e-b5f3-011a350b97f9" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968680 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06229: Setting custom transformation properties

- page_id: `243968680`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968680/Setting+custom+transformation+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:07.091+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Working with Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **Transformation Details**panel displays the various properties of a specific transformation, selected in the first step of the **Model Transformation Wizard**. Each transformation type has its own set of options, which govern functionality of that transformation.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To change the transformation properties to the default values, click **Reset to Defaults**.

The following operations are available in the **Specify transformation details** window:

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Back | Return to the previous dialog box. |
| Finish | Finish the transformation configuration. The Model Transformations Wizard exits and the transformation results appear in the project. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>Transformation Details </strong>panel displays the various properties of a specific transformation, selected in the first step of the <strong>Model Transformation Wizard</strong>. Each transformation type has its own set of options, which govern functionality of that transformation.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_transformation_wizard_5th_step.png" /></ac:image></p><p>To change the transformation properties to the default values, click <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong>.</p><p>The following operations are available in the <strong>Specify transformation details</strong> window:</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Button</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Back</strong></td><td>Return to the previous dialog box.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Finish</strong></td><td><p>Finish the transformation configuration. The <strong>Model Transformations Wizard</strong> exits and the transformation results appear in the project.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968500 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06230: Setting date and time

- page_id: `243968500`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968500/Setting+date+and+time
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:00.813+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Date and time are set in the **Date and Time Settings** dialog. This dialog is always used when specifying the properties that have a date type value. It allows you to select a date, time, and time zone using a visual calendar and/or the **Date**, **Time**, and **Time zone** boxes.

#### NOTE: Date/time format information

Date/time format information

The **Date and Time Settings** dialog uses your computer's locale to set the date and time format. You can still enter date and time values of another format manually in the **Set date and time** box, but the dialog will not recognize them; i.e., you won't be able to use a calendar or the **Date** and **Time** boxes. Using them would automatically change the date and time format to the one specified in your computer's locale.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The figure displays the Date and Time Settings dialog with specified date, time and time zone values.

The **Date and Time Settings** dialog can be opened from:

- The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of an element that has a property with a date type value
- A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generic table' space=''] that has a column of date type values.

##### The Date and Time Settings dialog elements

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Set the date and time | Text box | Allows you to enter date and time values in the following order:DateTime (optional)Time zone (optional)Values should be separated by spaces. |
| Date | Visual calendar | To select a date, click a day on the calendar. To switch between months, use the arrows on the left and right sides of the title section of the calendar. To switch between years, use the double-arrows on the left and right sides of the title section of the calendar.The selected date is automatically entered in the Set the date and time box using the format specified in your computer's locale. |
| Date | Spin box | Allows you to select a date value in the format specified in your computer's locale. The default value is the current date.To change a date value, use the up or down arrow to increase or decrease the current value. The selected value is automatically entered in the Set the date and time box. |
| Time | Spin box | Allows you to select a time value in the format specified in your computer's locale. The default value is the current time.To change a time value, use the up or down arrow to increase or decrease the current value. The selected value is automatically entered in the Set the date and time box. |
| Time zone | Drop-down list | Click the arrow on the right side of the box to select the desired time zone. |
| Today | Button | Click the button to set the Date box value to the current date of your computer. |
| Now | Button | Click the button to set the Time box value to the current time of your computer. |
| Default | Button | Click the button to set the Time zone box value to the value set in your computer. |

#### PANEL: On this page

On this page

4

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Default property values' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extracting' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Formatting symbols' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing lists of properties and commands' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Date and time are set in the <strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog. This dialog is always used when specifying the properties that have a date type value. It allows you to select a date, time, and time zone using a visual calendar and/or the <strong>Date</strong>, <strong>Time</strong>, and <strong>Time zone</strong> boxes.<br /> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5d911e83-2fca-4f6d-bbcf-05b26aeb2fec"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Date/time format information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog uses your computer's locale to set the date and time format. You can still enter date and time values of another format manually in the <strong>Set date and time</strong> box, but the dialog will not recognize them; i.e., you won't be able to use a calendar or the <strong>Date</strong> and <strong>Time</strong> boxes. Using them would automatically change the date and time format to the one specified in your computer's locale.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><br /><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="date_and_time_settings_dialog.png" /></ac:image><br /><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">The figure displays the Date and Time Settings dialog with specified date, time and time zone values.</span></h6><p>The <strong>Date and Time Settings</strong> dialog can be opened from:</p><ul><li>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link> of an element that has a property with a date type value</li><li>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generic table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generic Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> that has a column of date type values.</li></ul><h3><br />The Date and Time Settings dialog elements</h3><table class="relative-table" style="width: 78.0604%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 14.2857%;" /><col style="width: 16.2245%;" /><col style="width: 69.4898%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Dialog element</th><th>Element type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Set the date and time</strong></td><td>Text box</td><td><p>Allows you to enter date and time values in the following order:</p><ul><li>Date</li><li>Time (optional)</li><li>Time zone (optional)</li></ul><p>Values should be separated by spaces.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Date</strong></td><td>Visual calendar</td><td><p>To select a date, click a day on the calendar. To switch between months, use the arrows on the left and right sides of the title section of the calendar. To switch between years, use the double-arrows on the left and right sides of the title section of the calendar.</p><p>The selected date is automatically entered in the<strong> Set the date and time</strong> box using the format specified in your computer's locale.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Date</strong></td><td>Spin box</td><td><p>Allows you to select a date value in the format specified in your computer's locale. The default value is the current date.</p><p>To change a date value, use the up or down arrow to increase or decrease the current value. The selected value is automatically entered in the <strong>Set the date and time</strong><span> box.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Time</strong></td><td>Spin box</td><td><p>Allows you to select a time value in the format specified in your computer's locale. The default value is the current time.</p><p>To change a time value, use the up or down arrow to increase or decrease the current value. <span>The selected value is automatically entered in the </span><strong>Set the date and time</strong><span> box.</span></p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Time zone</strong></td><td> Drop-down list</td><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p>Click the arrow on the right side of the box to select the desired time zone.</p></div></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Today</strong></td><td colspan="1">Button</td><td colspan="1"><span>Click the button to set the </span><strong>Date</strong><span> box value to the current date of your computer.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Now</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Button</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Click the button to set the </span><strong>Time</strong><span> box value to the current time of your computer.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><strong>Default</strong></td><td colspan="1"><span>Button</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>Click the button to set the </span><strong>Time</strong><span><strong> zone</strong> box value to the value set in your computer.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61ead333-dfa1-4b64-ab45-56ccb8c36d74"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">On this page</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="997038c4-d158-4f16-aa95-a5a33d73cdec"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967366 space=MT version=4 -->
````
## PAGE 06231: Setting diagram as layout template

- page_id: `243967366`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967366/Setting+diagram+as+layout+template
- version_number: 4
- version_date: `2025-09-10T12:18:33.079+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After the layout template (appearance of the required Class and its Ports in the Class diagram) is defined, you can now use it as the layout template, which you can reuse in other diagrams.The procedure below describes how to set the diagram as the layout template. Note that the layout template name automatically becomes the same as diagram name. This will be important when applying the layout template, and choosing which template you want to apply.

To set the Class Diagram as a layout template

1. Open the Class Diagram that you want to set as layout template.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Right click the diagram and select Display > Layout Template > Use as Layout Template .
  - Ensure that nothing is selected on the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] , then in the diagram toolbar, click [ATTACHMENT filename='display_icon.png'] and select Layout Template > Use as Layout Template .

The «layoutTemplate» stereotype appears on the diagram header (see the figure below).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The Definition BDD is set as the layout template named Definition. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.

#### INFO: Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template

- From the version 19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.
- If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating layout template' space=''] directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable**which you can download here: *[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>After the layout template (appearance of the required Class and its Ports in the Class diagram) is defined, you can now use it as the layout template, which you can reuse in other diagrams.<span class="confluence-link"> The procedure below describes how to set the diagram as the layout template. Note that the layout template name automatically becomes the same as diagram name. This will be important when applying the layout template, and choosing which template you want to apply.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To set the Class Diagram as a layout template</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="6c17c076-1d61-4217-aa4b-968d40617539">Open the Class Diagram that you want to set as layout template.</li><li data-uuid="3262ba58-2a85-488b-9039-84682562d95c">Do one of the following:<ul><li>Right click the diagram and select <strong>Display</strong> &gt; <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Use as Layout Template</strong>.</li><li>Ensure that nothing is selected on the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[diagram pane]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, then in the diagram toolbar, click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_icon.png" /></ac:image> and select <strong>Layout Template</strong> &gt; <strong>Use as Layout Template</strong>.</li></ul></li></ol><p><br />The «layoutTemplate» stereotype appears on the diagram header (see the figure below).</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="defined_layout_template.png" /></ac:image>The Definition BDD is set as the layout template named Definition. The illustration displays concepts from SysML v1 Plugin.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="136bf21e-c771-40e3-b5f7-62d0f1b41818"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Composite Structure Diagram as a layout template</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="8b5b4098-2335-436d-8129-6079e29eba92">From the <span>version </span>19.0 SP1, you can define the appearance and layout of a Part Property with its Ports in the Composite Structure Diagram and set this diagram as layout template.</li><li data-uuid="bf65c15f-5625-4942-bc15-460a23688f4f">If a Composite Structure Diagram displays several Part Properties, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating layout template" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[create a layout template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> directly from a particular Part Property. It's appearance and layout will be copied to the template automatically.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample model</strong></p><p>The sample model used in the figure of this page is <strong>Modem Cable </strong>which you can download here: <span class="confluence-link"><em><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&amp;modificationDate=1481817717837&amp;api=v2">Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip</a>.</em></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971107 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06232: Setting image DPI

- page_id: `243971107`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971107/Setting+image+DPI
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:03.865+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Generating Reports from Report Wizard
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Magic Report provides three levels to set DPI for images in generated reports as follows:

1. Global level By adding the template.output.image.dpi variable in the Report Environment options, this DPI setting takes effect in images in any reports generated from any templates.
2. **Template** level 
The**ImageDPI** variable supported in Report Data of a template can be added so that the defined DPI value takes effect in images in reports generated from this template. NoteThe template level setting has a higher priority than the global level setting.
3. **Object** level 
Supporting**$report.getImageFor($element, $dpi)**, this DPI setting takes effect in some images in a template whereas: NoteThe object level setting has the highest priority. The DPI from this level setting overrides the global and template level settings and takes effect in specified images.
  - $element is a modeling tool element. The image of $element is returned with specified DPI.
  - $dpi is an Integer.
    - The normal value boundary of the integer is 1 to 4800.
      - If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI setting in the template and global levels will be used respectively.
      - If more than 4800, the value will be rounded down to 4800.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Magic Report provides three levels to set DPI for images in generated reports as follows:</p><ol><li><strong>Global</strong> level<br />By adding the<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> template.output.image.dpi</strong> variable in the Report Environment options, this DPI setting takes effect in images in any reports generated from any templates.<br /><br /></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Template</strong> level<br />The<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> ImageDPI</strong> variable supported in Report Data of a template can be added so that the defined DPI value takes effect in images in reports generated from this template.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bb0523d6-b7f8-49cb-98cc-5dc23d7a6327"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The template level setting has a higher priority than the global level setting.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Object</strong> level<br />Supporting<strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> $report.getImageFor($element, $dpi)</strong>, this DPI setting takes effect in some images in a template whereas:</p><ul class="alternate"><li><strong>$element</strong> is a modeling tool element. The image of $element is returned with specified DPI.</li><li><strong>$dpi</strong> is an Integer.<ul class="alternate"><li>The normal value boundary of the integer is 1 to 4800.<ul class="alternate"><li>If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI setting in the template and global levels will be used respectively.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">If more than 4800, the value will be rounded down to 4800.</p></li></ul></li></ul></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b351b559-0d05-4791-af7a-4b0d9bd621f7"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The object level setting has the highest priority. The DPI from this level setting overrides the global and template level settings and takes effect in specified images.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562292 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06233: Setting initial values

- page_id: `249562292`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562292/Setting+initial+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T12:49:36.546+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Exporting to External Simulation Models > Sample models > Creating a simple model
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The last step of the modeling is to set the value for the resistance in the circuit. This can be done by creating two *InstanceSpecifications*, one for the source and one for the resistor. Values are used for the amplitude of the source, and for the resistance of the resistor respectively. These*InstanceSpecifications* are then set as default values of the circuit source and the circuit resistor (see the image below).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The *Source* and *Resistor* Instance Specifications with set default values.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The last step of the modeling is to set the value for the resistance in the circuit. This can be done by creating two <em>InstanceSpecifications</em>, one for the source and one for the resistor. Values are used for the amplitude of the source, and for the resistance of the resistor respectively. These<em> InstanceSpecifications</em> are then set as default values of the circuit source and the circuit resistor (see the image below).  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="400"><ri:attachment ri:filename="initial_value_of_the_resistor.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The <em>Source</em> and <em>Resistor</em> Instance Specifications with set default values.</h6><p />
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967382 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06234: Setting layout template as default

- page_id: `243967382`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967382/Setting+layout+template+as+default
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:30.504+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

59632409

59632412

59632411

If you have a commonly used layout template, you can set it as the default. The default layout template will always be the first and separated by a line when trying to apply or open the layout templates. Only one layout template can be set as the default. The procedures below explain how to:

- .
- .

###### setSetting the layout template as default template

To set the layout template as default template

1. From the diagram shortcut menu, select Specification .
2. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , select the Tags property group.
3. Find the <<layoutTemplate>> tag, and select its default property. [ATTACHMENT filename='layout_template_tag_specification_window.png']
4. Click the Create Value button to create a new value for the <<layoutTemplate>> tag, if it is not already created.
5. Ensure that the Value check box is selected and set to true . [ATTACHMENT filename='layout_template_tag_value.png']
6. Click Close . The layout template diagram is now set as the default layout.

###### changeChanging the default layout template

To change the default layout template

1. Open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] of the default layout template diagram.
2. Select the Tags property group.
3. Clear the Value check box to set it to false . [ATTACHMENT filename='layout_template_tag_value_false.png']
4. Click Close .
5. Open the layout diagram template that you want to be the default template.
6. Repeat all steps of the .

59632408

**Sample model**

The sample model used in the figure of this page is **Modem Cable** which you can download here:*[Modem Cable Layout Templates.mdzip](https://docs.nomagic.com/download/attachments/9915051/Modem%20Cable%20Layout%20Templates.mdzip?version=1&modificationDate=1481817717837&api=v2).*
