symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to ask to update outdated 'Defined Elsewhere' symbol styles.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Suspect Links</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Enable Tracking</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to start tracking suspect links.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Deprecated Elements</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track element deletions.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Track Links in main Project Only</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to track suspect links in the main project only.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Tracking Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select elements whose suspicions should be tracked.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" title="Background colour : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">SysML</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Propagate SysML Values</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If True, the propagation mechanism is turned on which allows value reconfiguration from the nested structures of the context. Propagated values will be displayed in the initialValue compartment. Set to False to turn off the Value Propagation mechanism. Then the values will be collected only from the specified instances.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Change allocated Behavior context</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, Behavior::context will be the Block to which behavior is allocated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Property on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the property or port (except Association End) that is default tool-generated is automatically changed to a type name after specifying the type for them. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Rename Association End on Type Change</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If set to true, the name of the Association End that is default tool-generated is automatically named as a type specified at the role end after connecting or reconnecting the Association. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Create Association on Part Creation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, an association between a part property type and part owner is created automatically.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Include Directed Features into Combined Direction of Proxy Port</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, includes all owned and inherited Directed Features of the property type into derived combined direction of the Proxy Port. Tread Provided as Out, Required as In direction. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Units</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If enabled, the units are shown next to values and can be specified or changed directly on a Value Specification.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Type Names in Property Path</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Use type name in property path &quot;dot notation&quot; when property name is empty.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Allocation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Ask and Remember </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Definition (Activity allocated to Block)</p><p>Usage (Activity allocated to Part)</p><p>Ask and Remember </p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the Usage mode to create allocation relationships between an Action and Part Property representing a swimlane in which the action is contained. Select the Definition mode to create an allocation relationship between a behavior of action (Activity) and Block representing a swimlane in which the action is contained.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Item Flow Creation Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Direct</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Between Part Types</p><p>Direct</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select the mode for managing Item Flow in your project:</p><p>Select &quot;Direct&quot; to create the Item Flow between ports or parts in SysML Internal Block Diagram.</p><p>Select &quot;Between Part Types&quot; to create the Item Flow between Part property types. The &quot;Between Part Types&quot; option also allows you to quickly realize and synchronize Flows on Activity Edges, Connectors, and Messages. </p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Remove Not Realized Item Flows</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to automatically remove Item Flows after the last element that realizes them is deleted. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Creation of Contextual Relationships</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Disabled</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Fully enabled</p><p>Partially Enabled</p><p>Disabled</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Select &quot;Fully Enabled&quot; to establish relationships contextualization throughout the project.</p><p>Select &quot;Partially Enabled&quot; to ignore the contextualization while creating relationships on the diagram pane.</p><p>Select &quot;Disabled&quot; to bypass contextualization.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Item Flows on Affected Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to update all affected diagrams and show the Conveyed Items of Item Flows realized on Connectors, Activity Edges, Messages, or Associations whenever a new Item Flow is created or an existing one is modified.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Check Item Flow Realization Compatibility</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set to true to check the Item Flow compatibility with the behavior model while running the Behavior to Structure Synchronization. <br />Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UAF Behavior to structure synchronization" /></ac:link>.</p><p>Learn more about <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Behavior to structure synchronization" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Behavior to structure synchronization]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></td></tr><tr><td>Generate Diagram Layout Annotations</td><td>true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Set the default option for generating diagram layout annotations for the Modelica export.</p></td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-grey" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background colour :" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="grey">UAF</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Architecture Framework</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0, UAF<br />UAF Enterprise Architecture</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The architecture framework option identifies the environment and a subset of aliases to use within the context of the project (i.e., UAF, DoDAF, MODAF, etc.). To change the framework, go to File &gt; Convert To.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">ISO Date Time format</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">YYYY-MM-DD</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Various, according to your locale</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select ISO Date Time format for setting dates for elements.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">None</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Available library versions</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies which MIL-STD-2525 Symbology Library Version to use for the project.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">BPMN Compliance Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the compliant BPMN stereotypes should be applied to UAF Elements. The options must be true to create UAF diagrams dependent on the Business Process Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Map UAF Information Elements on ERD Entities</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the Entity stereotype should be applied to the UAF Entity Item element. The options must be true to create a UAF Entity Relationship Diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Auto Check Model Completeness</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">If true, the user will be automatically notified about model incompleteness (recommended for novice UAF users). Note, that the auto-check may decrease the performance of the modeling tool. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of inherited Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Respect UAF Information Flows on Aggregation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specifies whether the realization of aggregated Operational Exchanges/Resource Exchanges is allowed or not. </td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Definition as Documentation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Specifies wether the Definition or Comment should be treated as Documentation for an annotaded UAF element.</p><p>Set to true to use the newly created Definition as documentation for the UAF element.</p><p>Set to false to keep using the Comment as documenation for the UAF element.</p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Property Sets owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Property Sets, that are created using Property Set Models such as SV-7, NSV-7, or SvcV-7.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Inform about user read-only projects after conversion</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to inform about user read-only projects that haven't been converted during project conversion.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><p>Specifies whether the default Package documentation should be shown in the UAF Packages Documentation property:</p><p>Set to true to show the default Package documentation for the newly created UAF Packages.</p><p>Set to false to create the new UAF Packages without the default documentation. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Show default documentation for UAF packages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more>>>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Sub-resources depth in Strategic Phasing Gantt Chart</span></td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">3</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">1-10</td><td style="width: 55.98%;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">Set the level of sub-resources hierarchy displayed in the Capability Provisions panel of the Strategic Phasing, CV-3, StV-3, NCV-3, or Cr Gantt Chart.</span></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Operational Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Operational Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Operational constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Operational Constraints, Business Rules, OV-6a, NOV-6a, or L8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Resource Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Resource Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Resource constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Resource Constraints, SV-10a, NSV-10a, or P8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Service Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Service Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Service Policies and Service Contracts constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner for the Service Policies constraints is Service Constraints, SOV-4a, SvcV-10a, or S8 and for the Service Contracts, the default owner is Service Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Strategic Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Strategic Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Strategic constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Strategic Constraints or C8. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Default Security Constraints Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;"> Security Constraints </td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">-</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Defines the default owner for Security constraints. Depending on the framework, the default owner is Security Constraints. Change the default owner by choosing a different one from the model.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Validation</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validation Scope</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation scope. The validation scope can be the entire model or some part of it. When the validation is run, each validation rule is evaluated for each suitable element in the validation scope.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Validate only Visible Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to validate only elements in one or more visible diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Exclude Elements from Used Read-Only Project</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to exclude projects used in read-only mode and the elements that exist in these projects. Excluded projects will not be validated.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Mark in Tree and Diagrams</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to highlight the invalid model elements in the browser with a small x symbol. The element symbol will be colored according to the severity level of the problem in the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules that should not be run during the automatic model validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When the validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Composition Inspection</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Standard</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Standard</p><p>Advanced</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Select the level of composition inspection in your projects. The Standard inspection searches for issues only in the project, while the Advanced inspection, in addition to the Standard inspection, searches for issues in all the used projects. The inspection runs validation rules which validate the correctness of model integrity and project composition.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Detect Illegal Model References in Shared Projects</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to check for illegal model references in projects. Illegal references are one of the main reasons why recovered elements appear in shared projects.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Suites</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation suites which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Ignored Validation Rules</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">-</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-selection</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the validation rules which should not be run during the passive diagram validation.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Minimal Severity</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">warning</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>debug</p><p>info</p><p>warning</p><p>error</p><p>fatal</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the minimal severity. When passive diagram validation is running, only the validation rules of the specified and the higher severity level will be run.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Diagram Info</strong></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 100.0%;" colspan="4">Diagram information shows summarized data in each diagram, e.g. the author, creation and modification dates, etc. Choose the diagram tags in standard mode, their values should be shown in a predefined diagram information table, or choose how diagram information should e represented using the HTML code.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#deebff" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light blue 35%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#deebff"><strong>Symbol styles</strong></td></tr><tr><td style="width: 100.0%;" colspan="4">Specify the shape, path, diagram, and stereotype symbol properties. Create, edit, clone, import/export, or remove element display styles. Also, set default styles or apply new styles to existing diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Default</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [255, 255, 204]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the fill color of the shape.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Shapes are colored on the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Pen Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [66, 66, 66]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the color of the path or lines around shapes.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Text Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [0, 0, 0]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the color of the text.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Line Width</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">1</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Real</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the line width of the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Font</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Arial 11</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-list</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the font style of the text.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Number Tag Name</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The option allows you to turn on or turn off the element number tag name on the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Element Number Display Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Before the element name</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Above the element name</p><p>Before the element name</p><p>Do not display on symbol</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The element number display mode option specifies the position on the symbol where the element number should be displayed.</td></tr><tr><td class="highlight-#f4f5f7" style="width: 100.0%;" title="Background color : Light grey 100%" colspan="4" data-highlight-colour="#f4f5f7">Shapes</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Fill Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [255, 255, 204]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Shapes are colored on the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Pen Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [66, 66, 66]</td><td 17.8038%;">Arial 11</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">multi-list</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the font style of the text.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Number Tag Name</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The option allows you to turn on or turn off the element number tag name on the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Element Number Display Mode</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Before the element name</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Above the element name</p><p>Before the element name</p><p>Do not display on symbol</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The element number display mode option specifies the position on the symbol where the element number should be displayed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Autosize</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Adjusts the size of a symbol to the contained information so that it uses minimum space.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Fixed Connection Points</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">When drawing a path between shapes, the end of the path is connected to the fixed point of the shape.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Resizing of Shape</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true to keep the original size of the selected shape while editing the model by cutting text to fit the shape. You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Displaying of Label</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. You can use the Show Suspended labels command (a small black square on the selected path) to show suspended labels on the diagram pane. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Label Orientation</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Horizontal</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Horizontal</p><p>Vertical</p><p>Automatic</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify the orientation of the labels. Automatic label orientation will rotate labels of symbols according to the symbol orientation or edge orientation on which the symbol is placed.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Header in Bold</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Shows the name of the symbol as bolded.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Text Vertical Position</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Top</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Top</p><p>Center</p><p>Bottom</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Choose text and compartments' vertical position within the symbol.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Stereotype Color</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">RGB [0, 0, 0]</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">Custom</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Change the color of the stereotype text label.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Stereotype Font</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Arial style="width: 17.8038%;">Text and Icon</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Text and Icon</p><p>Text </p><p>Icon</p><p>Shape Image and Text</p><p>Shape Image</p><p>Do Not Display</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Display stereotypes on symbols.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show DSL Stereotypes</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">All</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>All</p><p>Only Last</p><p>None</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Specify whether to show all, none, or only the last of the DSL Stereotypes (stereotypes that have DSL customization with the option hideMetatype = true) on a symbol.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Constraints</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Displays constraints on symbols.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Owner</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Do Not Display</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Do Not Display</p><p>Below Element Name</p><p>In Same Line With Name</p><p>Above Element Name</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Displays the name of the owner model element (beneath the corresponding model element name - old style notation, in the same line with the name, or above the element name).</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Wrap Words</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td You can use the Resize to Fit Content command (a small black square on the bottom line of the selected shape) to adjust the size of the shape to fit the content completely. By default, the appropriate property defined for the diagram is used.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Suspend Auto-Displaying of Label</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Same as Diagram</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Same as Diagram</p><p>true</p><p>false</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Set to true if you do not want to automatically show labels on the diagram pane while editing the model. 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Displays diagram frame on the diagram.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Abbreviated Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Shows full/abbreviated diagram keyword type on the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Diagram Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram kind is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Parameters</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram context element parameters (if there are any) are displayed in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Context Name</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram context element name is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Context Type</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The diagram context element type is displayed or hidden in the diagram frame header.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Show Context Kind</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">true</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">The context kind is a keyword predefined in UML (e.g., package, class, activity).</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Autosize</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">false</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;">true/false</td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Adjusts the size of a symbol to the contained information so that it uses minimum space.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Aspect Ratio</td><td style="width: 17.8038%;">Unspecified</td><td style="width: 10.3634%;"><p>Unspecified</p><p>1:1</p><p>4:3</p><p>3:4</p><p>16:9</p><p>9:16</p><p>A4 Landscape</p><p>A4 Portrait</p><p>US Letter Landscape</p><p>US Letter Portrait</p></td><td style="width: 55.98%;">Choose the diagram frame aspect ratio from the list or define a new one. the format of your defined aspect ratio should be 'W:H'.</td></tr><tr><td style="width: 15.8529%;">Use Rounded <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968760 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06236: Setting the spell checking options

- page_id: `243968760`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968760/Setting+the+spell+checking+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:10.384+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Dictionary Link

266217422

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Dictionary Link

266217424

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Dictionary Link

266217423

In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space=''] dialog, you can change general spelling options and the default language to be checked in the modeling tool, as well as manage dictionaries to be used in the modeling tool.

To change spell checker options

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment . The **Environment Options** dialog box opens.
2. In the options group list, select Spelling . Spelling options appear to specify in the options list. [ATTACHMENT filename='environment_optins_spelling.png']

Spell checking options are described in the following table:

| Property Name | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Check Spelling as you type | Underlines incorrectly spelled words and provides a list of suggested corrections in a shortcut menu. For more information about spell check while typing, see . |
| Check Tagged Values | Checks all tagged values of a string type. |
| Dictionary | Select language for spelling. All "Open Office" supportive spelling languages can be added to the existing ones. For more information about spelling dictionaries, see . |
| Case Sensitive | If true, words will differ in meaning based on the differing use of uppercase and lowercase letters. |
| Use Camel Case Words | If true, compound words or phrases in which the words are joined without spaces and are capitalized within the compound, e.g. BackColor will be spelled as separate words. |
| Ignore Upper Case Words | If true, words with uppercase words only are not to be spell checked. |
| Skip Numbers | If true, numbers are not spell checked. |

##### Spell checking dictionaries

All Apache OpenOffice supportive spelling languages are available.

To import spelling dictionaries

1. Click the Add button in the Environment Options dialog box > the Spelling branch > the Add Spelling Dictionaries group. The Dictionary dialog box opens.
2. Type the name of a new spelling dictionary in the Name text box.
3. Click the ... button and select the OpenOffice zip file location.
4. Type the description of the new spelling dictionary in the Description text box. [ATTACHMENT filename='dictionary_dialog.png']

#### INFO: Dictionary Link

Dictionary Link

http://wiki.services.openoffice.org/wiki/Dictionaries

266217421

**Related Pages:**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='']**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565298 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06237: Setting up Automatic Population of Measurement Sets

- page_id: `249565298`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565298/Setting+up+Automatic+Population+of+Measurement+Sets
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-04T15:53:50.848+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['set-up-automatic-population-of-measurement-sets', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can set up the automatic measurementSet population each time you create an Instance of the associated type. 
 
To set up the automatic Measurement Set population

1. Create a Measurement Set.
2. Add measurements for the created Measurement Set.
3. Apply the « UAF Properties» stereotype to the created Measurement Set.
4. Open the Measurement Set Specification window and make sure the Expert properties mode is selected.
5. In the Tags property group, select the appliesFor and click the Create Value button.
6. Select the element stereotype (e.g., Operational Performer in the example below) as the appliesFor tag value. [ATTACHMENT filename='exchange_element_as_the appliedFor_tag_value.png']
7. Save and reload the project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

After the automatic Measurements Set population is set up, you can apply Actual Measurements via the Specification window. To learn more, see the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Actual Measurements' space=''] page.

If you choose to add a measurable element as an**appliesFor** tag value, the Measurement Set property is applied automatically.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can set up the automatic measurementSet population each time you create an Instance of the associated type. <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" />To set up the automatic Measurement Set population</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a Measurement Set.</li><li>Add measurements for the created Measurement Set.<span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span></li><li>Apply the «<em>UAF</em><em>Properties»</em> stereotype to the created Measurement Set.</li><li>Open the Measurement Set Specification window and make sure the <strong>Expert </strong>properties mode is selected.</li><li>In the <strong>Tags</strong> property group, select the appliesFor and click the <strong>Create</strong> <strong>Value</strong> button.</li><li>Select the element stereotype (e.g., Operational Performer in the example below) as the <strong>appliesFor</strong> tag value.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="exchange_element_as_the appliedFor_tag_value.png" /></ac:image></li><li><span style="color:var(--ds-chart-gray-bold,#8590a2);"> </span>Save and reload the project.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="operational_performer_as_applyFor.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8fab1531-491e-4239-8f55-01cc7734c411"><ac:rich-text-body><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a3222beb-7b96-4f2b-8ef2-dc1cc2098762"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);">After the automatic Measurements Set population is set up, you can apply Actual Measurements via the Specification window. To learn more, see the </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Actual Measurements" /></ac:link> page.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target">If you choose to add a measurable element as an<strong> appliesFor</strong> tag value, the <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="840b7820-e817-4e96-84bf-e0d808cf798b">Measurement Set</ac:inline-comment-marker> property is applied automatically. </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurable_element.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966135 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06238: Sharing project data

- page_id: `243966135`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966135/Sharing+project+data
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:53.499+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Decomposing model
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The shared packages are available only for local projects. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing project usages' space='CM'] is an alternative for shared packages in the Teamwork Cloud environment.

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.

Not all contents of a used project are visible in the main project. The used project has a shared part and the part that is not shared. Only contents of the shared part are visible in the main project. The concept “shared” is similar to the public/private attributes of a class in programming languages (e.g., Java).

To designate packages of the project as shared

1. Select a package.
2. Do one of the following: The **Shared Packages** dialog opens. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - From the **File** menu, select **Share Packages**.
  - From the package shortcut menu, select **Project Usages** > **Share Packages**.
3. If not all desired packages are showing in the Selected packages list, select them in the Tree/List tabs and click the Add button.
4. Click OK .

The selected package including its owned elements is shared.

When a project is used in another project, all shared packages of the used project appear in that project. Moreover, each shared package can have a different location in the Containment tree. Any available package in a project can be used as a container for the shared package of the used project.

Additionally, each shared package can provide a “Preferred Path” that can be used as a hint where to mount a package when it is used in other project. No “Preferred Path” means that the package will be mounted directly under the root package Data. Profiles are typically mounted directly under the root package Data, however this can be changed.

Shared package “util” from the used project can be mounted on the “com::company” path in the main project in order to form the “com::company::util” path. The Preferred Path of the Shared Package can be used in this case to serve as a hint for the suggested location where to mount the package.

Used projects form a recursive data structure — the main project uses one or more other projects; these projects in turn can use other projects; those other projects can use another set of projects etc. All model pieces from these projects are gathered and connected into the integral model, which is shown in the Model Browser when the main project is opened.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d0b6f6a2-cb26-418c-a484-30deafa22eab"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The shared packages are available only for local projects. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CM" ri:content-title="Managing project usages" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Used Project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is an alternative for shared packages in the Teamwork Cloud environment.</p><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions only.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Not all contents of a used project are visible in the main project. The used project has a shared part and the part that is not shared. Only contents of the shared part are visible in the main project. The concept “shared” is similar to the public/private attributes of a class in programming languages (e.g., Java).</p><p><br /></p><p>To designate packages of the project as shared</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a package. </li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li><span>From the <strong>File</strong> menu, select <strong>Share Packages</strong>.</span></li><li><p><span>From the package shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Share Packages</strong>.</span></p></li></ul><p>The <strong>Shared Packages</strong> dialog opens.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="shared_packages_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></li><li>If not all desired packages are showing in the <strong>Selected packages</strong> list, select them in the <strong>Tree/List </strong>tabs and click the <strong>Add</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>The selected package including its owned elements is shared.</p><p>When a project is used in another project, all shared packages of the used project appear in that project. Moreover, each shared package can have a different location in the Containment tree. Any available package in a project can be used as a container for the shared package of the used project.</p><p>Additionally, each shared package can provide a “Preferred Path” that can be used as a hint where to mount a package when it is used in other project. No “Preferred Path” means that the package will be mounted directly under the root package Data. Profiles are typically mounted directly under the root package Data, however this can be changed.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9f9d3aa3-d253-436f-8334-7a9f02534a7c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Shared package “util” from the used project can be mounted on the “com::company” path in the main project in order to form the “com::company::util” path. The Preferred Path of the Shared Package can be used in this case to serve as a hint for the suggested location where to mount the package.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Used projects form a recursive data structure — the main project uses one or more other projects; these projects in turn can use other projects; those other projects can use another set of projects etc. All model pieces from these projects are gathered and connected into the integral model, which is shown in the Model Browser when the main project is opened.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965832 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06239: Sharing Usage Analytics information

- page_id: `243965832`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965832/Sharing+Usage+Analytics+information
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:47.131+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can help us improve the quality and performance of our products by sharing anonymous information and usage data. The collected information will not be disclosed to third parties. Additionally, no personal or sensitive data will be collected.

You can disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. To do so, go to *<modeling tool installation directory>\bin*and open for editing*<modeling tool name>.properties*file. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property:

*-Dno.phone.home=true*

This setting:

- Removes environment options related to internal exception submission and usage analytics
- Hides prompts asking to enable usage analytics and exception submission
- Ensures that the stats.nomagic.com submitter is disabled
- Removes the submit button from the internal exception dialog

If you agree to share usage analytics information, the following types of information will be collected:

**Software specification**

- Product used
- Product edition
- Product version
- License type
- Plugins installed

**Hardware specification**

- OS
- RAM
- CPU
- Drives
- Heap size
- Java version

The first time you launch your modeling tool and activate the license, you will be asked whether you want to start sharing usage analytics automatically. Click **Share Analytics** or **Don't Share** in the dialog. You can change your decision at any time in the **Environment** options dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To manage usage analytics sharing

1. In the main menu, go to Options > Environment .
2. Expand the General options group and select Usage Analytics .
3. Do one of the following:
  - Select Share Anonymous Information to start sharing anonymous usage data.
  - Clear the Share Anonymous Information option to stop sharing anonymous usage data.
4. When you are done, click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can help us improve the quality and performance of our products by sharing anonymous information and usage data. The collected information will not be disclosed to third parties. Additionally, n<span>o personal or sensitive data will be collected. </span></p><p><span> </span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a507ecf7-96ac-4213-9f3d-c8a3e8298104"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can disable all automatic and manual usage analytics, prevent internal error submissions, and remove related environment properties by modifying the system properties. To do so, go to <em style="text-align: left;">&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin </em>and open for editing<em style="text-align: left;"> &lt;modeling tool name&gt;.properties<span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"> </span></em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">file. In the JAVA_ARGS line, add the following property:</span></p><p><em><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">-Dno.phone.home=true</span></em></p><p>This setting:</p><ul><li>Removes environment options related to internal exception submission and usage analytics</li><li>Hides prompts asking to enable usage analytics and exception submission</li><li>Ensures that the <em>stats.nomagic.com</em> submitter is disabled</li><li>Removes the submit button from the internal exception dialog</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>If you agree to share usage analytics information, the following types of information will be collected:</span></p><p><strong>Software specification</strong></p><ul><li>Product used</li><li>Product edition</li><li>Product version</li><li>License type</li><li>Plugins installed</li></ul><p><strong>Hardware specification</strong></p><ul><li>OS</li><li>RAM</li><li>CPU</li><li>Drives</li><li>Heap size</li><li>Java version</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The first time you launch your modeling tool and activate the license, you will be asked whether you want to start sharing usage analytics automatically. Click <strong>Share Analytics</strong> or <strong>Don't Share</strong> in the dialog. You can change your decision at any time in the <strong>Environment</strong> options dialog. </p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="usage_analytics_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>To manage usage analytics sharing</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>Expand the<strong> General </strong>options group and select <strong>Usage Analytics</strong>.</li><li>Do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select <strong>Share Anonymous Information</strong> to start sharing anonymous usage data.</li><li>Clear the <strong>Share Anonymous Information</strong> option to stop sharing anonymous usage data.</li></ul></li><li>When you are done, click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="share_analytics_information_environment_option.png" /></ac:image></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565331 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06240: Show default documentation for UAF packages

- page_id: `249565331`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565331/Show+default+documentation+for+UAF+packages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:43.857+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To show the default documentation when creating a new project

1. In the New Project dialog, choose the required project template.
2. In the UAF Project Options area, set the Show default documentation for UAF Packages option to true. If you set the option to false, the new UAF Packages will be created without the default documentation. [ATTACHMENT filename='default_package_documentation_new_project.png']

The default documentation is initially displayed in the**Select Framework Views**dialog, which is shown when creatinga new project. You canchoose to show the defaultdocumentationin:

- The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property
- The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To show the default documentation for the existing projects

1. In the modeling tool main menu, click Option > Project . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF .
3. Find the Show default documentation for UAF Packages project option and set it to true (if it was set to false) .

If you set the option to true, a message appears where you can choose if the default Package documentation will be shown for the existing UAF Packages. In the message:

- Click Yes to show the default Documentation for existing UAF Packages if they have no custom documentation.
- Click No to keep the existing UAF Packages documentation unchanged.

- The “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option will not affect read-only elements and elements locked by other users.
- Turning the “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option off will NOT affect the existing UAF Packages' documentation to prevent data loss.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Y</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">ou can </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3c747802-afc5-4600-88cc-6e00906346a2"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">choose to show the default</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2"> </ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c8eecc38-fbc3-4933-a424-289cde9e37e2"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2">documentation</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="60c8c3c5-65f1-44c9-8faf-6bfb670831f2"> in:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li>The viewpoint package element's Specification window, Documentation/Comment property</li><li>The selected viewpoint package element's Documentation tab, which is located in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>.</li></ul><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="default_package_documentation.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="a68132b6-e7e8-4e66-8cf9-d907c1ae46c7">T</ac:inline-comment-marker>o show the default documentation for the existing projects</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Option </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project Options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>UAF</strong>.</li><li>Find the <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Show default documentation for UAF Packages </strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">project option and set it to true (if it was set to false)</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.</span></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>If you set the option to true, a message appears where you can choose if the default Package documentation will be shown for the existing UAF Packages. In the message:</p><ul><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to show the default Documentation for existing UAF Packages if they have no custom documentation.</li><li>Click <strong>No</strong> to keep the existing UAF Packages documentation unchanged.</li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1a5b78b2-fee4-468d-88c3-7f3de0e29589"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option will not affect read-only elements and elements locked by other users.</li><li>Turning the “Show default documentation for UAF Packages” option off will NOT affect the existing UAF Packages' documentation to prevent data loss.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249565336 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06241: Show diagram abbreviation in Containment tree

- page_id: `249565336`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249565336/Show+diagram+abbreviation+in+Containment+tree
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:07:44.363+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > Procedures
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can choose whether or not to display the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree. A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting project options' space=''] controls this functionality.

To show the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree

1. In the modeling tool main menu, click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, go to General > Browser .
3. Set the Show Diagram Abbreviations option to true.
4. Click OK .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

This Project option is by default true for the new projects:

- Blank UAF Project
- UAF Project
- UAF Project Template with Process Guide
- EA Project Template with Process Guide
- EA with BPMN Project

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can choose whether or not to display the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree. A <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting project options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Project option]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> controls this functionality.</p><p><br /></p><p>To show the diagram abbreviations in the Containment tree</p><hr /><ol><li>In the modeling tool main menu, click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, go to <strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Browser</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Show Diagram Abbreviations</strong> option to true.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_abbreviations_sample.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd26eaec-a50c-4dc7-b64d-093141bbe4d5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This Project option is by default true for the new projects: </p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Blank UAF Project</li><li>UAF Project</li><li>UAF Project Template with Process Guide</li><li>EA Project Template with Process Guide</li><li>EA with BPMN Project</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561737 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 06242: Showing allocations using SysML v1 Callout notation

- page_id: `249561737`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561737/Showing+allocations+using+SysML+v1+Callout+notation
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-08T17:13:42.565+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Functional analysis > Functional Allocations
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display allocations in a callout or in a compartment of an element symbol.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Examples of displaying allocations.

To display element properties, constraints, and/or tagged values on a note symbol

To see compartment editing results, make sure element properties, constraints, and/or tagged values are displayed on the note symbol.

1. Draw a note to an element whose documentation you want to display.
2. Select a note and click the Compartments button on the left.
3. In the list, click to select the following:

- 
  - **Element Properties**
  - **Constraints**
  - **Tagged Values**

Element properties, constraints, and / or tagged values are displayed on the note symbol in the separate compartments.

You can choose which element properties, constraints, or tagged values to display on the note symbol by editing an appropriate compartment.

To edit a compartment on a note symbol

1. Open the Compartment Edit dialog by doing one of the following:
  - From the note shortcut menu, select Edit Compartments .
  - Select a note and click Edit Element Properties on the right. [IMAGE alt='' src='']Compartment Edit dialog
2. Move elements between Hidden and Selected lists using the > , < , >> , or << buttons . Only items from the selected list will be displayed in the compartment on the note’s symbol.
3. Click OK after you are done.

#### NOTE: Allocations

Allocations

To display allocations, in the **Element Properties**tab, select **Allocated To**and/or **Allocated From** to display.

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Callout Style' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Compartments' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML v1 specific compartments' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966593 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06243: Showing single node per element

- page_id: `243966593`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966593/Showing+single+node+per+element
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:36.851+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-single-node', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To represent element node in relation map

- Turn the Show Single Node Per Element option off in the [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] , if you need to see separate nodes of the same element representing every occurrence of the element in different branches of the graph. [ATTACHMENT filename='relation_map_show_single_node1.png']
- Turn the **Show Single Node Per Element** option on in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''], if you need to see only one node of the element representing all occurrence of the element in the whole graph. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p> To represent element node in relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>Turn the <strong>Show Single Node Per Element</strong> option off in the <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, if you need to see separate nodes of the same element representing every occurrence of the element in different branches of the graph.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Separate nodes of element" ac:alt="Separate nodes of element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_show_single_node1.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p>Turn the <strong>Show Single Node Per Element</strong> option on in the <ac:image ac:title="Options" ac:alt="Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="options_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, if you need to see only one node of the element representing all occurrence of the element in the whole graph.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Single node of element" ac:alt="Single node of element"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_show_single_node2.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566652 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06244: Signal Boundary Event

- page_id: `249566652`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566652/Signal+Boundary+Event
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T14:01:24.873+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram > Boundary Events
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Signal Boundary Event can receive a Signal. In this context, it will change a normal flow into an exception flow upon being triggered.

A Signal Event differs from an Error Event because it defines a more general, non-error condition for interrupting Activities, such as the successful completion of another Activity, and it has a larger scope than the Error Event does.

Signal Boundary Event displays either of the following on a diagram:

- A Signal Boundary Event name if the name is specified.
- A Signal Ref property value if the name is unspecified.

**Notation**

- Signal Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - True) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_signal.png']
- Signal Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - False) [ATTACHMENT filename='boundary_event_signal_false.png']

**Related elements**

- Boundary Events
- Activities

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Signal Boundary Event can receive a Signal. In this context, it will change a normal flow into an exception flow upon being triggered.</p><p>A Signal Event differs from an Error Event because it defines a more general, non-error condition for interrupting Activities, such as the successful completion of another Activity, and it has a larger scope than the Error Event does.</p><p>Signal Boundary Event displays either of the following on a diagram:</p><ul><li data-uuid="74362b02-a9ce-4fd0-9c6b-8cdd63874da0">A Signal Boundary Event name if the name is specified.</li><li data-uuid="3bc5c33f-0469-41c7-97f6-a0430cfeb6b2">A Signal Ref property value if the name is unspecified.</li></ul><p><strong>Notation</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="ebf37686-cf93-4842-b5cc-d8967d25143b">Signal Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - True)<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_event_signal.png" /></ac:image></li><li data-uuid="e5d949ed-136c-40ea-96a4-9e0adc7ee273">Signal Boundary Event (Cancel Activity - False)<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="boundary_event_signal_false.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link parent-link" data-uuid="ff486733-d3bf-424e-a1e5-b6860399ef0f"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Boundary+Events">Boundary Events</a></li><li data-uuid="2fddd0fd-5eaa-4f4c-82bf-42344d59af22"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Activities">Activities</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related diagrams</strong></p><ul><li class="ancestor-link" data-uuid="0b248216-f866-49a3-9bcb-13bad6a6dc41"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Process+Diagram">BPMN Process Diagram</a></li><li data-uuid="a6129cdb-704d-4b1e-a836-8db23fffc769"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Collaboration+Diagram">BPMN Collaboration Diagram</a></li><li data-uuid="d1c88d7a-3c61-4d37-afbf-d80629956bd1"><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/BPMN+Choreography+Diagram">BPMN Choreography Diagram</a></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedure</strong></p><ul><li data-uuid="cc5a4c12-510e-422f-b8d1-71975fc107d8"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating and Using a BPMN Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Creating and Using an Event]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566595 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06245: Signal Catching Intermediate Event

- page_id: `249566595`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566595/Signal+Catching+Intermediate+Event
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-10T14:52:19.193+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram > Intermediate Catch Event
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A Signal Catching Intermediate Event is used to receive a signal.

This event displays either of the following on the diagram:

- A Signal Catching Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.
- A Signal Ref property value if the name is unspecified.

Signals in business process modeling are used for general communications within and across process levels.

**Notation**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

**Related element**

- Intermediate Catch Event

**Related diagrams**

- BPMN Process Diagram
- BPMN Collaboration Diagram
- BPMN Choreography Diagram

**Related procedure**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and Using a BPMN Event' space='']

of the following on the diagram:</p><ul><li data-uuid="8063507a-a20e-480d-8d59-705c740608dc">A Signal Catching Intermediate Event name if the name is specified.</li><li data-uuid="a7865a4a-9f3e-4e69-bd71-7013b04cf840">A Signal Ref property value if the name is unspecified.</li></ul><p>Signals in business process modeling are used for general communications within and
