Its Specification window opens.</li><li>In the property group list on the left side of the window, select the <strong>Inner Elements</strong> node.</li><li>Make sure the sorting view is selected in the Specification window toolbar, as displayed in the figure above.</li><li>On the right side of the window, select any element in the group of elements that you want to sort.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click the <strong>Sort A to Z</strong> button.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7fa18bdc-2e30-46b2-99dc-adc4bfbb6687"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the <strong>Sort A to Z</strong> button is disabled (grayed out), the elements of the selected element group are already sorted alphabetically.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>Close the Specification window.</li></ol><p><br />After completing the steps above, all the elements in the selected element group are sorted alphabetically. </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562994 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06264: SOV-3 Working with Services to Service Contracts Mapping matrix

- page_id: `249562994`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562994/SOV-3+Working+with+Services+to+Service+Contracts+Mapping+matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:09:28.495+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-3 Capability to Service Mapping
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The **SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping** matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.

The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,and the Columns are Service Contracts.

To create a SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix

1. Specify**Row Scope** (Services or their specializations);
2. Specify**Column Scope** (Service Contracts);
3. Click[IMAGE alt='' src=''].

Services or their specializations map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.

To mapServices or their specializations to Service Contracts

1. Double-click an empty intersection.

To remove the relation

1. Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The <strong>SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping</strong> matrix depicts mapping between the Services or their specializations and the Service Contracts they govern.</p><p><span>The Rows of this matrix are Services or their specializations,</span><span> and the Columns are Service Contracts</span><span>.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><span>To create a SOV-3 Services to Service Contracts Mapping Matrix</span></p><hr /><ol><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify</span><strong> Row Scope</strong> (Services or their specializations);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Specify </span><strong>Column Scope</strong> (Service Contracts);</span></li><li><span><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Click </span><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper" style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image ac:alt="Refresh"><ri:attachment ri:filename="refresh.png" /></ac:image></span></span>.</span></li></ol><p> Services <span>or their specializations</span> map to Service Contracts using a Governed By relationship.</p><p><br /></p><p>To map<span> Services or their specializations to Service Contracts </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click an empty intersection.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To remove the relation</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click a full intersection between the desired elements.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563002 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06265: SOV-4a Service Parametric

- page_id: `249563002`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563002/SOV-4a+Service+Parametric
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:05:59.682+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint > MODAF. SOV-4a Service Constraints
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

374424572

374424575

374424574

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='MED'] to constrain the properties of another Service Specification. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='MED'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

374424571

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c6555c36-af0f-4d0b-a807-c13628b704f5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">374424572</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cf422c56-515c-47a5-a55e-f5e5f8afff4e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">374424575</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22e77423-3ee1-4377-85db-214360f97560"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">374424574</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint block]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to constrain the properties of another Service Specification. It contains <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Constraint Property" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[constraint properties]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contain many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5352cfdc-efd8-4a4e-9386-399ed9cb8bc0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">374424571</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965869 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06266: Special characters

- page_id: `243965869`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965869/Special+characters
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:21:50.981+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Our modeling tool can use either XML version 1.0 or XML version 1.1. Because XML version 1.0 does not support some special characters, our modeling tool contains a mechanism that replaces certain characters in element names with the underscore symbol ( _ ). However, only a small part of them are replaced, including profile, stereotype and stereotype property names. Please try to avoid special characters and use ASCII letters or numbers. In comparison,XML 1.1 supports all characters,except code point 3000 from “Japanese-style punctuation,"which means the tool will replace it withan underscore symbol ( _ ).

The default XML version in the modeling tool is 1.0, to use XML 1.1, you need to manually change it.

To change the XML version

1. In the main menu of a modeling tool, select**Options**>**Environment**. The**Environment Options**dialog opens.
2. In the Environment Options dialog, select General > Save/Load from the options group list.
3. Click the XML Version drop-down list in the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, and select the desired XML version.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Our modeling tool can use either XML version 1.0 or XML version 1.1. <span>Because XML version 1.0 does not support some special characters, our modeling tool contains a mechanism that replaces certain characters in element names with the underscore symbol ( _ ). However, only a small part of them are replaced, including profile, stereotype and stereotype property names. Please try to avoid special characters and use ASCII letters or numbers. In comparison,</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> XML 1.1 supports all characters, </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">except code point 3000 from “Japanese-style punctuation,&quot;</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> which means the tool will replace it with </span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">an underscore symbol ( _ )</span><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span></p><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The default XML version in the modeling tool is 1.0, to use XML 1.1, you need to manually change it. </span></p><p><span><br /></span></p><p>To change the XML version</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the main menu of a modeling tool, select </span><strong>Options</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> &gt; </span><strong>Environment</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">. The </span><strong>Environment Options</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog opens.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In the </span><a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Environment+Options">Environment Options</a><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> dialog, select </span><strong>General </strong>&gt; <strong>Save/Load</strong><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">from the options group list.</span><br /></span></li><li>Click the <strong>XML Version</strong> drop-down list in the option specification area on the right side of the dialog, and select the desired XML version.<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><br /></span></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="left"><ri:attachment ri:filename="XML_version.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965907 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06267: Special Transformation

- page_id: `243965907`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965907/Special+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:48.981+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

An EA-exported XMI contains both non-standard UML elements and elements that can break the XMI schema. To retain standard UML elements and keep the XMI schema intact, the Enterprise Architect Import applies specific transformation rules. The following sections describe how each model element is transformed, enabling you to import a complete XMI model conforming to UML standards.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>An EA-exported XMI contains both non-standard UML elements and elements that can break the XMI schema. To retain standard UML elements and keep the XMI schema intact, the Enterprise Architect Import applies specific transformation rules. The following sections describe how each model element is transformed, enabling you to import a complete XMI model conforming to UML standards.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f34cc2ac-1b86-4e92-a521-57071f59087c" /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968661 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06268: Specific use cases

- page_id: `243968661`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968661/Specific+use+cases
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:06.199+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1905557019

1905557021

1905557017

**On this page**

4

1905557020

##### How to build a metric definition for date and time tracking?

If your metric suite isn't created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, you must build the metric definition for date and time tracking on your own.

To build the metric definition for date and time tracking

1. Add a new metric definition with the following characteristics to your metric suite: [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_metric_definition_for_date_and_time_tracking.png']
  - date and time as name
  - String as the result type
  - StructuredExpression as the language of the metric definition body
2. In the Body area of the Default Value dialog, click Create operation , and then select Operation from Model on the right side of the dialog.
3. In the open dialog, select the **FormatDate** built-in operation which is stored in the *UML Standard Profile* and click **OK**. Make sure you clear the**Apply Filter**check box in the **Select Opaque Behavior** dialog.
4. In the **Body** area of the **Default Value** dialog, select the **Date** parameter of the **FormatDate** operation and click The **Reset** button.
5. Select Operation from Model on the right side of the dialog.
6. In the open dialog, select the Timestamp built-in operation which is stored in the UML Standard Profile and click OK . [ATTACHMENT filename='specifying_date_parameter_value.png']
7. In the Body area of the Default Value dialog, select the Format parameter of the FormatDate operation and in the Value box type "dd.MM.yyyy hh:mm aaa".
8. Click OK , and then close the metric definition Specification window.

Now you can track the date and time of each instance of the metric suite.

##### How to access parameter values in a metric definition?

If you need to use the values of a parameter for specifying calculations in a metric definition, you can easily access them with the help of the functionality provided by the StructuredExpression language. Let’s overview the case when you need to use the value of the *scope* parameter inherited from the *BaseMetricSuite* to calculate your metrics.

To access the value of the *scope* parameter inherited from the *BaseMetricSuite*

1. Add a new metric definition to your metric suite.
2. Select the StructuredExpression language for specifying the calculations.
3. In the structured expression, create a script operation with single parameter named scopes .
4. Select the InstanceNavigation built-in operation as the scopes parameter value.
5. Specify the following parameter values of that operation: [ATTACHMENT filename='image2016-5-30 20:51:42.png']
  - Contextual Variable as the Instance parameter value.
  - The scope parameter of the BaseMetricSuite as the Navigate Property parameter value.
6. In the script operation, refer to the *scopes* parameter simply by its name as the following Groovy script sample illustrates: import static com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelmetrics.ScriptHelper.*; 
import static com.nomagic.requirements.util.SysMLProfile.*; getElementsRecursively(scopes) 
findAll {el -> isRequirement(el)} 
size()

An alternative way to access parameters is calling the *getValue()* method from MagicDraw Open API on the *valueContext* globally defined value, as the following Groovy script sample illustrates:

```text
def scopes = valueContext.getValue(“scope”)
```

##### How to build a metric definition that takes the result of another metric definition?

Let’s say, you need to calculate the percentage of requirements that are covered by blocks. To do this, you must have the following values:

- Overall requirements count
- Requirements covered by blocks count

Let’s say, these values are the results of two already existing metric definitions: *requirementsCount* and *requirementsCoveredByBlocksCount*. You need another metric definition, which takes the results of the latter metric definitions and calculates the percentage. To access these values and make sure they are already calculated, we must call the *getOneValue()* method from MagicDraw Open API on the *valueContext* globally defined value for both metric definitions, as the following Groovy script sample illustrates:

```text

```

```text
int all = valueContext.getOneValue(“requirementsCount”);int covered = valueContext.getOneValue(“requirementsCoveredByBlocksCount”);com.nomagic.magicdraw.modelmetrics.ScriptHelper.calcPercentage(all, covered)
```

1905832998

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968176 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06269: Specification window

- page_id: `243968176`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968176/Specification+window
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:52.008+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with model elements
- labels: ['specification-window', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

496564810

496564812

496564811

You can define all model elements in the Specification window.

Shortcut menus, toolbars, and browser help ease the task of editing model elements.

You can also edit model elements and symbol properties directly in the[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Properties tab' space='']**Properties**tab, which is located at the bottom of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Specification window is used to define UML model elements such as class, package, activity. The Specification window is a non-modal window in which you can [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Editing property values' space=''] and work with a model simultaneously.

To open the Specification window, do one of the following

- From the selected symbol shortcut menu, select **Specification**.
- Double-click a symbol on the Diagram pane or in the Model Browser.
- Select a symbol on the Diagram pane and press Enter.
- The element Specification window opens when you add a model element to an owning model element in its Specification window. The second Specification window opens on top of the first. Use the **Back to**or **Forward to**arrow buttons for switching between windows.

496564809

**Related pages**

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1655005484

1655005486

1655005485

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Icon | Name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Open Specification | Opens specification of the selected referenced element in the same window. |
|  | Select in Containment Tree | Selects the chosen element in the Containment tree of the Model Browser. |
|  | Specification Opening Mode | If selected, the referenced Specification windows are opened in the same window. To navigate between specifications, use Back and Forward buttons. You can cancel the selection, if you need the Specification window to be open for each element in a separate window, for example, to compare specifications of several elements at the same time. |
|  | Refresh | Refresh data of the Specification window. Refresh the Specification window of an element to see if any changes that are not directly related to that element were made. Also you may need to refresh data if the Specification window is open for a long time. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968643 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06271: Specify the target for the Metric Suite

- page_id: `243968643`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968643/Specify+the+target+for+the+Metric+Suite
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:35.146+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Important

1891057916

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Important

1891057919

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Important

1891057917

A Metric Suite must have a target. It specifies model elements, for which the metrics of a particular Metric Suite can be calculated. The target value can be any type of elements or even a particular element of your model. For example, if the target of a Metric Suite is the Package type, you can calculate metrics for any package in your model by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric table' space=''].

Although the target of a Metric Suite can be any type of element or even a particular element of your model, we recommend selecting the required Package or the Package type.

A target needs to be specified as the property of a Metric Suite. The element, or at least the type of the element, must be a target value of that Metric Suite.

To specify the target of a Metric Suite

1. Double-click the shape of the newly created Metric Suite to open its Specification window.
2. Click the cell of the Target property value and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] . The Select Elements dialog opens.
3. In the tree or list on the left side of the dialog, select the element that you want to use as a target. If you want to select the type of elements (e.g., Package), which is stored in a standard/system profile used in your project, make sure you clear the **Apply Filter** check box. Besides, you can select more than one element. For more information about the manipulations in the element selection dialog, see[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Elements multiple selection' space=''].
4. Add the element to the **Selected elements** list by clicking [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''] (if you want to add elements recursively).
5. Click **OK.**
6. In the Specification window, click**Close**. ImportantIf you cannot see a particular Metric Suite in the **Create Metric Table** dialog, check . It is possible that the element selected in step 1 is not the target of this Metric Suite.

The target is specified.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A Metric Suite with the Package type target.

If you need to calculate metrics for the whole model, select the *Model* Package as the target of your Metric Suite.

1891057915

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric Suites' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying parameter definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specific use cases' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="22eea2ae-00bb-45e2-b048-960b914be221"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1891057916</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1def296f-095b-4d1d-b80f-9312d9082496"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1891057919</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b9ee04ee-9d59-4e03-8070-f6db5676eedf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1891057917</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>A Metric Suite must have a target. It specifies model elements, for which the metrics of a particular Metric Suite can be calculated. The target value can be any type of elements or even a particular element of your model. For example, if the target of a Metric Suite is the Package type, you can calculate metrics for any package in your model by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metric table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating a Metric Table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2167cf33-f6f2-49ea-9a68-51e67108f0eb"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Although the target of a Metric Suite can be any type of element or even a particular element of your model, we recommend selecting the required Package or the Package type.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>A target needs to be specified as the property of a Metric Suite. The element, or at least the type of the element, must be a target value of that Metric Suite.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the target of a Metric Suite</p><hr /><ol><li>Double-click the shape of the newly created Metric Suite to open its Specification window.</li><li>Click the cell of the <strong>Target</strong> property value and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>. The <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog opens.</li><li><p>In the tree or list on the left side of the dialog, select the element that you want to use as a target.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6e302fd-83ad-40a1-96f1-4c3b26899263"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you want to select the type of elements (e.g., Package), which is stored in a standard/system profile used in your project, make sure you clear the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box. Besides, y<span>ou can select more than one element. For more information about the manipulations in the element selection dialog, see </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Elements multiple selection" /></ac:link><span>.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li><p>Add the element to the <strong>Selected elements</strong> list by clicking <ac:image ac:title="Add" ac:alt="Add"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> or <ac:image ac:title="Add Recursively" ac:alt="Add Recursively"><ri:attachment ri:filename="add_recursively_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> (if you want to add elements recursively).</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK.</strong></p></li><li><p><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">In the Specification window, click </span><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Close</strong><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">.</span></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3e16c58b-8133-42a8-8a2c-43d064a5a24e"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Important</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you cannot see a particular Metric Suite in the <strong>Create Metric Table</strong> dialog, check <ac:link><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[the target value of that Metric Suite]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. It is possible that the element selected in step 1 is not the target of this Metric Suite.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol><p>The target is specified.<br /> </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="metric_suite_with_specified_target.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">A Metric Suite with the Package type target.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60601265-1426-4f77-b53e-ec95e8f2c0f7"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to calculate metrics for the whole model, select the <em>Model</em> Package as the target of your Metric Suite.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e63fbad2-f2a3-4250-8e0c-449df987b9fd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1891057915</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Metric Suites" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Adding a new Metric Suite" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying parameter definitions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building metric definitions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building validation-based metric definitions" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specific use cases" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966598 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06273: Specifying colors and legend

- page_id: `243966598`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966598/Specifying+colors+and+legend
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:37.652+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-colors-legend', 'relation-map-colors', 'relation-map-legend', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

As of version 18.3, you can specify the colors and line styles to be used for representing the relationships in your relation maps. Your selection is displayed in the legend in the top right corner of the diagram pane.

To specify colors and line styles

1. Open the Relation Criteria dialog by
  - double-clicking the legend header ( Legend ).
  - clicking the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button next to Relation Criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='']**Criteria** area .
2. Open the Style dialog by clicking the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button in the Style column of your selected relation criterion. [ATTACHMENT filename='relation_criteria_dialog.png']
3. Change the line color and style to what you like and click OK when you're done. [ATTACHMENT filename='style_dialog.png']
4. When you've changed line colors and styles for all the relation criteria you needed to, click OK to close the Relation Criteria dialog.

The customized legend helps you identify relation criteria. When working with relation maps, the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button is clicked to select by default. If you don't want to see the legend all the time, simply click the button to cancel the selection.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>As of version 18.3, you can specify the colors and line styles to be used for representing the relationships in your relation maps. Your selection is displayed in the legend in the top right corner of the diagram pane.</p><p> </p><p>To specify colors and line styles</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog by<br /> <br /><ul><li>double-clicking the legend header (<strong>Legend</strong>).</li><li>clicking the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button next to <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:link-body> <strong>Criteria</strong> area</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul></li><li>Open the <strong>Style</strong> dialog by clicking the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button in the <strong>Style</strong> column of your selected relation criterion.<br /> <br /> <ac:image ac:title="Relation Criteria Dialog" ac:alt="Relation Criteria Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_criteria_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Change the line color and style to what you like and click <strong>OK</strong> when you're done. <br /> <br /> <ac:image ac:title="Style Dialog" ac:alt="Style Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="style_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li>When you've changed line colors and styles for all the relation criteria you needed to, click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Relation Criteria</strong> dialog.</li></ol><p> </p><p>The customized legend helps you identify relation criteria. When working with relation maps, the <ac:image ac:title="Show Legend" ac:alt="Show Legend"><ri:attachment ri:filename="show_legend.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> button is clicked to select by default. If you don't want to see the legend all the time, simply click the button to cancel the selection.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Relation Map Legend and Color Scheme" ac:alt="Relation Map Legend and Color Scheme"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_legend.png" /></ac:image></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969588 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06274: Specifying Constrained Element property

- page_id: `243969588`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969588/Specifying+Constrained+Element+property
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-09-12T13:33:08.982+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

You must ensure that the constrained element is specified and points to the necessary classifier. Each validation rule modeled as a Constraint has a**Constrained Element** property. This property determines the type of element this rule applies to. If you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new Constraint element' space='']create the Constraint for a classifier or stereotype, this property is filled automatically. Otherwise, you need to specify it manually, shown in the following procedure:

To specify a constrained element manually

1. Open the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']>]]>
2. Find the Constrained Element property and select it's value box.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select an appropriate element from the **Select Elements** dialog. For constraints on metaclasses, select an appropriate element from the **UML Standard Profile** > **UML2 Metamodel**. Only the single **Constrained Element** property value is supported for validation rules.
4. Click **OK**. 
The **Constrained Element** property value is specified.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d6f77b84-96bc-4ac2-9880-5164476e1c85"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You must ensure that the constrained element is specified and points to the necessary classifier. Each validation rule modeled as a Constraint has a<span> <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property</span>. This property determines the type of element this rule applies to. If you <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new Constraint element" /><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">create the Constraint for a classifier or stereotype</span></ac:link-body></ac:link>, this property is filled automatically. Otherwise, you need to specify it manually, shown in the following procedure:</p><p>To specify a constrained element manually</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Constraint Specification window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Specification window >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Find the <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property and select it's value box.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png" /></ac:image> and select an appropriate element from the <strong>Select Elements</strong> dialog.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9e15d554-92b2-46f5-be9b-61477a090f51"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For constraints on metaclasses, select an appropriate element from the <strong>UML Standard Profile</strong> &gt; <strong>UML2 Metamodel</strong>. Only the single <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property value is supported for validation rules.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The <strong>Constrained Element</strong> property value is specified.</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968965 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06275: Specifying criteria for querying model

- page_id: `243968965`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968965/Specifying+criteria+for+querying+model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:15.022+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

265829020

INLINE

265829022

INLINE

265829021

INLINE

Specifying criteria is necessary to

- Associate row and column elements in a dependency matrix.
- Display relations between elements in a relation map.
- Gather the contents of a smart package.
- Calculate/gather the value of a derived property.

A structured expression can also be the body of the executable opaque behavior, metric definition or validation rule, when the StructuredExpression language is selected for specifying that body.

Use one of the following operation types to specify the criterion:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Simple Navigation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Metachain Navigation' space=''] (indirect relations through chains of properties)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Find operation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating new operations' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Property Test operation' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Calling operations from the model' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating script operations' space='']

You can also use custom operation types. In addition, a criterion can be an expression combining several operations by passing the results of one operation call to another operation.

If multiple criteria (operations) are specified, the result will be calculated according to all these criteria (operations).

You can copy and paste the selected structured expressions in the**Expression**dialog. Select all or part of the structured expression in one dialog and paste it into the same or another dialog.

You can also copy the structured expression:

- from one smart package and paste it into another;
- from the matrices and paste them into the relation maps and vice versa;
- and use it in the smart package.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Copying from the Expression dialog

##### Concepts

Read the following description to better understand the material.

| Concept | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Contextual ElementContextual element | The element that begins the calculation of the result according to the specified criteria. In a dependency matrix, the context is a row or column element. In a relation map, this is every node of the structure. In a table, the context is the table scope value. A smart package is the context itself. The context of a derived property is the target of the customization element that owns this derived property. |

265841031

INLINE

**Related pages**

true

Test"><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating new operations" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Type Test]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using Property Test operation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Property Test]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Calling operations from the model" In addition, a <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="1a7fcd48-b82b-41b2-bd8b-cd859a4ee803">criterion</ac:inline-comment-marker> can be an expression combining several operations by passing the results of one operation call to another operation.</p><p>If multiple criteria (operations) are specified, the result will be calculated according to all these criteria (operations).</p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ced3df14-a57a-4e20-8b7e-5287b48726cb">You can copy and paste the selected structured expressions in the </ac:inline-comment-marker><strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ced3df14-a57a-4e20-8b7e-5287b48726cb">Expression </ac:inline-comment-marker></strong><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ced3df14-a57a-4e20-8b7e-5287b48726cb">dialog. Select all or part of the structured expression in one dialog and paste it into the same or another dialog.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ced3df14-a57a-4e20-8b7e-5287b48726cb">You can also copy the structured expression:</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ced3df14-a57a-4e20-8b7e-5287b48726cb">from one smart package and paste it into another;</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ced3df14-a57a-4e20-8b7e-5287b48726cb">from the matrices and paste them into the relation maps and vice versa;</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="ced3df14-a57a-4e20-8b7e-5287b48726cb">and use it in the smart package.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="expression.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Copying from the Expression dialog</h6><h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6d68575d-9fa1-4742-ae1a-20d7d928f3e3">Concepts</ac:inline-comment-marker></h3><p>Read the following description to better understand the material.</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 30.938%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 16.6963%;" /><col style="width: 83.3037%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Concept</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ef6ba79e-aa19-4515-8fc8-52e74a020b4d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Contextual Element</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Contextual </strong><br /><strong>element</strong></p></div></td><td><span>The element that begins the calculation of the result </span><span>according to the specified criteria. In a dependency matrix, </span><span>the context is a row or column element. In a relation map, this is every node of the structure. In a table, the context is the table scope value. A smart package is the context itself. The context of a derived property is the target of the customization element that owns this derived property.</span></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="616b5b14-205f-4505-9a02-2aebf58c9803"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">265841031</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4b29f81b-de42-4590-a517-2cb769b9eaab"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966580 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06276: Specifying criteria, layout, and depth

- page_id: `243966580`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966580/Specifying+criteria+layout+and+depth
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:35.662+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Relation Map
- labels: ['relation-map-criteria-layout-depth', 'relation-map-criteria', 'relation-map-layout', 'relation-map-depth', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

After creating a relation map, you need to specify the criteria, layout, and the depth for it.

To change the relation map properties

- Right-click the diagram pane and from shortcut menu, select Specification , or click [ATTACHMENT filename='options_button.png'] on the Options toolbar and then select Specification . Change the property values in the Specification window of the relation map.
- Specify the most popular property values by using the relation map environment capabilities, such as toolbars and the Criteria area.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can choose whether you want to show or hide Context, Relation Criteria, Element Type, and/or Scope. In the Relation Map **Specification** window, set the needed check-box to true or false.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To select the specified value in the Model Browser or to open its Specification window, you can right-click on the value boxes (except for the Relation Criteria) in the **Criteria** area.

| Element name | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Context | Text box with | Click to select the main element from which the structure is started, that is, the context of the structure. You can . |
| Relation Criteria | Text box with | Click to select relation criteria that will be displayed on the relation map. |
| Element Type | Text box with | Click to that will be displayed on the relation map.Standard and custom subtypes of the selected element types can be optionally included when displaying them on the relation map.Select the Include Subtypes check box to includes the subtypes of the selected elements, e.g., models and profiles of Packages in the relation map.Select the Include Custom Types check box to show the custom types extending the selected element types, e.g., a Smart Package extending a Package on the relation map. |
| Scope | Text box with | Click to select packages from which the relation map structure will be shown. |
|  | Command on the Options button menu | Select the relation map layout:Tree.Radial. |
| Depth | Spin box | Type or select the level of the relation map branches that will be automatically expanded. |

## PAGE 06277: Specifying diagram properties

- space_key: `MT`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

568086116

568086118

568086117

You can specify your diagram's properties by accessing the diagram's [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''].

To open the diagram's specification window

- Right-click the diagram's window and click on Specification in the shortcut menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram Specification window.

568086115

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967128 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06278: Specifying diagram style properties

- page_id: `243967128`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967128/Specifying+diagram+style+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:21.120+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

In the **Diagram Properties** dialog, you can customize the diagram's style: color, grid, diagram info, font, etc. This allows for your diagram to stand out from other diagrams in your project, or simply just for aesthetic purposes.

To open the **Diagram Properties** dialog

There are multiple ways to bring up the dialog, so do one of the following:

- Select Diagram Properties from the diagram shortcut menu.
- Press Shift+Enter.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Diagram properties dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>In the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog, you can customize the diagram's style: color, grid, diagram info, font, etc. This allows for your diagram to stand out from other diagrams in your project, or simply just for aesthetic purposes.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> dialog</p><hr /><p>There are multiple ways to bring up the dialog, so do one of the following:</p><ul><li>Select <strong>Diagram Properties</strong> from the diagram shortcut menu.</li><li>Press Shift+Enter.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="diagram_properties.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Diagram properties dialog.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561289 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06279: Specifying feature directions

- page_id: `249561289`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561289/Specifying+feature+directions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:00:22.207+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Defining interfaces using ports > Provided and Required Interfaces
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before specifying the feature directions, you need to understand the following concepts:

- A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Directed Feature' space='MED'] is an enumeration type that defines literals used by directed features for specifying whether they are supported by the owning [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] , or is to be supported by other Blocks for the owning Block to use.
- A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Directed Feature' space='MED'] indicates whether the feature is supported by the owning [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] (provided), or is to be supported by other Blocks for the owning Block to use (required), or both (the owning block for features on types of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Proxy Port' space='MED'] is the type of the Block usage the Proxy Port is standing in for, which might be an internal part). Using non-flow properties means to read or write them, and using behavioral features means to invoke them. Provided non-flow properties are read and written on the owning Block, while required non-flow properties are read or written on an external Block. Provided behavioral features are invoked with the owning Block as target, while required behavioral features are invoked with an external Block as target (required).

The directed feature is a feature element that applies the «DirectedFeature» [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Stereotype' space='']. You can specify the feature direction of the selected element.

To specify a feature direction

1. Right-click a feature’s symbols owned by a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Block' space='MED'] such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Part Property' space='MED'] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space=''] , [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space=''] , and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Signal Event' space=''] reception.
2. From the shortcut menu, point to Feature Direction and select one of the following: The «DirectedFeature» stereotype is applied automatically.
  - Provided . Indicates that the feature shall be supported by the owning Block.
  - Required . Indicates that the feature shall be supported by other Blocks.
  - Provided and Required . Indicates that the feature shall be both provided and required.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before specifying the feature directions, you need to understand the following concepts:</p><ul><li>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Directed Feature" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Feature Direction]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is an enumeration type that defines literals used by directed features for specifying whether they are supported by the owning <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link>, or is to be supported by other Blocks for the owning Block to use.</li><li>A <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Directed Feature" /></ac:link> indicates whether the feature is supported by the owning <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Block ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>(provided), or is to be supported by other Blocks for the owning Block to use (required), or both (the owning block for features on types of <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Proxy Port" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Proxy Ports]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> is the type of the Block usage the Proxy Port is standing in for, which might be an internal part). Using non-flow properties means to read or write them, and using behavioral features means to invoke them. Provided non-flow properties are read and written on the owning Block, while required non-flow properties are read or written on an external Block. Provided behavioral features are invoked with the owning Block as target, while required behavioral features are invoked with an external Block as target (required).</li></ul><p>The directed feature is a feature element that applies the «DirectedFeature» <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Stereotype" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[stereotype]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. You can specify the feature direction of the selected element.</p><p>To specify a feature direction</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click a feature’s symbols owned by a <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Block" /></ac:link> such as <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Part Property" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Attribute" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operation" /></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Signal Event" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Signal ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>reception.</li><li>From the shortcut menu, point to <strong>Feature Direction</strong> and select one of the following:<br /><ul><li><strong>Provided</strong>. Indicates that the feature shall be supported by the owning Block.</li><li><strong>Required</strong>. Indicates that the feature shall be supported by other Blocks.</li><li><strong>Provided and Required</strong>. Indicates that the feature shall be both provided and required.</li></ul>The «DirectedFeature» stereotype is applied automatically.<br /><br /></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967009 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06280: Specifying instances as slot values

- page_id: `243967009`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967009/Specifying+instances+as+slot+values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:17.070+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the slot type is a classifier, you can specify an instance of this classifier as the slot value. Do this in one of the following ways:

- By creating a new instance.
- By selecting an already existing instance.

To select an instance from the model, simply click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] in the slot value cell and choose the instance from the tree or list in the open dialog.

To create an instance as a slot value in the Instance Table

1. Select the slot value cell.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='create_instance_specification.png'] . If the slot type has subtypes, choose one of them. [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_selecting_subtype_for_new_instance.png'] The new instance is simultaneously created in the model and set as the slot value. You can see this instance in the Model Browser, where it is owned by the classifier, which is a type or a subtype of the selected slot.

To remove an instance from the cell

1. Select the cell.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_instance_specification.png'] . The instance is removed from the Instance Table, but not from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the slot type is a classifier, you can specify an instance of this classifier as the slot value. Do this in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link">By creating a new instance.</span></li><li>By selecting an already existing instance.</li></ul><p>To select an instance from the model, simply click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:alt="Edit"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png" /></ac:image> in the slot value cell and choose the instance from the tree or list in the open dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To create an instance as a slot value in the Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the slot value cell.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Create Instance Specification" ac:alt="Create Instance Specification"><ri:attachment ri:filename="create_instance_specification.png" /></ac:image>. If the slot type has subtypes, choose one of them.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Choosing subtype for new instance" ac:alt="Choosing subtype for new instance"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_selecting_subtype_for_new_instance.png" /></ac:image> <br /><p>The new instance is simultaneously created in the model and set as the slot value. You can see this instance in the Model Browser, where it is owned by the classifier, which is a type or a subtype of the selected slot.</p></li></ol><p>To remove an instance from the cell</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the cell.</li><li>Click <ac:image ac:title="Remove" ac:alt="Remove"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_instance_specification.png" /></ac:image>.<br />The instance is removed from the Instance Table, but not from the model.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967184 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06281: Specifying Legend Item adornment

- page_id: `243967184`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967184/Specifying+Legend+Item+adornment
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:24.149+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Legends > Creating Legends > Specifying Legend Items
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

582405493

582405495

582405494

Each Legend Item of a Legend should have a unique style. This style is defined by specifying Legend Item adornment properties. After [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space=''] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legends' space=''] a Legend in a diagram or table, Legend Item adornment (style) is [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space=''] to the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements and represented as the Legend Item icon inside the Legend. There is a wide range of Legend Item adornment properties, including:

- Fill color
- Pen color
- Text color
- Line width
- Shape or path line color, style, and width
- Icon and icon position on shape or path
- Representation as a square shape, rectangular shape, line, or extended line

You can specify the adornment properties of a Legend Item in the **Adornment Properties** dialog.

To open the adornment properties of a Legend Item

1. Open the Specification window of a Legend Item.
2. Click the specification cell of the Adornment property, and then click [ATTACHMENT filename='edit.png'] .
3. In the Adornment Properties dialog, specify the desired adornment properties.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Adornment Properties dialog.

582461300

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying Legend Items' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying Legend Items' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6356b39a-8b7d-4ed6-9424-2fe5b4a5fb19"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">582405493</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba54ed94-7bde-49ca-89ae-c498c44b3874"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">582405495</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48315add-3ccf-4125-ab41-96ed910e4d5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">582405494</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Each Legend Item of a Legend should have a unique style. This style is defined by specifying Legend Item adornment properties. After <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[creating]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legends" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[displaying]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> a Legend in a diagram or table, Legend Item adornment (style) is <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[applied]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to the symbols or table rows of corresponding elements and represented as the Legend Item icon inside the Legend. There is a wide range of Legend Item adornment properties, including:</p><ul><li>Fill color</li><li>Pen color</li><li>Text color</li><li>Line width</li><li>Shape or path line color, style, and width</li><li>Icon and icon position on shape or path</li><li>Representation as a square shape, rectangular shape, line, or extended line<br /><br /></li></ul><p>You can specify the adornment properties of a Legend Item in the <strong>Adornment Properties</strong> dialog.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open the adornment properties of a Legend Item</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Open the Specification window of a Legend Item.</p></li><li>Click the specification cell of the <strong>Adornment</strong> property, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>In the <strong>Adornment Properties</strong> dialog, specify the desired adornment properties.<br /><br /></li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="adornment_properties_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Adornment Properties dialog.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="754563e4-7408-4865-b335-75ab8ee8fc9c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">582461300</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying Legend Items" /></ac:link><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying Legend Items" /></ac:link></li></ul></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 06282: Specifying Legend Items

- page_id: `243967182`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

582278894

582278896

582278895

When you [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating and managing Legend Items' space=''], you have to specify its name, adornment and application properties. The following pages explain how to do it:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Specification window of a Legend Item.

582278893

**Related pages**

  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Legends' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243967407 space=MT version=3 -->
## PAGE 06283: Specifying or removing ignored aspects

- page_id: `243967407`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243967407/Specifying+or+removing+ignored+aspects
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-08-25T12:11:14.702+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagramming > Diagram aspects
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All of the available aspects are visible when trying:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating an aspect diagram' space='']: in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']**Create Diagram** dialog under the Aspect Diagrams group.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams' space=''] : in the diagram shortcut menu under the Aspect command group.

If you have many diagram aspects defined, but you need to use only a few of them in your project, you can restrict the visibility of any unnecessary aspects in your project. The procedure below describes how to specify/remove ignored aspects.

To specify/remove ignored aspects in your project

1. From the main menu, select Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, expand the General property group, and select the Diagram Aspects .
3. On the right side of the Project Options dialog, select the Ignored Aspects option value.
4. Click the Edit button.
5. In the **Select Stereotype** dialog, select aspects that you do not want to use in your project. If you want to use them in your project again, remove them from this list by double-clicking on the aspect in the Selected elements area. Click **OK**. InformationIf you have [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space=''] installed, you can see predefined aspects in the **List** tab by clearing the **Apply Filter** check box in the **Select Stereotype** dialog.
6. Click OK .

All aspects added into **Ignored Aspect**option value are no longer visible when trying to create a new aspect diagram or to apply it to a pre-existing diagram. To see them again in the model, you must remove them from the **Ignored Aspect**option value.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>All of the available aspects are visible when trying:</p><ul><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating an aspect diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To create a new aspect diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>:</span> in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating diagrams" /><ac:link-body><strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> under the <strong>Aspect Diagrams</strong> group.</li><li><span class="confluence-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Applying or removing aspect for preexisting diagrams" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[To apply aspect for a diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></span>: in the diagram shortcut menu under the <strong>Aspect</strong> command group.</li></ul><p>If you have many diagram aspects defined, but you need to use only a few of them in your project, you can restrict the visibility of any unnecessary aspects in your project. The procedure below describes how to specify/remove ignored aspects.</p><p>To specify/remove ignored aspects in your project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, expand the <strong>General</strong> property group, and select the <strong>Diagram Aspects</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the <strong>Project Options</strong> dialog, select the <strong>Ignored Aspects </strong>option value.</li><li>Click the <strong>Edit</strong> button.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Select Stereotype</strong> dialog, select aspects that you do not want to use in your project. If you want to use them in your project again, remove them from this list by double-clicking on the aspect in the Selected elements area. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="533336c9-f263-4f4d-860b-3a0ad35545be"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML v1 Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> installed, you can see predefined aspects in the <strong>List</strong> tab by clearing the <strong>Apply Filter</strong> check box in the <strong>Select Stereotype</strong> dialog.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p>All aspects added into <strong>Ignored Aspect </strong>option value are no longer visible when trying to create a new aspect diagram or to apply it to a pre-existing diagram. To see them again in the model, you must remove them from the <strong>Ignored Aspect </strong>option value.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968646 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06284: Specifying parameter definitions

- page_id: `243968646`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968646/Specifying+parameter+definitions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:35.674+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Metric Suites
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1891316524

1891316527

1891316522

**On this page**

4

1891316526

The parameter definition of a Metric Suite can be specified:

- As an attribute with the «ParameterDefinition» stereotype.
- In the body of a metric definition.

If you need to track parameter values, the parameter definition should be specified as the attribute of a Metric Suite. In this case, each value of the parameter becomes the slot of an instance created when running the Metric Suite. If a Metric Suite is created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, it already has a few parameter definitions inherited through the Generalization relationship, such as *scope* and *coveringScope*. If you have created a Metric Suite from scratch or the inherited parameters don't meet your needs, you should specify your own parameter definitions.

To specify a parameter definition as the attribute of a Metric Suite

1. Select the shape of a Metric Suite.
2. Click the Create Element button on the right side of the shape and select Parameter definition . The attribute with the «ParamterDefinition» stereotype is created. [ATTACHMENT filename='create_element_button.png']
3. Type its name directly on the shape, and then click a free space on the diagram.
4. Select the attribute on the shape and click [ATTACHMENT filename='specify_type_button.png'] .
5. Select the type of the parameter. This step is mandatory. A parameter must have a type.

The parameter definition is specified.

Instead of defining the parameters from scratch, you can copy them from the *BaseMetricSuite* and then modify according to your needs.

##### Declaring the context

One of the parameter definitions must be declared as the context of a Metric Suite. If a Metric Suite has the context declared, then any element, for which you select to calculate appropriate metrics, becomes the value of the context parameter definition of this Metric Suite and is used for calculating metrics. Otherwise, calculating metrics for the selected element makes no sense (unless the context is declared in the body of a metric definition).

Make sure the type of the parameter you are going to declare as the context is the same as the type of the target of a Metric Suite.

To declare the context for a Metric Suite

1. On the shape of a Metric Suite, double-click the parameter you need to define as the context for the Metric Suite. The Specification window of the parameter opens.
2. Set the Set As Context property value as true .
3. Click the Close button.

The parameter is declared as the context of the Metric Suite.

If your Metric Suite is created using the *BaseMetricSuite*, it already has the context parameter definition - the *scope*.

1891322135

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Metric Suites' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Adding a new Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specify the target for the Metric Suite' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Building validation-based metric definitions' space='']
  - [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specific use cases' space='']

Otherwise, calculating metrics for the selected element makes no sense (unless the context is declared in the body of a metric definition).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5089673b-180e-45b5-9d99-87f3f046a2e5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Make sure the type of the parameter you are going to declare as the context is the same as the type of the target of a Metric Suite.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /><br />To declare the context for a Metric Suite</p><hr /><ol><li>On the shape of a Metric Suite, double-click the parameter you need to define as the context for the Metric Suite.
