<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969095 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06285: Specifying parameters

- page_id: `243969095`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969095/Specifying+parameters
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:18.037+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Specifying criteria for querying model > Creating new operations > Creating script operations
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The number of parameters a script operation can have depends on the script operation language:

- An OCL expression can have a single parameter, which by default specifies the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] . Though the parameter cannot be removed, you can easily reset the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space=''] , if necessary.
- A binary expression inherits parameters from the Java class to which the expression body references. You cannot create additional parameters.
- Other script operations, such as JavaScript or Groovy, can have as many parameters as you need (see the following figure).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The number of parameters a script operation can have depends on the script operation language:</p><ul><li>An OCL expression can have a single parameter, which by default specifies the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. Though the parameter cannot be removed, you can easily reset the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Contextual element]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, if necessary.</li><li>A binary expression inherits parameters from the Java class to which the expression body references. You cannot create additional parameters.</li><li>Other script operations, such as JavaScript or Groovy, can have as many parameters as you need (see the following figure).</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_parameters.png" /></ac:image> </p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=254421849 space=MT version=6 -->
## PAGE 06286: Specifying Parent Object and Parent Rule properties

- page_id: `254421849`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/254421849/Specifying+Parent+Object+and+Parent+Rule+properties
- version_number: 6
- version_date: `2025-09-12T14:46:04.537+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Specifying the **Parent Rule** and **Parent Object** properties for validation rules allows you to relate validation rules and group their validation results hierarchically in the **Validation Results** panel for easier analysis.

##### Specifying the Parent Rule and Parent Object properties

To specify the Parent Rule and Parent Object properties

1. Open the **Specification**window of the validation rule whose results you want to group in the **Validation Results**panel. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']>]]>
2. Specify the Parent Rule property:
  1. Find the **Parent Rule** property and click the [...] button in its value box. The Select Constraint dialog opens.
  2. In the Select Constraint dialog, click the validation rule whose failing element you want to be displayed as the grouping one in the Validation Results panel. Click OK .
3. Specify the Parent Object property:
  1. Find the **Parent Object** property and click the [...] button in its value box. The Parent Object dialog opens.
  2. In the Parent Object dialog, select Simple Navigation, locate the Owner property, and set it to true in the Is Applied column. Click OK .
4. Click Close .

Now, in the **Validation Results** panel, elements that fail validation will display their failed elements as child items.

##### Example: Relating validation rules to display grouped validation results

In this example, we define two validation rules:

- **Disapproved Requirements** – identifies requirements with the status value *Disapproved*.
- **Package with Disapproved Requirements** – identifies packages that contain at least one requirement with a *Disapproved* status.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Then we set the **Parent Rule** and **Parent Object** properties for the **Disapproved Requirements** validation rule:

- **Parent Rule**property is set to the **Package with Disapproved Requirements**validation rule. This links the requirement rule to the package rule.
- **Parent Object**property is set to a Simple Navigation expression with the **Owner** criterion. This ensures failed requirements are grouped under their owning package in the **Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

As a result, the individual requirement failures (raised by the **Disapproved Requirements**validation rule) are grouped under the requirements' parent package failure (raised by **Package with Disapproved Requirements**)****in the **Validation Results** panel.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To explore the example further, please find the attached sample project [ATTACHMENT filename='parent rule and parent object of validation rules.mdzip'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Specifying the <strong>Parent Rule</strong> and <strong>Parent Object</strong> properties for validation rules allows you to relate validation rules and group their validation results hierarchically in the <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel for easier analysis.</p><h3>Specifying the Parent Rule and Parent Object properties</h3><p>To specify the Parent Rule and Parent Object properties</p><hr /><ol><li data-uuid="fc7d14eb-b18f-4416-831f-d4a8f2878ad3"><p>Open the <strong>Specification </strong>window of the validation rule whose results you want to group in the <strong>Validation Results </strong>panel. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Specification window >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></li><li data-uuid="12cfc4e9-5602-4390-8d6e-2e2e4b93181b">Specify the <strong>Parent Rule</strong> property:<ol><li>Find the<span> <strong>Parent Rule</strong></span><span> </span>property and click the <strong>[...]</strong><span> </span>button in its value box. The<span> </span><strong>Select Constraint<span> </span></strong>dialog opens. </li><li>In the<span> </span><strong>Select Constraint<span> </span></strong>dialog, click the validation rule whose failing element you want to be displayed as the grouping one in the <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="4cb831b5-578b-4451-99ee-49bda63e1975">Specify the <strong>Parent Object</strong> property:<ol><li>Find the<span> <strong>Parent Object</strong></span><span> </span>property and click the <strong>[...]</strong><span> </span>button in its value box. The<span> </span><strong>Parent Object<span> </span></strong>dialog opens. </li><li>In the <strong>Parent Object</strong> dialog, select Simple Navigation, locate the <strong>Owner </strong>property, and set it to <strong>true </strong>in the <strong>Is Applied </strong>column. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol></li><li data-uuid="f2401885-6433-4838-8bec-1c80eac12080">Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><p>Now, in the <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel, elements that fail validation will display their failed elements as child items.</p><h3>Example: Relating validation rules to display grouped validation results</h3><p>In this example, we define two validation rules:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c31c29ff-299a-4573-a955-899addbb256a"><p><strong>Disapproved Requirements</strong> – identifies requirements with the status value <em>Disapproved</em>.</p></li><li data-uuid="85585011-208c-47a1-b090-e1ce6677ea7a"><p><strong>Package with Disapproved Requirements</strong> – identifies packages that contain at least one requirement with a <em>Disapproved</em> status.</p></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validation_rules.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Then we set the <strong>Parent Rule</strong> and <strong>Parent Object</strong> properties for the <strong>Disapproved Requirements</strong> validation rule:</p><ul><li data-uuid="e022840e-98e2-4726-bc2d-bacb2b4e6505"><p><strong>Parent Rule </strong>property is set to the <strong>Package with Disapproved Requirements </strong>validation rule. This links the requirement rule to the package rule.</p></li><li data-uuid="28f1e6e7-ea12-4558-9395-f2e352d6056d"><p><strong>Parent Object </strong>property is set to a Simple Navigation expression with the <strong>Owner</strong> criterion. This ensures failed requirements are grouped under their owning package in the <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel.</p></li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="specifying_parent_object_parent_rule.png" /></ac:image></p><p>As a result, the individual requirement failures (raised by the <strong>Disapproved Requirements </strong>validation rule) are grouped under the requirements' parent package failure (raised by <strong>Package with Disapproved Requirements</strong>)<strong> </strong>in the <strong>Validation Results</strong> panel.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="grouped_validation_results.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="901ec99d-bc43-464a-860e-6dd686439774"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To explore the example further, please find the attached sample project <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="parent rule and parent object of validation rules.mdzip" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966504 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06287: Specifying relationship line styles

- page_id: `243966504`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966504/Specifying+relationship+line+styles
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:22:33.208+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Using Dependency Matrix > Selecting criteria
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify the colors, line styles, or, alternatively, set an icon for the representation of your relationships in **Dependency Matrices**. To be able to do this, you need to access the **Style** dialog in the first place.

To open the **Style** dialog

1. Open the Dependency Criteria dialog by
  - double-clicking the Legend .
  - clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to Dependency Criteria in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix Criteria area' space='']**Criteria** area .
2. Open the Style dialog by clicking the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button in the Style column of your selected relation criterion. [ATTACHMENT filename='dependency_criteria_dependency_matrix.png']
3. In the Style dialog, customize Color , Style , Start Arrow , End Arrow or set Representation Icon (see the below for more information). [ATTACHMENT filename='style_dialog.png']
4. When you are done, click OK to close the dialog.

##### TableStyle Options

| Color | Specify the line color. |
| --- | --- |
| Style | Specify the line style. |
| Start Arrow | Specify the arrowhead style. |
| End Arrow | Specify the arrow end style. |
| Representation Icon | Represent relationships using an icon.File - upload the icon image from your file system.URL - add a path to the icon available on a shared location.Remove - remove the uploaded icon image or the specified URL. |

Your selection is shown in the customized legend in the top-left corner or above the matrix. The legend is displayed by default in all new projects, but if you don't want to see it all the time, simply click the [IMAGE alt='' src='']button in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix toolbar' space=''] and then click **Legend Location** > **Do Not Display**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The legend shown in the top-left corner of the Dependency matrix.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The legend shown above column header in the Dependency matrix.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">You can specify the colors, line styles, or, alternatively, set an icon for the representation of your relationships in <strong>Dependency Matrices</strong>. To be able to do this, you need to access the <strong>Style</strong> dialog in the first place.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To open the <strong>Style</strong> dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Dependency Criteria</strong> dialog by<br /><ul><li>double-clicking the <strong>Legend</strong>.</li><li>clicking the <span criterion.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dependency_criteria_dependency_matrix.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li><li>In the <strong>Style</strong> dialog, customize /></ac:image></li><li>When you are done, click <strong>OK</strong> to close the dialog.<br /><br /></li></ol><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="87a1eabb-2cae-4242-ab97-2d6efd75dae2"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Table</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Style Options</h3><table class="relative-table" style="width: <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966495 space=MT version=3 -->
````
## PAGE 06288: Specifying scope

- page_id: `243966495`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966495/Specifying+scope
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2025-09-12T08:53:22.490+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams > Dependency Matrix > Using Dependency Matrix > Selecting criteria
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you define a scope for the row or a column, you can:

- , dragging selected packages on the scope box
- 
- , building a list of elements related through the defined criteria

##### Specify the row/column scopeSpecify the row/column scope

To specify the row/column scope

Do one of the following:

- In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] , select one or more packages and drag them to the Row / Column Scope box in the Criteria area. [ATTACHMENT filename='multiple_drag_scope.png']

- For the multiple selection Press and hold Shift or Ctrl.
- If you want to add additional elements, press and hold Ctrl while dragging the elements to the Row / Column Scope box.

- Click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button next to the **Row**/**Column Scope** box and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click **OK**.
- Open the matrix [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] , click the Row / Column Scope property value cell, then click the [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] button, and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click Close .

##### Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy treesDefine expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees

You can specify the criterion for building a personalized Row/Column hierarchy tree in matrices by choosing from the following tree-view options:

- Select Containment to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.
- Select Structure to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.
- Select Custom to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice.

By selecting Structure, you can build a custom tree-like view for the rows and columns of the matrix. Currently it is possible to build a tree hierarchy by specifying your own criteria. You can select the predefined Structure criteria which builds the same hierarchy as in the Structure Tree in Model Browser.

Select Custom if you need to collect elements that are not necessarily owned by the same package. You can specify the query in the **Row/Column Query** dialog and see the results added to it immediately.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Specifying criteria for building a personalized tree-view in a matrix

##### Specify the row/column scope querySpecify the row/column scope query

To specify the row/column scope query

1. Click the Specify Row/Column Query icon in the Row/Column Scope field. [ATTACHMENT filename='row_query_open.png']
2. In the Row/Column Query dialog, select one of the following:
  - Containment . Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the Containment tree. O wned elements will be collected recursively starting from the elements specified as scope values.
  - Structure . Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Structure tab' space=''] . This option is available only when the SysML v1 Plugin is installed.
  - **Custom**. Select if you want to make your own adjustments. Select the Build Hierarchy check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.******** If you have selected **Custom**, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specifying criteria for querying model' space='']. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Click OK .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When you define a scope for the row or a column, you can:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specify the row/column scope" />, dragging selected packages on the scope box</li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees" /></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Specify the row/column scope query" />, building a list of elements related through the defined criteria </li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2e8616cd-efd9-4aec-857c-5e7cd8f744d7"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specify the row/column scope</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specify the row/column scope</h3><p>To specify the row/column scope</p><hr /><p>Do one of the following:</p><ul><li>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Model Browser" /></ac:link>, select one or more packages and drag them to the <strong>Row</strong>/<strong>Column Scope</strong> box in the <strong>Criteria</strong> area.<br /><ac:image ac:border="true" ac:height="557" ac:width="599"><ri:attachment ri:filename="multiple_drag_scope.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="851f1af8-ced8-4532-8dc3-e2ad2cd88ae2"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>For the multiple selection Press and hold Shift or Ctrl.</li><li>If you want to add additional elements, press and hold Ctrl while dragging the elements to the <strong>Row</strong>/<strong>Column Scope</strong> box.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li><p>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png" /></ac:image> button next to the <strong>Row</strong>/<strong>Column Scope</strong> box and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click <strong>OK</strong>.</p></li><li>Open the matrix <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /></ac:link>, click the <strong>Row</strong>/<strong>Column Scope</strong> property value cell, then click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="3dot_button.png" /></ac:image> button, and in the open dialog select the needed packages. Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ul><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d3dead4-bc85-4c96-a989-c3c13439f138"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Define expressions for building personalized hierarchy trees</h3><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">You can specify the criterion for building a personalized Row/Column hierarchy tree in matrices by choosing from the following tree-view options:</span></p><ul><li>Select <strong>Containment</strong> to display the decomposition of elements related through the Containment relationship.</li><li>Select <strong>Structure </strong>to display the decomposition of Part Properties related through the Composition relationship.</li><li>Select <strong>Custom</strong> to display the decomposition of elements related through any criteria of your choice. </li></ul><p><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">By selecting Structure, you can build a custom tree-like view for the rows and columns of the matrix. Currently it is possible to build a tree hierarchy by specifying your own criteria. You can select the predefined Structure criteria which builds the same hierarchy as in the Structure Tree in Model Browser.</span></p><p>Select Custom if you need to collect elements that are not necessarily owned by the same package. You can specify the query in the <strong>Row/Column Query</strong> dialog and see the results added to it immediately.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="customization_of_matrices.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#707070);">Specifying criteria for building a personalized tree-view in a matrix</span></h6><h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a0c1cbce-527d-4cc5-b01b-f2d732b6ba02"><ac:parameter ac:name="">Specify the row/column scope query</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Specify the row/column scope query</h3><p>To specify the row/column scope query</p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Specify Row/Column Query</strong> icon in the <strong>Row/Column Scope</strong> field.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="row_query_open.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the Row/Column Query dialog, select one of the following:<ul><li><strong>Containment</strong>. Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the Containment tree. O<span style="color:var(--ds-text,#333333);">wned elements will be collected recursively starting from the elements specified as scope values.</span></li><li><strong>Structure</strong>. Select if you want that the row/column elements would be displayed as in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Structure tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Structure tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This option is available only when the SysML v1 Plugin is installed.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Custom</strong>. Select if you want to make your own adjustments. Select the Build Hierarchy check box to recursively execute the specified expression for building multilevel hierarchy starting from the scope as the root.<strong><strong><br /></strong></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e34aa9ca-e310-490a-bc04-cb51f8324902"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have selected <strong>Custom</strong>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specifying criteria for querying model" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[specify the expression]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="row_query_dependency_matrix.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966998 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06289: Specifying the classifier

- page_id: `243966998`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966998/Specifying+the+classifier
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:16.577+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Instance table
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Specifying the classifier criteria is necessary. You are not able to add a single instance to the table until the classifier is known.

To specify the classifier for the Instance Table

1. In the Criteria area of the Instance Table, click the ... button next to the Classifier box. The Select Classifier dialog opens.
2. Select one or more elements and add them to the Selected elements list. These are the elements that can be selected:
  - Classifier.
  - Classifier that owns other classifiers. In this case, the classifier and all the classifiers owned by it are specified as criteria.
  - Package that contains classifiers. In this case, the contents of the package, but not the package itself, are specified as criteria.
3. Click OK when you are done.

- To specify the classifier, you can also drag a selected element to the Classifier box directly from the Model Browser.
- To select adjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.
- To select nonadjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.
- Keep in mind that the new value of the Classifier box replaces the old value. [ATTACHMENT filename='instance_table_dnd_to_specify_classifier.png']

Once the classifier is specified, the following columns are displayed in the Instance Table (on the right of the row number # column):

- Name which is up to display the names of the instances of the specified classifier.
- Columns corresponding to the Data type attributes of the specified classifier and named after these attributes using sentence style capitalization. The columns are up to display the slot values of the instances of the specified classifier.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Specifying the classifier criteria is necessary. You are not able to add a single instance to the table until the classifier is known.</p><p>To specify the classifier for the Instance Table</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Criteria area of the Instance Table, click the<strong> ...</strong> button next to the Classifier box. The <strong>Select Classifier</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select one or more elements and add them to the Selected elements list. These are the elements that can be selected:<br /><ul><li>Classifier.</li><li>Classifier that owns other classifiers. In this case, the classifier and all the classifiers owned by it are specified as criteria.</li><li>Package that contains classifiers. In this case, the contents of the package, but not the package itself, are specified as criteria.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong> when you are done.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4e142bbc-9627-4f9b-b361-4be03d8c69fd"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>To specify the classifier, you can also drag a selected element to the Classifier box directly from the Model Browser.</li><li>To select adjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Shift key while you click other elements.</li><li>To select nonadjacent elements, select a single element and then hold down the Ctrl key while you click other elements.</li><li>Keep in mind that the new value of the Classifier box replaces the old value.<br /> <ac:image ac:title="Using drag-and-drop operation to specify classifier for Instance Table" ac:alt="Using drag-and-drop operation to specify classifier for Instance Table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="instance_table_dnd_to_specify_classifier.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Once the classifier is specified, the following columns are displayed in the Instance Table (on the right of the row number # column):</p><ul><li>Name which is up to display the names of the instances of the specified classifier.</li><li>Columns corresponding to the Data type attributes of the specified classifier and named after these attributes using sentence style capitalization. The columns are up to display the slot values of the instances of the specified classifier.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966825 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06290: Specifying the scope for the table

- page_id: `243966825`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966825/Specifying+the+scope+for+the+table
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:50:09.687+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > Tables > Basic tasks in tables
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can specify the scope in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Table Criteria area' space=''] for every table.

To specify the scope

1. In the Criteria area of the table, click the ... button next to the Scope (optional) box.
2. In the **Select Scope** dialog, select one or more containers, such as packages or smart packages, and add them to the**Selected Elements** list. The **Select Scope** dialog is a modification of the element[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Elements multiple selection' space='']**Selection** dialog.
3. Click **OK**. 
The scope is defined, and the contents of the table are updated with the elements from the selected scope. In the future, all elements created in that scope will automatically be added to the table.

Alternatively, you can select one or more elements directly in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Browser' space=''] and drag them to the **Scope (optional)**box, as shown in the image below:

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If the contents of the table have not been updated, try the following:

- Click [ATTACHMENT filename='refresh_button.png'] on the View toolbar, in case a smart package is specified as the scope criteria.
- Make sure the specified scope contains elements specified in the Element Type box, Metric Suite box, or Classifier box.
- Make sure at least one element is specified for the table.

In addition to specifying the scope for the table yourself, you can set the expression to collect table elements automatically.

To specify the expression to collect row elements

1. In the **Criteria** area, next to the **Scope** box, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] icon . [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. Once the**Query**dialog opens (see an image below), select **Custom**from the list of available query criteria.
3. Specify the desired expression to collect elements for your table automatically.
4. Click **OK**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']Specifying the expression in the Query dialog

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can specify the scope in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Table Criteria area" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[table Criteria area]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for every table.</p><p>To specify the scope</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Criteria area of the table, click the ... button next to the <strong>Scope (optional)</strong> box.</li><li><p>In the <strong>Select Scope</strong> dialog, select one or more containers, such as packages or smart packages, and add them to the<strong> Selected Elements</strong> list.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96b6bea9-1434-4bc8-a02f-b0131266eb14"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Select Scope</strong> dialog is a modification of the <span class="confluence-link">element </span><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Elements multiple selection" /><ac:link-body><strong>Selection</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br />The scope is defined, and the contents of the table are updated with the elements from the selected scope. In the future, all elements created in that scope will automatically be added to the table.</p></li></ol><p>Alternatively, you can select one or more elements directly in the <ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969590 space=MT version=2 -->
````
## PAGE 06291: Specifying the validation rule properties

- page_id: `243969590`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969590/Specifying+the+validation+rule+properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:24:08.310+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation > Custom validation > Creating new validation rules
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

If you applied the «validationRule» stereotype to the Constraint element, you must specify the following validation rule properties in its Specification window:**Abbreviation**, **Error Message**, and **Severity**.

To specify the validation rule properties

1. Open the Constraint Specification window. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']>]]>
2. On the right side of the window, find the Validation Rule group.
3. Specify the following properties:
  - **Abbreviation**. Select the **Abbreviation** property value box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and type a short word, phase, acronym of the validation rule. It allows you to distinguish the validation rules among other rules when sorting or filtering.
  - **Error Message**. Select the **Error Message** property value box, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and type the description of the invalid situation when this validation rule fails. If you need to define the variable error message, you can embed expressions directly in the **Error Message** value box by using curly brackets - {}. Everything between them is treated as an expression and evaluated for each validation result. Expressions are treated as OCL2.0 expressions by default, but you can also use binary expressions: {bin: <binary expression>}. For example, in the following error message the budget is calculated according to variables. The result of the expression is provided as number in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel, e.g. Budget not balanced - overbudget by $500.sum() - 
Sponsor::allInstances().contribution->sum()}$]]>You can insert a hyperlink. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining hyperlinks' space='']Learn more about defining hyperlinks >>
  - Severity . Select the Severity property value and from the drop-down list select one of the following severity levels:
  - 
    - debug - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated for the developer.
    - info - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated mostly for the end-user.
    - warning - for incorrect situations that can cause errors. It is used for less severe situations than the error.
    - error - for incorrect situations that must be solved.
    - **fatal** - for critical errors such as model corruption or model is invalid according to the UML metamodel. This severity level is mostly reserved for future use. The icons on invalid elements in the model depend on the selected severity level described above. [Learn more about invalid elements representation >>](https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation#Validation-Invalidelementsrepresentation)You can customize severity levels. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing severity levels' space='']>]]>
4. Click Close . The validation rule properties are specified.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1443801d-146f-4840-9bbe-738a899e05a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you applied the «validationRule» stereotype to the Constraint element, you must specify the following validation rule properties in its Specification window:<strong> Abbreviation</strong>, <strong>Error Message</strong>, and <strong>Severity</strong>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To specify the validation rule properties</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the Constraint Specification window. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Specification window" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Specification window >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>On the right side of the window, find the <strong>Validation Rule</strong> group.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Specify the following properties:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Abbreviation</strong>. Select the <strong>Abbreviation</strong> property value box, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png" /></ac:image> and type a short word, phase, acronym of the validation rule. It allows you to distinguish the validation rules among other rules when sorting or filtering.</p></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Error Message</strong>. Select the <strong>Error Message</strong> property value box, click <ac:image ac:title="Edit" ac:thumbnail="true" ac:alt="Edit" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button_in_specification_window.png" /></ac:image> and type the description of the invalid situation when this validation rule fails.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2997c06-a86d-48c7-b726-15df7b242706"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to define the variable error message, you can embed expressions directly in the <strong>Error Message</strong> value box by using curly brackets - {}. Everything between them is treated as an expression and evaluated for each validation result. Expressions are treated as OCL2.0 expressions by default, but you can also use binary expressions: {bin: &lt;binary expression&gt;}. For example, in the following error message the budget is calculated according to variables. The result of the expression is provided as number in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation Results panel" /><ac:link-body><strong>Validation Results</strong> panel</ac:link-body></ac:link>, e.g. Budget not  balanced - overbudget by $500.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2db7e489-1562-4746-87c3-a6f22b58fdbf"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Budget not  balanced - overbudget by {
ResearchProject::allInstances().budget->sum() - 
Sponsor::allInstances().contribution->sum()}$]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">You can insert a hyperlink.</span> <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Defining hyperlinks" /><ac:link-body>L<span class="confluence-link">earn more about defining hyperlinks &gt;&gt;</span> </ac:link-body></ac:link></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li><strong>Severity</strong>. Select the <strong>Severity </strong>property value and from the drop-down list select one of the following severity levels:</li></ul><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li><strong>debug</strong> - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated for the developer.</li><li><strong>info</strong> - for missing information of a constrained element that is dedicated mostly for the end-user.</li><li><strong>warning</strong> - for incorrect situations that can cause errors. It is used for less severe situations than the error.</li><li><strong>error</strong> - for incorrect situations that must be solved.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>fatal</strong> - for critical errors such as model corruption or model is invalid according to the UML metamodel. This severity level is mostly reserved for future use.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab9e91b2-a79a-4e36-ab79-0c0cecf3cf86"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The icons on invalid elements in the model depend on the selected severity level described above. <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/MT/Validation#Validation-Invalidelementsrepresentation">Learn more about invalid elements representation &gt;&gt;</a> </p><p>You can customize severity levels. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing severity levels" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to customize severity levels >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.<br />The validation rule properties are specified.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968749 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06292: Spell Checking as you type

- page_id: `243968749`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968749/Spell+Checking+as+you+type
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:09.908+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

264626882

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

264626885

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

264626884

Spell Checker checks if a word is correct as you type. If the word is incorrect, it is underlined with a red winding line. Right-clicking the underlined word brings up a shortcut menu menu with suggested edits and gives you the option to add the word to the dictionary or custom glossary.

Spell checking as you type is performed in the following locations in a modeling tool:

- symbol names on a diagram pane,
- for properties in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''] ,
- in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Documentation tab' space=''] ,
- in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] ,
- In various locations on log messages, names, typing and comment boxes.

Right-click the underlined word to invoke the shortcut menu in the following locations:

- Diagram pane [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_diagram_pane.png']
- Containment tree [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_containment_tree.png']
- Specification windows [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_Specification_window.png']

Correct the spelling error

Do one of the following:

- An incorrect word can be changed by typing or by selecting provided suggestion that is always correct syntactically, but not always correct semantically.
- The Ignore command. If you select Ignore , the incorrectly spelled word is treated as correct for this time but will be considered to be incorrectly spelled in other cases.
- The Add to dictionary command. If you select Add to dictionary , the incorrect word will be added to a custom dictionary. Next time it will not be treated as incorrect.
- The Add to New Glossary command. Select Add to New Glossary to create a new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Glossary table' space=''] of terms.

#### INFO: Note

Note

Incorrectly spelled words are underlined only in the edit mode of a particular component. If you turn edit mode off, the underline disappears and vice versa.

264626881

**Related Pages:**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='']**

If you select <strong>Ignore</strong>, the incorrectly spelled word is treated as correct for this time but will be considered to be incorrectly spelled in other cases.</li><li>The <strong>Add to dictionary</strong> command. If you select <strong>Add to dictionary</strong>, the incorrect word will be added to a custom dictionary. Next time it will not be treated as incorrect.</li><li>The <strong>Add to New Glossary</strong> command. Select <strong>Add to New Glossary </strong>to create a new <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Glossary table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[glossary]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of terms.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba6c68f7-eb13-4e98-b241-488152f95fec"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>Incorrectly spelled words are underlined only in the edit mode of a particular component. If you turn edit mode off, the underline disappears and vice versa. </ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span><br /></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9603248f-c1ef-4014-8102-de6f98eda747"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">264626881</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related Pages:</strong></p><p><strong><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="80636947-51f9-4dfa-bca3-c54b3bc9a6d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="page"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Spelling Checker" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></strong></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968753 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06293: Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope

- page_id: `243968753`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968753/Spell+checking+for+the+entire+project+or+a+selected+scope
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:42.284+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Spelling Checker
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Correct the error

265580468

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Correct the error

265580471

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Correct the error

265580470

To check spelling for the entire project or a selected scope

1. On the main menu, click Tools > Check Spelling . The Check Spelling dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='check_spelling_dialog.png']
2. In the Check Spelling For drop-down list, select the scope. For Selection , click [ATTACHMENT filename='3dot_button.png'] to define a project scope to check. It may be a package, a diagram, or an element.
3. Click the Spelling Options button to specify spelling options in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options**dialog .
4. Click the Check button. Results are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation Results panel' space='']**Validation Results** panel .

To correct an error

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the Validation Results panel, select the error and right-click it to open the shortcut menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_error_validation_results_pane.png']
  - In the Containment tree, select the element with an error (marked with a red circle and white cross inside) and right-click it to open the shortcut menu. In the shortcut menu, select **Validation** > **<Error place> Spelling**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Correct the errorClick the element name to edit and right-click it to see a spelling solution.[IMAGE alt='' src='']
  - On the diagram pane, select the element with an error (highlighted with a red border) and click the failure indicator to open the shortcut menu. [ATTACHMENT filename='spelling_error_shape.png']
2. In the shortcut menu of an element with an error, select the Correct command. The Spell Checker dialog opens. [ATTACHMENT filename='spell_checker_dialog.png']
3. In the Value area, all incorrectly spelled words are underlined. Correct the words.
4. Click Next to go to the next spelling error (or Previous to return to the previous spelling error) found during the validation.
5. To close the dialog and save changes, click OK . To close the dialog without saving changes, click Cancel .

265580467

**Related Pages:**

**[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spelling Checker' space='']**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968748 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06294: Spelling Checker

- page_id: `243968748`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968748/Spelling+Checker
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:09.816+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1122009104

1122009106

1122009105

Spelling Checker enables you to:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spell Checking as you type' space=''] . A shortcut menu provides spelling options. Right-click the word underlined in red to enter the shortcut menu and display the Spelling commands. You can also enter words in a customized dictionary.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope' space=''] You can list all the spelling errors found in a project and easily correct them.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting the spell checking options' space=''] . You can set spelling checker options, such as skipping numbers or uppercase words, in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space='']**Environment Options** dialog .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining properties of the customized element to be spell checked' space=''] . All "Open Office" supportive spelling languages can be added to the existing ones.

1122009103

**Related pages**

You can also enter words in a customized dictionary.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Spell checking for the entire project or a selected scope" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Check the spelling of a whole project or of a selected part.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> You can list all the spelling errors found in a project and easily correct them.</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting the spell checking options" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Set Spelling Checker options]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970825 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06295: Splitting images

- page_id: `243970825`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970825/Splitting+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:59.744+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

2050262061

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

2050262073

2050262059

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

2050262063

If you have an image that is larger than the paper size, you can split the image into smaller tiles.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**$image.split()** supports only the JPG and PNG image formats.

###### **$image.split($diagram.image, columns, rows)**

Split an image into rows and columns. The returned array of the image contains chunks of images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object. |
|  | column | int | The number of columns. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of columns from the paper width. |
|  | row | int | The number of rows. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of rows from the paper height. |
| Return |  | java.util.List<com.nomagic.magicreport.Image> | The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom. |

For example, to split an image into 3x2 tiles, type the following lines:

```text

```

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']The original image vs. 3x2 tiled image.

###### **$image.split($diagram.image)**

Split an image into rows and columns by automatically calculating the number of rows and columns based on the paper size. The returned array of the image contains divided images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object. |
| Return |  | java.util.List<com.nomagic.magicreport.Image> | The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom. |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d21bc3f-6328-4953-93ad-77e37bbc087f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050262061</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58755579-3df8-4571-8e2a-9ba4e78a4267"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050262073</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="66c941c5-887d-490b-bf3a-66c434e06381"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050262059</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ab4f9a35-a6c1-4036-9b04-feb095c74624"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b8573900-a0bf-4bc1-b6cf-f0ec5a0d2e42"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">2050262063</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you have an image that is larger than the paper size, you can split the image into smaller tiles.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b2ddd318-00f9-494c-b751-c15117431e50"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>$image.split()</strong> supports only the JPG and PNG image formats.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h4><strong>$image.split($diagram.image, columns, rows)</strong></h4><p>Split an image into rows and columns. The returned array of the image contains chunks of images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 88.0173%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.95816%;" /><col style="width: 5.89722%;" /><col style="width: 26.7902%;" /><col style="width: 58.4106%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>column </td><td> int </td><td>The number of columns. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of columns from the paper width. </td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>row</td><td>int </td><td>The number of rows. Specifies -1 to automate the calculated number of rows from the paper height.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td><br /></td><td>java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.magicreport.Image&gt; </td><td>The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example, to split an image into 3x2 tiles, type the following lines:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="96dc5580-2e90-4e00-8b1c-55357ec5c880"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($d in $Diagram)
#foreach ($chunk in $image.split($d.image, 3, 2))
$chunk
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Original Image vs. 3x2 Tiled Image" ac:alt="Original Image vs. 3x2 Tiled Image"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Normal and Split Image.png" /></ac:image>The original image vs. 3x2 tiled image.</h6><h4><strong>$image.split($diagram.image)</strong></h4><p>Split an image into rows and columns by automatically calculating the number of rows and columns based on the paper size. The returned array of the image contains divided images arranged from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 88.1654%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.94309%;" /><col style="width: 5.29857%;" /><col style="width: 27.0816%;" /><col style="width: 58.7328%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>image </td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image </td><td>An image object. </td></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td><br /></td><td>java.util.List&lt;com.nomagic.magicreport.Image&gt;  </td><td>The array of the split image ordered from left-to-right and top-to-bottom.</td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243970708 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06296: Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram

- page_id: `243970708`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243970708/Spreadsheet+Report+Designer+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:53.596+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Report Designer
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

983474219

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

983474231

985834246

**On this page**

4

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

983474221

#### Using the Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram

In this section, you will learn how to create the Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram and generate it to an XLSX template that you can add in Report Wizard for generating reports later.

To create the Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram and generate it to an XLSX template

1. Right-click a Package in the Containment tree and select Report Designer Diagrams > Spreadsheet Report Designer Diagram . [ATTACHMENT filename='Spreadsheet Repot Designer Diagram Menu.png']
2. Create diagram elements in the working area of the diagram and specify values.
3. Arrange diagram elements as needed. [ATTACHMENT filename='Arranging Diagram Elements2.png']
4. In the Containment tree, right-click **Spreadsheet Report Designer Diagram** and select **Generate Template**> **XLSX Template** to generate the template. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] NoteGenerated templates get only symbols on the diagram. Elements in the Containment tree are not used to generate templates.
5. Refine more layouts, style, and code logic in Microsoft Word.
6. Add the complete template to Report Wizard.
7. Generate the report from the added template. [ATTACHMENT filename='Generating Report from Added Template.png']

#### Diagram elements

The Spreadsheet Report Designer diagram provides many diagram elements represented in Velocity syntax/directive.

###### Element Row

An Element Row is represented as the **#forrow** directive for fetching a collection of elements in rows. After creating the Element Row in the diagram, you need to specify an element type as well as column number and row number in the **Element Row** wizard dialog. You can also create the header and border for the table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Row wizard dialog.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you are back to update the element type, column number, or row number in Step 1, the table in Step 2 will be recreated with the blank content.

In Step 2 of the **Element Row** wizard dialog, the blank table is created where you can directly enter and edit text and Velocity code in the table cell. You can also press **CTRL** + **SPACE** to open the Property Guide pop-up dialog. Press **CTRL** + **SPACE** again to navigate you to Report Data Variables and Helper Tools. In this dialog, it guides you to available properties that you can select for the cell.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Property Guide pop-up dialog.

You can insert or delete columns and rows by right-clicking a cell to open the context menu.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The context menu for inserting and deleting rows and columns.

###### Updating an Element Row

After creating an Element Row, you can open the **Element Row** wizard dialog again for updating the **Element Row** table through the smart manipulator button or the **Element Row** editing context menu.

###### Element Page

An Element Page is represented as the **#forpage** directive for fetching a collection of elements in a table per worksheet. This element uses the **Element Row** wizard dialog. Only in Step 2, the **#forpage** directive will be created over the blank table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Element Page.

###### Page Break

You can place the Page Break element on the diagram to be in the template to start a new worksheet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Page Break.

983474217

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567311 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06297: SQL diagrams

- page_id: `249567311`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567311/SQL+diagrams
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:33:20.212+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > Database support
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Cameo Data Modeler provides **SQL Diagram**, a specialized diagram for database modeling that enables you to create and depict various SQL elements. It is located under the **Data Modeling**diagram subgroup.

In addition to the main SQL diagram, there is a slightly modified diagram for Oracle databases, **Oracle SQL Diagram**. It is located under the same **Data Modeling**diagram subgroup. This diagram is only slightly modified. It has an additional diagram button for Materialized View modeling. Other than that, it is identical to the main SQL diagram. If you are not modeling materialized views, you can freely use the generic diagram type instead of the specialized one for Oracle modeling.

##### Crow’s Foot Notation in SQL Diagrams

Once you apply the Cameo Data Modeler plugin to MagicDraw, you can display the crow’s foot notation or use standard UML notation of associations (displaying multiplicities in text format) in the SQL diagram.

To display Multiplicities or crow’s foot notation in a SQL diagram

1. Create the SQL diagram.
2. Draw two tables.
3. Create columns for the tables and some of them as primary keys.
4. Connect the table elements with the Foreign Key relationship.
5. Define Name , PK , and FK in the open Foreign Key dialog box.
6. Open the Project Options dialog box.
7. Select the General project options branch.
8. Change the Show relationship ends as property to either No special notation or Crow’s feet . Multiplicities or crow’s foot notation in the following figures will then be displayed on the Foreign Key ends.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Multiplicities on a Foreign Key relationship in an SQL diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Crow’s foot notation for a Foreign Key relationship in an SQL diagram.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Cameo Data Modeler provides <strong>SQL Diagram</strong>, a specialized diagram for database modeling that enables you to create and depict various SQL elements. It is located under the <strong>Data Modeling </strong>diagram subgroup.</p><p>In addition to the main SQL diagram, there is a slightly modified diagram for Oracle databases, <strong>Oracle SQL Diagram</strong>. It is located under the same <strong>Data Modeling </strong>diagram subgroup. This diagram is only slightly modified. It has an additional diagram button for Materialized View modeling. Other than that, it is identical to the main SQL diagram. If you are not modeling materialized views, you can freely use the generic diagram type instead of the specialized one for Oracle modeling.</p><h3>Crow’s Foot Notation in SQL Diagrams</h3><p>Once you apply the Cameo Data Modeler plugin to MagicDraw, you can display the crow’s foot notation or use standard UML notation of associations (displaying multiplicities in text format) in the SQL diagram.</p><p>To display Multiplicities or crow’s foot notation in a SQL diagram</p><hr /><ol><li>Create the SQL diagram.</li><li>Draw two tables.</li><li>Create columns for the tables and some of them as primary keys.</li><li>Connect the table elements with the Foreign Key relationship.</li><li>Define <strong>Name</strong>, <strong>PK</strong>, and <strong>FK </strong>in the open <strong>Foreign Key </strong>dialog box.</li><li>Open the <strong>Project Options </strong>dialog box.</li><li>Select the <strong>General project options </strong>branch.</li><li>Change the <strong>Show relationship ends as </strong>property to either <strong>No special notation </strong>or <strong>Crow’s feet</strong>. Multiplicities or crow’s foot notation in the following figures will then be displayed on the Foreign Key ends.</li></ol><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 14 Multiplicities on Foreign Key Relationship in SQL Diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Multiplicities on a Foreign Key relationship in an SQL diagram.</h6><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Figure 15 Crow’s Foot Notation for Foreign Key Relationship in SQL Diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Crow’s foot notation for a Foreign Key relationship in an SQL diagram.</h6>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249567401 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06298: SQL to UML transformation

- page_id: `249567401`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249567401/SQL+to+UML+transformation
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T18:32:24.605+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Data-related modeling > Transformations
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

33727203

33727205

33727204

SQL models and diagrams will be transformed into the platform-independent UML models and UML class diagrams. You can apply the SQL to UML transformation to SQL models of any database flavor.

##### **T****yp****e mapping**

After transformation, SQL types should be converted to UML types if types are specified in the SQL model for elements. There is a type mapping from SQL types to UML types.

Mapping rules are based on dependencies, which contain the **SQL to UML Type Map**profile. This profile is automatically attached when SQL to UML transformation is performed.

##### **T****ransformatio****n results**

The SQL stereotypes are discarded from tables, views, fields, and associations (except the PK stereotype). Views are discarded in a transformed class diagram.

There are additional properties to choose for SQL to UML transformation in **Model Transformation Wizard**. For more information about the wizard, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Transformation Wizard' space=''] in the MagicDraw User Guide.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Model transformation wizard for SQL to UML transformation with Specify transformation details wizard step.

| Option name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Use PK | Check box | If set to “true”, appropriate columns with the primary key stereotype are marked after transformation. |
| Use IE | Check box | If set to “true”, indexed columns with the inverted entity stereotype are marked after transformation. |
| Use AK | Check box | If set to “true”, unique columns with the alternative key stereotype are marked after transformation. |

The «IE» stereotype is applied to the columns in the UML model from *indexes*in the SQL.

The «AK» stereotypes are applied to the columns in the UML model from *unique*constraints in the SQL.

If the *unique*or *index*of the SQL contains more than one column, the **group**tag is created on the corresponding columns. The value of the tag is the name of the *unique / index*.

If the PK, *unique*constraint or *index*of the SQL contains more than one column, the **orderInXXGroup**tag is created on the corresponding columns. The value of the tag is the place number of the column in the PK, unique constraint or index (first column gets tag value=1, second column - 2, etc).

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']TBD 3 SQL before transformation.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']TBD 3 SQL after transformation.

There are some foreign key cases when an association with multiplicities is created in a class diagram after transformation, as shown below:****

###### **Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» stereotype is set**

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Transforming foreign key when the «unique» stereotype is set before transformation.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Transforming foreign key when the «unique» stereotype is set after transformation.

###### **Transforming foreign key, when the «not null» stereotype is set**

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Transforming foreign key when the «not null» stereotype is set before transformation.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Transforming foreign key when the «not null» stereotype is set after transformation.

###### **Transforming foreign key, when the «null» stereotype is set**

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Transforming foreign key when the «null» stereotype is set before transformation.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Transforming foreign key when the «null» stereotype is set after transformation.

###### **Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set before transformation.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src='']Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set after transformation.

919492417

**Related page**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Transformation Wizard' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bef0c5d5-e063-4249-abaa-2b6cdca9a8a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33727203</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f54c671-4d3d-4494-876c-7024358c120a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33727205</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e8c413c6-2ec7-41fb-a1a0-ab8b238c9909"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">33727204</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>SQL models and diagrams will be transformed into the platform-independent UML models and UML class diagrams. The value of the tag is the name of the <em>unique / index</em>.</p><p>If the PK, <em>unique </em>constraint or <em>index </em>of the SQL contains more than one column, the <strong>orderInXXGroup </strong>tag is created on the corresponding columns. The value of the tag is the place number of the column in the PK, unique constraint or index (first column gets tag value=1, second column - 2, etc).</p><p><br /></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(1) TBD 3 SQL Screenshots before Transformation.png" /></ac:image>TBD 3 SQL before transformation.</h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(2) TBD 3 SQL Screenshots after Transformation.png" /></ac:image>TBD 3 SQL after transformation.</h6><p><br /></p><p>There are some foreign key cases when an association with multiplicities is created in a class diagram after transformation, as shown below:<strong><br /></strong></p><h4><strong>Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» stereotype is set</strong></h4><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(3) Transforming foreign key when the «unique» stereotype is set (before transformation).png" /></ac:image>Transforming foreign key when the «unique» stereotype is set before transformation.</h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(4) Transforming foreign key when the «unique» stereotype is set (after transformation).png" /></ac:image>Transforming foreign key when the «unique» stereotype is set after transformation.</h6><h4 style="text-align: left;"><strong>Transforming foreign key, when the «not null» stereotype is set</strong></h4><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(5) Transforming foreign key when the «not null» stereotype is set before transformation.png" /></ac:image>Transforming foreign key when the «not null» stereotype is set before transformation.</h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(6) Transforming foreign ke, when the «not null» stereotype is set after transformation.png" /></ac:image>Transforming foreign key when the «not null» stereotype is set after transformation.</h6><h4 style="text-align: left;"><strong>Transforming foreign key, when the «null» stereotype is set</strong></h4><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(7) Transforming foreign key when the «null» stereotype is set before transformation.png" /></ac:image>Transforming foreign key when the «null» stereotype is set before transformation.</h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(8) Transforming foreign key when the «null» stereotype is set after transformation.png" /></ac:image>Transforming foreign key when the «null» stereotype is set after transformation.</h6><h4><strong>Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set</strong></h4><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(9) Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set before transformation.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set before transformation.</h6><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="(10) Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set after transformation.png" /></ac:image>Transforming foreign key, when the «unique» and the «not null» stereotypes are set after transformation.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4969d6a-6dc5-4f2d-b3b5-efb6975ef037"><ac:parameter <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249563666 space=MT version=1 -->
````
## PAGE 06299: Sr Service Roadmap

- page_id: `249563666`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249563666/Sr+Service+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:16:01.501+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > NAF 4.0 > Service viewpoint
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1974596998

1974597000

1974596999

**Description**

The Sr Services Roadmap domain shows the service changes over time. It provides an overview of how a service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Sr Services Roadmap domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.

In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.

The intended usage of the Sr Services Roadmap domain includes:

- Development of incremental acquisition strategy.
- Planning technology insertion.

**Implementation**

The Sr Services Roadmap domain is represented by a Services Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

1974596997

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Versioned Element' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Of Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Succession' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration Kind' space='MED']

It provides an overview of how a service changes over time. It shows the combination of several services mapped against a timeline. The Sr Services Roadmap domain presents a whole lifecycle view of resources (services), describing how it changes over time. It shows the structure of several resources mapped against a timeline.</p><p>In addition, this model is useful in support of net-centric (service-oriented) implementation of services. This model can present a timeline of services evolve or are replaced over time, including services that are internal and external to the scope of the architecture.</p><p>The intended usage of the Sr Services Roadmap domain includes:</p><ul><li>Development of incremental acquisition strategy.</li><li>Planning technology insertion.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Sr Services Roadmap domain is represented by a Services Roadmap diagram which is realized as a time based diagram. </p><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="719d0364-6e40-42c0-b6c3-9e2826b776cf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1974596997</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Project" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Project Milestone" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Milestone Dependency" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Service" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Versioned Element" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Version Of Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Version Succession" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Whole Life Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Whole Life Configuration Kind" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="855a21fb-4d2c-40e6-8025-0c3bc61644f0" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564731 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06302: Standards Roadmap

- page_id: `249564731`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564731/Standards+Roadmap
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:24.432+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Standards
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922198773

922198775

922198774

**Description**

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain shows the expected changes in technology-related standards and conventions, operational standards, or business standards and conventions. It defines the underlying current and expected standards. Expected standards are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology, and expected improvements/trends.

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain complements and expands on the Standards Taxonomy and Standards Traceability views and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is implemented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Roadmap table' space=''].

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subject Of Forecast' space='MED']
