It defines the underlying current and expected standards. Expected standards are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology, and expected improvements/trends.</p><p>The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. The Standards Roadmap (Sd-Rm) domain complements and expands on the Standards Taxonomy and Standards Traceability views and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the 922668141

**Description**

The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain shows technical and non-technical standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture. It shows the taxonomy of types of technical, operational, and business standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture.

The intended usage of the Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Taxonomy diagram' space=''] diagram.

It shows the taxonomy of types of technical, operational, and business standards, guidance and policy applicable to the architecture.</p><p>The intended usage of the Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain includes:</p><ul><li>Application of standards (informing project strategy).</li><li>Standards compliance.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The Standards Taxonomy (Pj-Tx) domain is represented by <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Standards Taxonomy diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Standards Taxonomy]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="standards_taxonomy.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>An example of the Standards Taxonomy diagram</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="58854485-1b9e-4dce-9ac4-b89b030dccd6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">922668139</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Actual Organization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Protocol" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Protocol Layer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Protocol Stack" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Resource Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Standard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="UAF Element" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="102f92e7-7e2d-4809-bba1-29be2d06b10e" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249564771 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06305: Standards Traceability

- page_id: `249564771`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249564771/Standards+Traceability
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:29:25.087+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > UAF 1.3 > Standards
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

922795287

922795290

922795289

**Description**

The Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain shows the traceability between capabilities and projects that deliver them. It depicts the mapping of projects to capabilities and thus identifies the transformation of a capability(ies) into a purposeful implementation via projects.

The intended usage of the Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

The Standards Traceability (Sd-Tr) domain is represented by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Standards Traceability table' space='']Standards Traceability table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of the Standards Traceability table

922795286

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UAF Element' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562839 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06306: Standards viewpoint

- page_id: `249562839`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562839/Standards+viewpoint
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:44.307+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['standards-viewpoint', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408621248

408621250

408621249

“The DoDAF-described Models within the Standards Viewpoint is the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of parts or elements of the Architectural Description. These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

408621246

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3fcad43-df99-4b19-b617-8ee2416fa484"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408621248</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="715c0151-026c-43d3-a655-8648d82e8168"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408621250</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="169965a6-1acf-4ee5-88fc-7c27d24ae577"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408621249</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>“The DoDAF-described Models within the Standards Viewpoint is the set of rules governing the arrangement, interaction, and interdependence of parts or elements of the Architectural Description. These sets of rules can be captured at the enterprise level and applied to each solution, while each solution’s architectural description depicts only those rules pertinent to architecture described. Its purpose is to ensure that a solution satisfies a specified set of operational or capability requirements. The Standards Models capture the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry implementation guidelines upon which engineering specifications are based, common building blocks are established, and solutions are developed. It includes a collection of the doctrinal, operational, business, technical, or industry standards, implementation conventions, standards options, rules, and criteria that can be organized into profiles that govern solution elements for a given architecture. Current DoD guidance requires the Technical Standards portions of models be produced from DISR to determine the minimum set of standards and guidelines for the acquisition of all DoD systems that produce, use, or exchange information.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a655e724-7701-447a-8367-9ecf6b667a06"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408621246</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="c46668b9-6e20-4c86-b041-dcbcb4118b67" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249566538 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06307: Start Events

- page_id: `249566538`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249566538/Start+Events
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:58:28.622+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > BPMN modeling > Business Process Model and Notation Concepts > BPMN Process Diagram
- labels: ['msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A Start Event indicates where a particular process starts. In terms of sequence flows, a Start Event starts the flow of a process, and thus cannot have any incoming sequence flows.

When a Start Event is owned by an Event SubProcess, it can be:

- Interrupting. The Start Event interrupts the process contained in the Event SubProcess. The Interrupting Message Start Event is drawn with a solid border.
- Non-Interrupting. The Start Event does not interrupt the process contained in the Event SubProcess and starts parallel flow. The Interrupting Message Start Event is drawn with a dashed border.

Types of Start Events are as follows:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A Start Event indicates where a particular process starts. In terms of sequence flows, a Start Event starts the flow of a process, and thus cannot have any incoming sequence flows.</p><p>When a Start Event is owned by an Event SubProcess, it can be:</p><ul><li>Interrupting. The Start Event interrupts the process contained in the Event SubProcess. The Interrupting Message Start Event is drawn with a solid border.</li><li>Non-Interrupting. The Start Event does not interrupt the process contained in the Event SubProcess and starts parallel flow. The Interrupting Message Start Event is drawn with a dashed border.</li></ul><p>Types of Start Events are as follows:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e0eded90-dc2f-47bd-a07b-f136f915e6d7" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969419 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06308: Starting Compare Projects

- page_id: `243969419`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969419/Starting+Compare+Projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:23:58.304+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Comparing projects
- labels: ['starting-project-compare', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are two methods to compare projects:

- 
- 

Compare_projectsTo compare projects fromthe Analyzemenu

1. Open a project you want to compare.
2. From the **Analyze** menu, select **Compare Projects**. By default, the open project is selected in the Compared box. [ATTACHMENT filename='compare_projects_dialog.png']
3. In the **Base** box, choose a project you want to compare your open project to:
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and select a project from your file system you want to compare the current project to.
  - Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']and in the **Select Server Project**dialog, select a project version, trunk or branch you want to compare the current project to.If your compared project is also a server project, you can only choose a server project as a base.
4. Advanced **Compare projects** options: In the same dialog, click the [IMAGE alt='' src='']**Advanced** button to see more compare options. The **Use the older project as Base**option is selected by default. This means that the project whose creation date is older is set as the Base project, while the one that was created later is set as the Compared one (i.e. the projects you indicated as the Compared and the Base might end up being swapped based on their creation date).In this case, all changes are marked as made in the Compared project. If you deselect the **Use the older project as Base**option, the Base and the Compared projects will remain as you selected them in the Compare Projects dialog. [ATTACHMENT filename='comparer_advanced_dropdown.png'] In the **Optimize for** drop-down list, select
  - Speed , to compare the projects faster. This is the default option and it is sufficient for most projects. Keep in mind that this option requires more memory.
  - **Memory,** to compare bigger projects. Sometimes the memory required for the **Speed** compare mode exceeds the memory available for the modeling tool on your machine. In that case, switch to **Memory** mode, which conserves memory. However, this option slows the procedure down considerably. You can also specify the **Optimize for** option in the **Environment Options** dialog. Find the option under the **Merge and Compare** category in the **General** options group.
5. Click**Compare**.

CollaborateTo start comparing projects from theCollaboratemenu

1. Log in to Teamwork Cloud server. To compare project versions in the Teamwork Cloud environment, you must have the Read Resources permission for the main projects you want to compare.
2. On the main menu, click Collaborate , then select either:
  - Projects – the Manage Projects dialog opens
  - Open Server Project – the Open Server Project dialog opens
3. Select the project you want to compare, then click the Project History button [ATTACHMENT filename='project_history_button.png'] in the toolbar above project selection list.
4. While holding down Ctrl (or [ATTACHMENT filename='command-button.jpg'] ), select the two versions of the server project you want to compare.
5. Click Compare .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968557 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06309: Starting Model Merge

- page_id: `243968557`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968557/Starting+Model+Merge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:02.455+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Merge
- labels: ['starting-merge', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Start the merge using either of the following:

- From Collaborate > Merge From :

You can apply this method of starting a merge exclusively to Teamwork Cloud projects. For Teamwork Cloud projects, you do not need to specify the ancestor; it will be instantly identified after the source is selected. By default, the lowest common parent of two branches is considered a common ancestor.

- From Tools > Project Merge .

You can apply this method of starting a merge to both local and Teamwork Cloud projects, although used projects can be included in a merge only for server projects. You must specify both the source and the ancestor, and manually choose the 3-way or 2-way merge.

- For Teamwork Cloud projects, the modeling tool automatically applies the 3-way merge, as a merge is only available between project versions from different branches.
- Partial locking of a project occurs when merging changes of a project in the Teamwork Cloud. Only changed/updated and merging elements are locked in the project, not the whole project. Thus, actions with other server project elements are not interrupted for other users by locking and merging activities.
- While merging with lock is in progress in a server project on the Teamwork Cloud server, other users cannot commit the changes to the server.

Conflicting changes detected during merge are automatically resolved by accepting changes either from the Source or the Target version. To specify a contributor, go to **Options** > **Environment** > **General** > **Merge and Compare** and then next to **Automatically Resolve Conflicts by Choosing**, select either **Target** or **Source**.

##### Local model merge

To start merge from **Tools** > **Project Merge**

1. Open a project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space=''] .
2. From the Tools menu, select Project Merge . The Merge Projects dialog will open within the active project selected as the target.
3. Select the type of merge. Learn about 3-way and 2-way merge in[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding merge types' space=''].
4. Select a project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space=''] .
5. If you selected 3-way merge, choose a project that will be the ancestor.
6. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='advanced_button.png'] Advanced button to see more merge options. Otherwise, go to .
7. In the Optimize for drop-down list, select either:
  - Speed , to merge the projects faster. This will require more memory.
  - **Memory,** to decrease memory usage while merging the projects if your computer does not have enough memory. This will use less memory but at the same time will slow down the merge procedure when the project has many changes in diagrams. It will not include the differing diagrams/elements count. If you need that information, select optimize for **Speed**. You can also specify the **Optimize for** option in the **Environment Options** dialog. Find this option under the **Merge and Compare** category in the **General** options group. If the **Low memory** notification keeps popping up during merge, we recommend that you do one of the following:increase the amount of memory allocated for the modeling tooloptimize Merge and Compare for **Memory** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
8. step8Click the **Merge** button. The project merge will start. If changes are found, the **Merge** window will open. Proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space=''].

##### Model merge for Teamwork Cloud

To start a merge of the from **Collaborate** > **Merge From**

1. Open a server project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space=''] .
2. From the Collaborate menu, select Merge From .
3. In the opened dialog, select a server project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space=''].
4. Click the **OK** button. The question message illustrated below appears. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
5. Do one of the following:
  - Click **Merge with Lock**to lock the server project before merging. Other users cannot commit the changes.
  - Click **Merge without Lock** to perform the merge operation without locking the server project. We recommend that you select**Merge with Lock** to merge the project changes. To review the Merge Result, choose**Merge without Lock**.
  - Click Cancel merge process and close the dialog.
6. The project merge will start. If changes are found, the **Merge** window will open.
7. Proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space=''].

In this case, **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding merge types in Teamwork Cloud' space='CM']** is considered a common ancestor by default.

To start merge from **Tools** > **Project Merge**

1. Open a project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space=''] .
2. From the Tools menu, select Project Merge . The Merge Projects dialog will open within the active project selected as the target.
3. Select a project that will be the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Model Merge' space=''].
4. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='advanced_button.png'] Advanced button to see more merge options. Otherwise, go to .
5. In the Ancestor drop-down list, select either:
  - Lowest Common Parent to select the lowest common ancestor according to graph logic during the project merge.
  - Branch Point to select the version the branch was created from during the project merge.
6. In the Optimize for drop-down list, select either:
  - Speed , to merge the projects faster. This will require more memory.
  - **Memory,** to decrease memory usage while merging the projects if your computer does not have enough memory. This will use less memory but at the same time will slow down the merge procedure when the project has many changes in diagrams. It will not include the differing diagrams/elements count. If you need that information, select optimize for **Speed**. You can also specify the **Optimize for** option in the **Environment Options** dialog. Find this option under the **Merge and Compare** category in the **General** options group. If the **Low memory** notification keeps popping up during merge, we recommend that you do one of the following:increase the amount of memory allocated for the modeling tooloptimize Merge and Compare for **Memory** [IMAGE alt='' src='']
7. step7 Click the Merge button. The question message illustrated in the previous section will appear.
8. Do one of the following:
  - Click **Continue** to lock the server project and continue the merge process. Other users will not be able to commit changes.
  - Click **Show Merge Results**to proceed directly to the Merge Results.
9. The project merge will start. If changes are found, the **Merge** window will open. Proceed to [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space=''].

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding merge types' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding change concept' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Preparing for merge' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing and managing merge results' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Finishing and canceling merge' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243968701 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06310: Starting Profile Migration Transformation

- page_id: `243968701`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243968701/Starting+Profile+Migration+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:07.894+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Profile Migration Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To start the **Model Transformation Wizard**, do one of the following

- From the Tools menu, choose Model Transformations .
- Right-click one or more packages and select Tools > Transform .

To start the **Profile Migration Transformation**

- In the Model Transformation Wizard , select the Profile Migration transformation.

The **Next** step is disabled in the wizard, if there are no mapping defined.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To start the <strong>Model Transformation Wizard</strong>, do one of the following</p><hr /><ul><li>From the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, choose <strong>Model Transformations</strong>.</li><li>Right-click one or more packages and select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Transform</strong>.</li></ul><p> </p><p>To start the <strong>Profile Migration Transformation</strong></p><hr /><ul><li>In the<strong> Model Transformation Wizard</strong>, select the <strong>Profile Migration</strong> transformation.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84c88999-8f3a-4b77-a9a8-f991412a9c4b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The <strong>Next</strong> step is disabled in the wizard, if there are no mapping defined.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243969543 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06311: Starting the validation

- page_id: `243969543`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243969543/Starting+the+validation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:51:32.279+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Model analysis > Validation
- labels: ['validate-diagram', 'model-validation', 'validate-entire-model', 'msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

Validation can run on:

- 
- 
- 

modelTo start the validation of the entire model or its part

1. Open the **Validation** dialog . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='']>]]>
2. Specify the following options: - Validation Suite . - Validate For . - Minimal Severity . [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation dialog' space='']>]]>
3. Click Validate . The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite.

diagramTo validate the diagram, table, matrix, or relation map

- On the toolbar of diagram, table, matrix, or map, click the Validate Diagram button [ATTACHMENT filename='validate_diagram.png'] .
- Right-click the diagram, table, matrix, or map in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] , select Validation > [ATTACHMENT filename='validate_diagram.png'] Validate Diagram .

elementTo validate the element(s)

1. Right-click the element in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] .
2. In the shortcut menu, point to Validation and select:
  - Validate Element - to validate only selected element.
  - Validate Elements Recursively - to validate all child elements of the selected element.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5a7e29fe-b4d9-451e-9cfc-6e280a3851ed"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Validation can run on:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="model"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[The entire model, or its part (e.g. several packages).]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="diagram"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Diagram, table, matrix, or relation map]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="element"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f42833fa-a435-402a-a374-ef7a856ea81b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">model</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To start the validation of the entire model or its part</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <span class="confluence-link"><strong>Validation</strong> dialog</span>. <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[How to open the Validation dialog >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Specify the following options:<br />  - <strong>Validation Suite</strong>.<br />  - <strong>Validate For</strong>.<br />  - <strong>Minimal Severity</strong>.<br />  <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about each of the option >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Click <strong>Validate</strong>.<br />The model or its part is validated against the selected validation suite.</li></ol><br /><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e51f1451-0093-47fa-b195-f65a824feec5"><ac:parameter ac:name="">diagram</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To validate the diagram, table, matrix, or relation map</p><hr /><ul><li>On the toolbar of diagram, table, matrix, or map, click the <strong>Validate Diagram</strong> button <ac:image ac:title="Validate Diagram" ac:alt="Validate Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png" /></ac:image>.</li><li>Right-click the diagram, table, matrix, or map in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, select <strong>Validation</strong> &gt; <ac:image ac:title="Validate Diagram" ac:alt="Validate Diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="validate_diagram.png" /></ac:image><strong>Validate Diagram</strong>.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0fce2a9a-c6af-4708-a567-a75d79f2713a"><ac:parameter ac:name="">element</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To validate the element(s)</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the element in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>In the shortcut menu, point to <strong>Validation</strong> and select:<ul><li><strong>Validate Element </strong> - to validate only selected element.</li><li><strong>Validate Elements Recursively</strong> -  to validate all child elements of the selected element.</li></ul></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243966415 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06312: State Machine diagram

- page_id: `243966415`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243966415/State+Machine+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:59.713+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Diagrams > UML diagrams
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

307862529

307862531

307862530

INLINE

**Overview**

A State Machine diagram falls under the behavioral diagramming family.A State Machine diagram is a graph that represents a State Machine. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED'] and various other types of vertices (pseudostates) in the State Machine graph are rendered by the appropriate [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Pseudo States' space='MED'] symbols, while [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED'] are generally rendered by directed arcs that interconnect them. The states can also contain subdiagrams by a physical containment or tiling. Note that every State Machine has a top State containing all the other elements of the entire State Machine. The graphical rendering of this top state is optional.

***State Machine***. The State Machine is a specification of the sequence of states an object or an interaction undergoes in response to events during its life, combined with its responsive actions. The State Machine can represent the sequence of states of a particular collaboration, e.g. collection of objects, or even the whole system, which is also considered a collaboration. The abstraction of all possible states defined in a State Machine is similar to the way [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class diagram' space=''] are abstracted: all possible object types ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED']) of a particular system are described.

**Purpose**

A State Machine diagram (also called Statechart diagrams) represents the behavior of entities capable of dynamic behavior by specifying their response to the receipt of event instances. Typically, State Machine diagrams describe the behavior of [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Class' space='MED'], but the Statecharts can also describe the behavior of other model entities, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Use Case' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actor' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Subsystem' space='MED'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED'], or methods.****

**Usage**

A State Machine diagram can be used to:

- describe how an object moves
- show the behavior of a State Machine or a related set of State Machines
- illustrate Use Case scenarios
- depict event-driven objects

**Summary**

State Machine diagrams are valuable because they:

- identify events an object will/will not respond to depending on the object's current state
- identify the specific responses of an object to everything that can happen to it
- discover and validate the data that is needed to define the state of the object and affected attributes
- help to discover the internal effects of behaviors.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of a State Machine diagram

307862528

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating diagrams' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dragging objects' space='']

****

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The states can also contain subdiagrams by a physical containment or tiling. Note that every State Machine has a top State containing all the other elements of the entire State Machine. /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Classes]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, but the Statecharts can also describe the behavior of other model entities, such as <ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965969 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06313: State Machine diagram elements

- page_id: `243965969`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965969/State+Machine+diagram+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:49:49.943+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Working with projects > Importing data from other UML tools and formats > Enterprise Architect Import > Special Transformation
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

556348814

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

556348817

556348813

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

556348815

##### StateMachine

After conversion, a dummy StateMachine element will be created. The dummy StateMachine is either created by the XMI exporter from EA or by EA Import. A [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space=''] will be placed inside the dummy StateMachine and a dummy Region element will be created to contain all of the StateMachine elements.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### A dummy StateMachine element and a dummy Region element.

##### State

###### State containing other elements

A State element containing other elements will be transformed to a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED'][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MED']. All of the contained elements will be placed inside the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing regions' space='MED']element of the State element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### State Containing Other Elements.

###### State Containing StateMachine

If a State element contains a StateMachine element, the StateMachine element will be brought out and placed at the same level of the Region element of the State element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### State Containing StateMachine.

###### State containing Attribute and Operation

If a State element contains Attribute and Operation elements, the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Attribute' space='MED']and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operation' space='MED']elements will be removed from both the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='MED'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### State Containing Attribute and Operation.

The following transformation messages will open:

- Removed element <xmi:id>: State cannot contain Attribute.
- Removed element <xmi:id>: State cannot contain Operation.

###### State containing Diagram Element

If a diagram element is placed inside a State element, it will be removed.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### State Containing Diagram Element.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: State cannot contain diagram element.

##### StateMachine placed on a diagram

If a StateMachine element is drawn in a StateMachine diagram, an additional SubMachineState will be created to represent the StateMachine.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

A SubMachine State is a State whose SubMachine property is set to a StateMachine.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StateMachine Represented by a SubMachine State.

##### Object

An Object element placed in a State Machine diagram will be removed from the Diagram view. However, its data will be preserved in the Containment tree as an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Instance Specification' space='MED'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Object.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: The view represents element that does not support in State Machine Diagram.

###### Object containing a State Machine element

In EA, an Object element can contain a State Machine element. After it has been imported to the modeling tool, the State Machine element placed inside an Object element will be removed both from the Diagram pane and Containment tree. However, the Object element data will be preserved. All of the Object element data will be placed at the closest owner package of the Object element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Object Containing State Machine Element.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid element. Instancespecification can not contain element from State Machine.

##### Synch

A Synch element will be transformed to a Junction element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Synch.

The following transformation message will open:

Updated element <xmi:id>: Synch updated to Junction.

##### EntryPoint / ExitPoint

An Entry or Exit point placed inside a State (or StateMachine) element will be placed outside a Region element of its parent element in the Containment tree. It will be relocated to the nearest boundary of its parent element in the Diagram pane.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Entry/Exit Point.

##### Object Flow connecting State Machine elements

An Object Flow connecting the elements in a State Machine will be removed from both the Diagram view and Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### ObjectFlow Connecting State Machine Elements.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: Invalid ObjectFlow. Source or Target of theObjectFlow are connected to element from State Machine.

##### Information Flow connecting State Machine elements

An Information Flow connecting the elements in a State Machine will be removed from the Diagram pane; however, its data will be preserved.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Information Flow, Trace, and Dependency.

The following transformation message will open:

Removed element <xmi:id>: The view represents element that does not sup- port in State Machine Diagram.

##### Trigger

If there is a Trigger element unrelated to any Transition line, a dummy StateMachine will be created to hold the Trigger element. The dummy StateMachine will be named after the parent package name concatenating with 'trigger'.

In the case of a Trigger element related to Transition, the data of the Trigger element will be placed inside its parent [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Transition' space='MED']. Each Trigger element will be given an event type, represented by an Event element. After conversion, the Event will be placed at the closest owner package of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Event' space='MED'].

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Trigger.

##### Self Transition

Most of the elements in a StateMachine diagram can have a self transition, except the Initial, Final, and History elements. The self transition in these three elements will be removed from the Diagram pane, but the data of the self transition will be preserved in the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Self Transition.

The following transformation messages will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>: Self Transition does not support forInitial.Removed view <xmi:id>: Self Transition does not support forFinal.Removed view <xmi:id>: Self Transition does not support forHistory.

##### Removed element

Any element which is not the element of a State Machine diagram (such as Class, Actor, Use-case, or Action) drawn in the State Machine diagram will be removed from the Diagram pane. However, its data will be preserved in the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Removed element.

Ifan element which is not the element ofa StateMachine diagram has a child element and is drawn in the StateMachine diagram, the element will be removed from the Diagram pane. However, its data will be preserved in the Containment tree.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Removed Element with a Child Element

The following transformation message will open:

Removed view <xmi:id>:The view represents element that does not support in State Machine Diagram.

556355099

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State Machine diagram' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

All of the Object element data will be placed at the closest owner package of the Object element.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="object_containSMelement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Object Containing State Machine Element.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid element. Instancespecification can not contain element from State Machine.</pre></blockquote><h3>Synch</h3><p>A Synch element will be transformed to a Junction element.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="synch.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Synch.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Updated element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Synch updated to Junction.</pre></blockquote><h3>EntryPoint / ExitPoint</h3><p>An Entry or Exit point placed inside a State (or StateMachine) element will be placed outside a Region element of its parent element in the Containment tree. It will be relocated to the nearest boundary of its parent element in the Diagram pane.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="entryexit_point.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Entry/Exit Point.</h6><h3>Object Flow connecting State Machine elements</h3><p>An Object Flow connecting the elements in a State Machine will be removed from both the Diagram view and Containment tree.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="objectflow_connectSMelement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">ObjectFlow Connecting State Machine Elements.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Invalid ObjectFlow. Source or Target of theObjectFlow are connected to element from State Machine.</pre></blockquote><h3>Information Flow connecting State Machine elements</h3><p>An Information Flow connecting the elements in a State Machine will be removed from the Diagram pane; however, its data will be preserved.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="infoflow_connectSMelement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Information Flow, Trace, and Dependency.</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed element &lt;xmi:id&gt;: The view represents element that does not sup- port in State Machine Diagram.</pre></blockquote><h3>Trigger</h3><p>If there is a Trigger element unrelated to any Transition line, a dummy StateMachine will be created to hold the Trigger element. The dummy StateMachine will be named after the parent package name concatenating with 'trigger'.</p><p>In the case of a Trigger element related to Transition, the data of the Trigger element will be placed inside its parent <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Transition" /></ac:link>. Each Trigger element will be given an event type, represented by an Event element. After conversion, the Event will be placed at the closest owner package of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Event" /></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="trigger.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Trigger.</h6><h3>Self Transition</h3><p>Most of the elements in a StateMachine diagram can have a self transition, except the Initial, Final, and History elements. The self transition in these three elements will be removed from the Diagram pane, but the data of the self transition will be preserved in the Containment tree.</p><p><br /></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="selfTransition.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Self Transition.</h6><p>The following transformation messages will open:</p><blockquote><ul><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Self Transition does not support forInitial.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Self Transition does not support forFinal.</pre></li><li><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;: Self Transition does not support forHistory.</pre></li></ul></blockquote><h3>Removed element</h3><p>Any element which is not the element of a State Machine diagram (such as Class, Actor, Use-case, or Action) drawn in the State Machine diagram will be removed from the Diagram pane. However, its data will be preserved in the Containment tree.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="removedElement.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(112,112,112);">Removed element.</span></h6><p><span>If<span> </span>an element which is not the element of<span> </span>a State<span> </span>Machine diagram has a child element and is drawn in the State<span> </span>Machine diagram, the element will be removed from the Diagram pane<span>. H</span>oweve<span>r</span>, its data will be preserved in the Containment tree.</span></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="removedElement_withchild.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Removed Element with a Child Element</h6><p>The following transformation message will open:</p><blockquote><pre>Removed view &lt;xmi:id&gt;:The view represents element that does not support in State Machine Diagram.</pre></blockquote></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2cc7c32f-61ef-4514-bfa7-f5ab069ca4ea"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556355099</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="State Machine diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243965783 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06315: Status line

- page_id: `243965783`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243965783/Status+line
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-07T17:21:49.979+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > Getting started > Understanding the user interface
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

The Status line is located at the bottom of the modeling tool window. It displays various indications of active notifications, errors, the progress of currently running tasks, and some useful commands.

| Status bar icon | Status bar icon name | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  | Zoom Out | Click to zoom out on the current diagram. To learn more, see the page. |
|  | Zoom In | Click to zoom in on the current diagram. To learn more, see the page. |
|  | Diagram Zoom Slider | Click the zoom slider's cursor to zoom in or out of the current diagram. To learn more, see the page. |
|  | Fit To Window | Click to fit the current diagram in the window. To learn more, see the page. |
|  | Zoom 1:1 | Click to restore the diagram to its original size. To learn more, see the page. |
|  | Enter Presentation Mode | Click to enter the presentation mode. To learn more, see the Presentation Mode page. |
|  | Open Notification Window | Click to open the notification window. To learn more, see the page. |
|  | Background Tasks | Click to view the background tasks. To learn more, see the page. |
|  | Memory Monitor | Displays the memory used by the modeling tool. To learn more, see the page. |

##### Managing the display of the Status line's commands

To manage the display of some of the Status line's commands

- In the Main menu, click View > Status line .
- In the Main menu, click Options > Environment . In the open dialog, select General > Display . See the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space=''] page.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562841 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06316: StdV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `249562841`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562841/StdV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:44.393+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-1-standards-profile', 'stdv-1', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408664615

408664617

408664616

**Description**

The StdV-1 defines the technical, operational, and business standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture being described. As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the DoDAF V2.0 StdV-1 also documents the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context. The DISR is an architecture resource for technical standards that can be used in the generation of the StdV-1 and StdV-2 Standards Forecast.

In most cases, building a Standards Profile consists of identifying and listing the applicable portions of existing and emerging documentation. A StdV-1 should identify both existing guidelines, as well as any areas lacking guidance. As with other models, each profile is assigned a specific timescale (e.g., “As-Is”, “To-Be”, or transitional). Linking the profile to a defined timescale enables the profile to consider both emerging technologies and any current technical standards that are expected to be updated or become obsolete. If more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to an architecture, then a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space=''] should be completed as well as a StdV-1.

The intended usage of the StdV-1 includes:

- Application of standards (informing project strategy).
- Standards compliance.

**Implementation**

StdV-1 can be represented by a StdV-1 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The Standards cited are referenced as relationships to the systems, services, system functions, service functions, system data, service data, hardware/software items or communication protocols, where applicable, in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-3 Physical Data Model' space=''].

408664614

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol Stack' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-1 table' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249562847 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06317: StdV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `249562847`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249562847/StdV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-08-18T13:08:44.610+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > UAF 1.3 modeling > DoDAF 2.0 > Standards viewpoint
- labels: ['stdv-2-standards-forecast', 'stdv-2', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

408737149

408737151

408737150

**Description**

The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space=''] model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''].

[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''] models.

A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.

One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.

The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:

- Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).

**Implementation**

The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-1 Systems Interface Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-4 Systems Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-1 Services Context Description' space=''],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description' space=''],[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='']) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-8 Systems Evolution Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast' space=''].

408737148

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='MED']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol Stack' space='MED']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StdV-2 table' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5dab08df-45ed-463f-9891-33ad7dc25deb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737149</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="508d5c6e-be40-4292-b0a8-f37df7f0298f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737151</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8a3450f7-473b-46f2-95f8-cea894e70d93"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737150</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The StdV-2 contains expected changes in technology related standards, operational standards, or business standards and conventions, which are documented in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="StdV-1 Standards Profile" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StdV-1]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> model. The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards need to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /></ac:link>.</p><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9 Systems Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9 Services Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> models.</p><p>A StdV-2 is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems, operational, and business activities covered by the Architectural Description. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given Architectural Description is being built, and should identify issues that affect the architecture. A StdV-2 complements and expands on the StdV-1Standards Profile model and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this model is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p>The intended usage of the StdV-2 includes:</p><ul><li>Forecasting future changes in standards (informing project strategy).</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The StdV-2 can be represented using a StdV-2 table.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" ac:alt="StdV-2 Standards Forecast"><ri:attachment ri:filename="TV-2_StdV-2.PNG" /></ac:image></p><p>StdV-2 delineates the standards that potentially impact the relevant system and service elements (from <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-1 Systems Interface Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-2 Systems Resource Flow Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-4 Systems Functionality Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-1 Services Context Description" /></ac:link>,<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SvcV-2 Services Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>,<ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ SvcV-4 Services Functionality Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6 Services Resource Flow Matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /></ac:link>) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-8 Systems Evolution Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-8 Services Evolution Description" /></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SV-9 Systems Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-9 Systems Technology & Skills Forecast]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="SvcV-9 Services Technology and Skills Forecast" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SvcV-9 Services Technology & Skills Forecast models]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="acf44592-71cd-48ac-a7fd-0d8fc1c99390"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">408737148</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Standard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Protocol" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Forecast" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MED" ri:content-title="Protocol Stack" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating StdV-2 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=249561485 space=MT version=2 -->
## PAGE 06318: Steps of the Automatic Instantiation wizard

- page_id: `249561485`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/249561485/Steps+of+the+Automatic+Instantiation+wizard
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-08-18T17:13:05.904+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > SysML v1 modeling > Modeling structure with Blocks > Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML v1
- labels: ['mcse', 'msosa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Example

1349593571

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Example

1349593573

1349593569

**On this page**

43

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Example

1349593572

##### Introduction

This page describes each step of the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard in detail.If you are satisfied with the default values provided in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard, you can finish the wizard in the first step without making any changes. The instances will be created with the default values and in your working package. If you want to change the default values of a single instance or create multiple sets of instances, follow all steps of the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard. You can add each set in a different package, as well as display them on the newly created diagrams.

After finishing the wizard, you can create a very large number of instance specifications, depending on the size of your system. That is why we strongly recommend selecting a new package to store all created instance specifications.

##### Opening the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select the Block for which you want to create instances.
2. From the shortcut menu, select Tools > Create Instance . The Automatic Instantiation wizard opens.

##### The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard steps

The **Automatic Instantiation**wizard consists of three steps:

44

###### Step #1. Select parts to be instantiated

In this step, you can see all the available instance specification values (parts) of your selected element. At this point, you can select which parts or properties to instantiate, create or select values for parts and properties, as well as add or remove parallel parts.

You can select/deselect the parts by clicking the check marks next to the parts or the options in the Selection area.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Parts are displayed if the multiplicity is greater than 1. The number of parts displayed depends on the multiplicity.

- The number of parts displayed will equal the multiplicity. For example, if the multiplicity is 2, you will see 2 parts displayed in the **Automatic Instantiation**wizard. Also, in this case, you will not be able to add parallel parts or remove existing parallel parts.
- If the multiplicity is defined as an interval (for example, 0..*, 1..10, 1..* ) or *, only one part will be created by default. If you need more parts, you can create them by pressing **Insert** or selecting **Add parallel part** from the selected property’s shortcut menu.
- If the multiplicity is 1, parts will not be created.

The parts and properties are displayed with the suggested types, names, and values. In this step of the wizard, you can change them.

****

**Selecting a value for an instance**

To select a value for an instance

1. Select a part to be instantiated to which you want to add a value.
2. In **Instantiated properties**, click a **Value** specification cell and then click the ... button.
3. The **Select Instance Specification** dialog opens.
4. In the opened dialog, select the instance specification you want to add for the selected instance.
5. Click the **OK** button.

To create a value for an instance

1. Select a part to be instantiated for which you want to create a value.
2. In the **Instantiated properties**area, click the **Value** specification cell and then click the **Show Shortcut Menu** button. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. In the opened shortcut menu, point to the **Value Specification** and then select one of the available value types.
4. In the **Value**cell, type the desired value. 
 [IMAGE alt='' src='']

****Changing the name of an instance****

Each instance has a defaultname that uses the dot notation. However, you can change the default name by clicking the value row next to the **Name** property and typing the new name for the instance. ********

****[IMAGE alt='' src='']****

**Applying a customstereotype to an instance**

You can also apply the custom stereotype to the instance. Only those stereotypes can be applied that have *Instance Specification* as their metaclass and whose customizations have a meta property named c*lassifier* with the **New Types** property value set to the instantiated element’s type.

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

###### The custom «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes have *Instance Specification* as their metaclass 
and their respective customizations *ControllerFMU *Customization**and **ControllerSTM* Customization* have a meta property named c*lassifier* with the **New Types** property value set to the «Block» stereotype. 
Since the *Controller* block has the «Block» stereotype applied, the «ControllerFMU» and «ControllerSTM» stereotypes can be applied to the *Controller* block's instance.

**Changing a type for an instance**

You can change the type for the Part Property instance only if that type is a subtype of a Block (connected throughGeneralization). As shown in the following figure, the *Engine GM* Block and *Engine GMR*Block are subtypes of the *Engine*Block. When creating an instance for the *Vehicle* Block, you can change the type of the *engine*Part Property by selecting one of the following: *Engine GM* Block, *Engine GMR* Block, or *Engine*Block.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the type for a Part Property instance directly in the Automatic Instantiation dialog.

###### Step #2. Select a package

In this step, you can select the package to store instance specifications. You can select an existing package from the element tree, create a new package, or clone selectedpackages. Click the **Finish** buttonafter this step if you do not want to display your created instance specifications.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To create a new Package in the element tree

1. In the element tree, select the Package for which you want to create the new one.
2. Click the **Creation Mode** button.
3. Click the **Create Owner** button.
4. From the open menu, select the package kind. The Specification window of that package opens.
5. Type its name.
6. Click **Close**.

To clone the Packages in the element tree

1. In the element tree, select the Package to clone. The Package is cloned recursively including all nested structures.
2. Click the **Clone** button.
3. The Specification window of cloned Package opens.
4. Type its name.
5. Click **Close**.

###### Step #3. Create a diagram (optional)

In this step, you can choose whether or not to show your created instance specifications in a new diagram. If you do not wish to create a new diagram, you can simply skip this step by clearing the **Create a new diagram** check box.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To display instance specifications in the new diagram

1. Make sure the **Create a new diagram** check box is selected.
2. (Optional) If you want to create and display links between your instance specification(s), select the **Create link between instances** check box.
3. Fill in the **Type diagram name** field by typing the diagram name. NoteBy default, the diagram name is the selected Package's name in Step 2.
4. From the **Select diagram type** drop-down list, select one of the diagram types.
5. Specify the owner for a new diagram:
  - Select existing Packages from the tree.
  - Click the **Create Owner** button to create a new one.
  - Click the **Clone** button to clone existing Packages.
6. Click **Finish**.

#### TIP: Example

Example

For an example of how to create instances of Blocks with complex structures, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard in SysML v1' space=''].

1349605538

**Sample model**

The model used in the figures of this page is the**VehicleStructure**sample model. To open this sample, download*[ATTACHMENT filename='VehicleStructure.mdzip'].*

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
