colspan="1">RGB(0%,0%,50%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Blue</td><td colspan="1">#0000FF</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,0,255)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,0%,100%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Aqua</td><td colspan="1">#00FFFF</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,255,255)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,100%,100%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Teal</td><td colspan="1">#008080</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,128,128)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,50%,50%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Black</td><td colspan="1"> #000000 </td><td colspan="1">RGB(0,0,0) </td><td colspan="1">RGB(0%,0%,0%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Silver</td><td colspan="1">#C0C0C0</td><td colspan="1">RGB(192,192,192)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(75%,75%,75%)</td></tr><tr class=""><td colspan="1">Gray</td><td colspan="1">#808080</td><td colspan="1">RGB(128,128,128)</td><td colspan="1">RGB(50%,50%,50%)</td></tr></tbody></table><p>The format of an RGB specification value in form of the hexadecimal notation is a '#' immediately followed by either three or six hexadecimal characters. The three-digit RGB notation (#rgb) is converted to the six-digit form (#rrggbb) by replicating digits. For example, #FB0 is converted to #FFBB00.</p><p>For example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = “background-color:#FB0”&gt;<br />&lt;p style = “background-color:#FFBB00”&gt;</p><p>The RGB specification Integer and Percentage form is &quot;rgb&quot; followed by RGB values in the same format in brackets. You cannot use both integer and percentage at the same time. In the case that an RGB value is more than a maximum value (255 or 100%) or less than a minimum (0 or 0%), the value will be set to the maximum or minimum value instead, for example:</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-color:rgb(-100,500,0)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-color:rgb(0,255,0)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-colr:rgb(-100%,200%,0%)&quot;&gt;</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">&lt;p style = &quot;background-colr:rgb(0%,100%,0%)&quot;&gt;</p><p>The color used in all of the above examples is lime.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The ‘background-color’ property can be used in the following tags:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub. </pre><p>For example:</p><h2><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_1.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_2.png" /></ac:image></h2><h2><span style="color:var(--ds-text,#000000);">Border</span></h2><p>The shorthand property for the border property is 'border', which is used for setting the same width, color, and style of all four borders on four sides of a box. Border can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em>, and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Border Width</h3><p>Border width properties specify the width of border areas. The value of the border property is length. The border width properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="9ab48542-2554-49b7-9a6b-c716b650296e"><pre>'border-top-width'</pre></li><li data-uuid="feaf09b1-ffbc-4db2-a414-369011f97d27"><pre>'border-right-width'</pre></li><li data-uuid="0fcb49f0-4391-4892-814b-5cfcf9d5cd65"><pre>'border-bottom-width'</pre></li><li data-uuid="efecbdb0-58ca-4631-9aa3-fb5d3a0b00c1"><pre>'border-left-width'</pre></li></ul><h3>Length Specification</h3><p>The format of a length value is a real number immediately followed by a unit identifier. There are two types of length units: relative and absolute. Relative length units specify a length relative to another length property of the displaying device.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Relative units</em> are:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="7fc4ccae-adde-4b54-a0d6-68e1907822cf"><ul><li><strong>px</strong> <br />pixels, relative to the displaying device.</li></ul></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><em>Absolute units</em> are:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;" data-uuid="1e4edb9d-29e9-4945-95a4-ce0bbb25cfc7"><ul><li><strong>in</strong> <br />inches, 1 inch is equal to 2.54 centimeters.</li><li><strong>cm<br /></strong>centimeters.</li><li><strong>mm<br /></strong>millimeters.</li><li><strong>pt<br /></strong>points - the points used by CSS 2.1 are equal to 1/72nd of an inch.</li><li><strong>pc<br /></strong>picas - 1 pica is equal to 12 points.</li></ul></li></ul><h3>Border Color</h3><p>Border color properties specify the border colors of a box. The value of the border color has two meanings:</p><ul><li data-uuid="6ee37b8f-d951-4304-96ad-b327248e4667"><strong>Color<br /></strong>Specifies a border color. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="DEVG" ri:content-title="Custom renderers sample" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Color Specification]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> for more details on color.</li><li data-uuid="4d0e65c1-994a-47ca-9985-920e2ec1f33c"><strong>Transparent<br /></strong>Referred to as a transparent border.</li></ul><p>The border color properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="a2ba11e3-de6d-42a3-ae6d-21eb456455bd"><pre>'border-top-color'</pre></li><li data-uuid="a430357b-0493-416e-9042-d7b0a2b8febb"><pre>'border-right-color'</pre></li><li data-uuid="711f130a-c4b7-4f73-ad47-13826bedfee7"><pre>'border-bottom-color'</pre></li><li data-uuid="a30ddadf-e9e6-48e0-b87b-578b0ed6ae7e"><pre>'border-left-color'</pre></li></ul><h3>Border Style</h3><p>The Border style properties specify the style of a box's border lines. The border style may take one of the following 10 values:</p><ol><li data-uuid="78214c4a-dc69-48e6-a5b6-bfb3fe26cea9"><strong>none<br /></strong>This value will create no border because the computed border width is zero.</li><li data-uuid="3f57a534-4b35-427a-9480-efe2dcb4a2e1"><strong>hidden<br /></strong>The same as 'none'.</li><li data-uuid="980bc143-1ba2-4648-8e30-e3e4f93d7d35"><strong>dotted<br /></strong>This value will create a dotted border.</li><li data-uuid="34ff2f1e-8159-40a5-8337-bffe56538cde"><strong>dashed<br /></strong>This value will create a dashed border.</li><li data-uuid="1d197639-d4e3-49bc-a1e2-ccaea374f2e7"><strong>solid<br /></strong>This value will create a single line segment border.</li><li data-uuid="88e3ce73-0686-47b8-acf2-f9497ac4bf4f"><strong>double<br /></strong>This value will create a two solid line border.</li><li data-uuid="412a5153-0da0-4d10-808f-3f18c59bc2da"><strong>groove<br /></strong>This value will create a border that looks as though it were carved into the rendering surface.</li><li data-uuid="364894f8-13df-4348-a1b2-86c46be1b1e6"><strong>ridge<br /></strong>The opposite of 'groove'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.</li><li data-uuid="063e6392-2448-4757-b400-b9365de45588"><strong>inset<br /></strong>This value will create a border that looks as though it were embedded in the rendering surface.</li><li data-uuid="1fd2d5a3-6263-4abe-ba06-850a7e9ee841"><strong>outset<br /></strong>The opposite of 'inset'. This value will create a border that looks as though it were coming out of the rendering surface.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="786398f3-dd2e-487f-91ee-979b67f3ca16"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="5e28891d-f040-467d-950a-9109bb236760">The supported border values for <em>ODF</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>, <strong>solid</strong>, and <strong>double</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="30f256c3-9215-441b-a0c5-db70187a38ca">The supported border values for <em>RTF</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>,<strong>dotted</strong>, <strong>dashed</strong>, <strong>solid</strong>, and <strong>double</strong>.</li><li data-uuid="8e468ff7-86b6-467c-8947-83d2c1832a02">The supported border values for <em>DOCX</em> documents are <strong>none</strong>, <strong>hidden</strong>,<strong>dotted</strong>, <strong>dashed</strong>,<strong> solid</strong>,<strong> double</strong>, <strong>outset</strong>, and <strong>inset</strong> (<strong>groove</strong> and <strong>ridge</strong> will be rendered as solid).</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The border style properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="73f68d67-6e9c-4866-a0aa-7caff0228c9c"><pre>'border-top-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="af8bf20b-f86b-499f-a0b8-5a1a4a565a98"><pre>'border-right-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="cfef73b9-a772-4949-813b-fa11cd06c8ca"><pre>'border-bottom-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="bf713c2e-a0a5-411e-9596-e9bb18156a1e"><pre>'border-left-style'</pre></li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>Border properties can be used in the following tags:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have a red border, type the following line of code, see the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_3.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_4.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Margin</h2><p>Margin properties specify the width of margin area within a box. The 'margin' property is a shorthand property for setting the margin of all four sides while the other margin properties only set their respective side. The value of the margin property is length (See <ac:link ac:anchor="Length Specification" /> for more details). Margin can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates.</p><p>The margin properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="bfd0bb0d-9489-4454-888f-362db8dc1635"><pre>'margin-top'</pre></li><li data-uuid="16757f78-1fc1-4d9e-bc88-3530bf955215"><pre>'margin-right'</pre></li><li data-uuid="0c447e04-0a8d-4095-a563-c49135008fdc"><pre>'margin-bottom'</pre></li><li data-uuid="f399dac7-aa5e-44b4-b00f-d494084c02a1"><pre>'margin-left'</pre></li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for margins are as follows:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, table (supported only in RTF, ODT, and DOCX), p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to set a specific margin width for your cells, type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_5.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_6.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Padding</h2><p>Padding properties specify the width of padding area within a box. The 'padding' property is a shorthand property for setting the padding for all four sides while the other padding properties only set their respective side. The value of the padding property is length (See <ac:link ac:anchor="Length Specification" /> for more details) adding can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><p>The padding properties that can be used with a longhand property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="90dc259f-88c6-4e0e-9a90-793492545e92">'padding-top'</li><li data-uuid="e69c2cba-3e2d-4822-8082-fb9221f48b3c">'padding-right'</li><li data-uuid="11e42977-a3bc-4ac7-a8a6-89d5485f9c3a">'padding-bottom'</li><li data-uuid="7afd48ca-19ef-459b-bb4d-350a26e1610f">'padding-left'</li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for padding are as follows:</p><pre>h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, td, th, p, and div.</pre><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f1a21422-2bf3-4c82-b1b3-047b6b18433a"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li data-uuid="14a06f62-64d6-429b-9f93-48a6067fec4c">The supported border values of table tags for <em>RTF</em> documents are margin-left and margin-right.for more details.</li><li data-uuid="ff000129-81d3-40d8-92a8-29d8a7f30db4">The supported border values of table tags for <em>DOCX</em> documents are margin-left and margin-right.</li><li data-uuid="5435c3c5-a280-4e62-bbef-0fb8539b9268">The supported border values of all tags for <em>PPTX</em> documents are margin-left, margin-top and margin-bottom.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example, if you want to create specific padding styles for your cells, type the lines of code shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_7.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_8.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Color</h2><p>The 'color' property specifies the foreground color of an element's text contents. Only color values are allowed in this property (See Color Specification for more details on color).</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for colors are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have a green text color, type the lines of code indicated in figure.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_9.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_10.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Display</h2><p>The 'display' property specifies how text will be displayed. The possible display values are:</p><ul><li data-uuid="d37f6978-178a-4b45-969b-d78c6bd1ec6b"><strong>block</strong>: This value will make an element appear in the output report.</li><li data-uuid="8d345801-3315-4353-b25e-2ea49c7ff7d7"><strong>none</strong>: This value will make an element disappear from the output report.</li></ul><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the display property are:</p><pre>br, b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li (supported only in RTF, DOCX, and PPTX), table, tr, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to display an element but do not want to display the other(s), type the lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_11.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_12.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Font</h2><p>The 'font' property is a shorthand property for setting, except the following:</p><ul><li data-uuid="5cf96792-679f-4e2c-a7a7-62218cf48ec5"><pre>'font family'</pre></li><li data-uuid="a3eeb0ce-3d79-40ab-adbe-54bda5e915f2"><pre>'font-style'</pre></li><li data-uuid="0cf4f461-cb57-4c11-93cc-bb3195ab6b14"><pre>'font-variant'</pre></li><li data-uuid="dc561b21-5ff9-4c19-8d3c-4f7e4807cee9"><pre>'font-weight'</pre></li><li data-uuid="6eac9e10-aaee-4afd-9395-4ec83484fc5b"><pre>'font-size'</pre></li></ul><h3>Font Family</h3><p>Specify a prioritized list of font family names and/or generic family names.</p><h3>Font Style</h3><p>Specify either normal or italic face (within the specified font family).</p><h3>Font Variant</h3><p>Specify either normal or small-caps of variant (within the specified font family). Font variant is not supported in <em>XLSX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Font Weight</h3><p>Specifies the weight of the font. The following values are defined:</p><ul><li data-uuid="3bd58b5f-1959-41f1-aa74-1c4f660f1016"><strong>normal</strong>, <strong>lighter</strong>, <strong>100</strong>, <strong>200</strong>,<strong> 300</strong>, and <strong>400</strong> will be rendered as ‘<strong>normal</strong>’.</li><li data-uuid="24252641-27b5-4d7a-934e-306e83cae05b"><strong>bold</strong>, <strong>bolder</strong>, <strong>500</strong>, <strong>600</strong>, <strong>700</strong>,<strong> 800</strong>, and<strong> 900</strong> will be rendered as ‘<strong>bold</strong>’.</li></ul><h3>Font size</h3><p>Specify the font size. Possible values include xx-small, x-small, small, medium, large, xx-large, and the integer number from 1 to 7.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the font property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to have bold fonts in small caps, type the lines of code as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_13.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_14.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Text Align</h2><p>The 'text align' property describes how inline content of a block is aligned. Possible values of the text align property include: <strong>left</strong>, <strong>right</strong>, <strong>center</strong> and <strong>justify</strong>. The ‘text align’ property can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, ODP, DOCX,</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates.</p><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text align property are as follows:</p><pre>table, tr, td, th, p, and div.</pre><p>For example, if you want to align your paragraph to the left, center, and right, type the lines of code as shown in the Figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_15.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_16.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Text Transform</h2><p>The 'text transform' property controls capitalization effects of an element's text. Possible values of the 'text transform' property include:</p><ol><li data-uuid="59507ecf-6a16-4004-a2a0-80ccfe655b5a"><strong>capitalize<br /></strong>This will write the first character in an uppercase letter. Other characters will not be affected.</li><li data-uuid="2c7f5e69-cf23-4161-a47d-5439cde1ef1b"><strong>uppercase<br /></strong>This will write each word in upper case letters.</li><li data-uuid="dc1dcff0-bf30-467b-92ea-5a3e3a28ae44"><strong>lowercase<br /></strong>This will write each word in lower case letters.</li><li data-uuid="865d8471-210b-4c3e-96eb-51583e7414b7"><strong>none<br /></strong>This will not create any capitalization effects.</li></ol><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text transform property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to make all letters in a paragraph become uppercases, type the line of code displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_17.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_18.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>White-Space</h2><p>The 'white-space' property affects the vertical position of white-space inside a lined box. White-space can be used in <em>RTF, ODT, DOCX</em> and <em>PPTX</em> report templates. Possible values of the 'white-space' property include:</p><ol><li data-uuid="de6a1e0c-293b-4c6c-93ca-d7f0cecd590a"><strong>normal</strong> <br />To collapse sequences of white-space and break lines as necessary to fill line boxes.</li><li data-uuid="7bc3e5b0-f8aa-480d-892e-f801a46d2c1a"><strong>pre<br /></strong>To prevent from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are only broken at newlines in the source or at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content. Report Wizard will render this value as the &lt;pre&gt; tag.</li><li data-uuid="565d6d9a-6c56-46d2-a9c9-50525c9ac131"><strong>nowrap<br /></strong>To collapse white-space as for 'normal' but suppressing line breaks within text.</li><li data-uuid="d7416041-f51c-4a29-8718-57234d8943c6"><strong>pre-wrap<br /></strong>To prevent user agents from collapsing sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.</li><li data-uuid="54c3b793-86e7-49c7-a0b3-7cb756601b34"><strong>pre-line<br /></strong>To direct user agents to collapse sequences of white-space. Lines are broken at newlines in the source, at occurrences of &quot;\A&quot; in the generated content, and as necessary to fill line boxes. This value will not be rendered as pre.</li></ol><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the 'white-space' property are as follows:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to preserve some white-space, type these lines of code as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="White-space Style Example" ac:alt="White-space Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_19.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_20.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Width</h2><p>The 'width' property specifies the contents width of each box generated by a block-level. The value of the width property is length (See <span style="color:var(--ds-text,#172b4d);"><ac:link ac:anchor="Length Specification" /></span> for more details). Width can be used in <em>RTF, ODT,</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates.</p><p>The supported tag for the width property includes table.</p><p>For example, if you want to have a specific table width, type the lines of code as described in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Width Style Example" ac:alt="Width Style Example"><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_21.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_22.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Text Decoration</h2><p>The 'text decoration' property describes decorations that are added to text. The possible values of the text decoration property are as follows:</p><ul><li data-uuid="c1a29f14-786c-4d25-bb9a-fdc9e1a3ef71"><strong>none<br /></strong>To produce no text decoration.</li><li data-uuid="a6937401-ea4c-4572-a848-d0f862f0ab96"><strong>underline<br /></strong>To underline each line of text.</li><li data-uuid="a9be719d-5b36-4003-a752-8c3a95181767"><strong>overline<br /></strong>To add a line above each line of text. </li><li data-uuid="70874423-9e5b-4fa4-8884-c9463cc17eea"><strong>line-through<br /></strong>To add a line that strikes through each line of text.</li><li data-uuid="cd3abf0c-56e7-483e-bdd7-e0d427e1f2e6"><strong>blink<br /></strong>To add the blinking effect to text (being visible and invisible alternatively). The blink text decoration supports only the <em>ODT</em> report template.<br /> </li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5435e63f-2782-46e4-a4bc-0cccf7deac92"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Report Wizard does not currently support the overline text decoration.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Supported Tags</h3><p>The supported tags for the text decoration property are:</p><pre>b, i, u, h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, tt, code, samp, kbd, pre, big, small, strike, s, em, cite, dfn, var, strong, font, a, dl, dt, dd, ul, ol, li, table, tr, td, th, p, div, span, sup, and sub.</pre><p>For example, if you want to create a line-through text, type the line of code as displayed in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_23.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as indicated in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_24.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>Vertical Align</h2><p>The 'vertical align' property affects the vertical position of an element inside a lined box. This property can be used in <em>RTF, ODT</em> and <em>DOCX</em> report templates. Possible values of the vertical align property include:</p><ul><li data-uuid="725588bf-50fd-42b4-90cf-6b40d71327f1"><strong>baseline<br /></strong>This value will align the baseline of a box with the baseline of the parent box. Leave the vertical alignment to be default when you encounter this value.</li><li data-uuid="80e7c678-7d47-46e8-a9fb-44ec35250695"><strong>middle<br /></strong>This value will align the vertical midpoint of a box.</li><li data-uuid="22497f61-4476-4c83-a2bc-0152a3c86db4"><strong>top<br /></strong>This value will align the top of the aligned sub-tree with the top of a line box.</li><li data-uuid="b91cb349-fbd0-4d03-af01-7c4d13163dd4"><strong>bottom<br /></strong>This value will align the bottom of the aligned sub-tree with the bottom of a line box.</li></ul><p>The supported tags for the vertical align property are: table, tr, td, and th, for example, see figure below:</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_25.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The result of using the above example will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="addcss_26.png" /></ac:image></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=243971354 space=MT version=1 -->
## PAGE 06344: Supported file types

- page_id: `243971354`
- space_key: `MT`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MT/pages/243971354/Supported+file+types
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2025-07-31T10:52:12.167+02:00`
- ancestors: Modeling Tools Documentation > General modeling features > Report Wizard > Template Creation Tutorial > Linking report templates to the modeling tool
- labels: ['msosa', 'mcse', 'msa']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1351312429

1351312429

1351312429

Besides generating Rich Text Format templates and Rich Text Format reports, Report Wizard also generates other types of document:

1. *.html – Hyper Text Markup Language
2. *.rtf – Rich Text Format
3. *.odt – OpenDocument Text
4. *.ods – OpenDocument Spreadsheet
5. *.odp – OpenDocument Presentation
6. *.docx – Microsoft Word Document
7. *.xlsx – Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet
8. *.pptx – Microsoft PowerPoint Presentation
9. *.xml – Extensible Markup Language
10. *.txt – Text
11. *.docbook or *.dbk DocBook

To create a template for a specific type of document, simply open the editor designed for that particular type of document, and then save the template making sure that the filename extension is the same as that of the type of document.

1351312428

**Related pages**

Spreadsheet</li><li><em>*.odp</em> – OpenDocument Presentation</li><li><em>*.docx</em> – Microsoft Word Document</li><li><em>*.xlsx</em> – Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet</li><li><em>*.pptx</em> – Microsoft PowerPoint Presentation</li><li><em>*.xml</em> – Extensible Markup Language</li><li><em>*.txt</em> – Text</li><li><em>*.docbook</em> or <em>*.dbk</em> DocBook</li></ol><p>To create a template for a specific type of document, simply open the editor designed for that particular type of document, and then save the template making sure that the filename extension is the same as that of the type of document.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08106878-41a7-41d4-a67b-2c218aaf396e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1351312428</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro
